english: And sin is still in this world, and these are the things it has brought to us humans: shame, anger, deceit. And all these things appeared long ago on that very day that our forefathers ate the fruit of the tree that God had forbidden to them. And now they had a big problem, they didn’t know what to do about meeting up with God for he always came to see them every day.

ayoreo: Chi ore pota jeta ore chacose raque. Dupade Uruode chatatápo rĩ uje chi choquíbi ayoréode ore icaite iji taningane. Chi choquíbie chequé, chi choquíbipo jnani te rĩ, mu chi ca ore ajéngome cuchárique gotique. Uje que ore chiraja cuchárique uje cáma pisijnaringuérique, pajegátigárique, aníri, paringaquéode chacája erámone. Mu ore tagu Dupade gãrengué poria adie enga jec u uje ore chiraja dáringane choquíbio. Enga ore chiraja pajegátigade. Ore chiraja pisijnaringuéone enga ore chajéngome raque. Ore ajéngome Dupade yui uje que ore doi uruode enga ore ajéngopóme dáringane choquíbio enga chi ore chigaru pore acarã́quedatédie aja ñane enga jec u uje chi ore pésu doidode iji. Enga jec u uje aniri chi ore chucuepo dasocai rĩ ujetiga ore chacose rome Dupade ujetiga ore pógu raque.
english: The first thing they thought to do was to hide from him. The Bible tells us that the first people were naked -both the man and the woman but they were not embarrassed to be naked. That was because sin and shame had not yet entered the world – and they gave no thought to being naked – and they knew nothing about deceiving or being deceived. But when they ate God’s forbidden fruit then it was that they knew they were naked and they were embarrassed about it and didn’t want God to see them naked. Right away they got some large leaves and began sewing them together to cover themselves. And then they looked for a way to hide from God, so he wouldn’t see them.

ayoreo:
Dupade chise ore. Dupade
chajmita ore iji guedéjnoringai uté, mu chi ore pógu raque iji quedéjnarique.
english: God came to them in the late afternoon, but they had hidden themselves.

ayoreo:
Dupade Uruode chatata uje Dupade
chiraja ore jogadi tuté, mu Dupade chi jé ibidite ore. Chi tibidi ore, uje chi
chise dejnai uje chi que chimo ore iji daquei uje chi ore pógu raque.
english: The Bible explains that God knew where they were, but he called to them. He had come to his garden, and didn’t see them before him, because they had hidden from him.

ayoreo:
Mu
chi ca ore chajnai Dupade Uruode gotique. Chi ca ore
tibágui Dupade. Dupade chi chajmita, chajmita ore gu iji dirode jnese. Mu ore
ajéngome Dupade yui tenga chi ca ore chajnai Dupade. Chi ca ore chingongára
raja Dupade, mu chi diga jnacari chingo re ome Dupade.
english: At first, they didn’t answer his calls. And they didn’t come to God like they usually did each time God would come to check on them. This time they were ashamed and afraid of seeing him, so they didn’t say anything when he called them. They didn’t come to him, but finally the young man presented himself to God.

ayoreo:
Enga chi Dupade chi chojnirame
jnacare bajai: “¿Agúsi rique que uje que achajnai yu que a?”
english: And then God said to the first man: “Where were you, and why didn’t you answer me?”

ayoreo:
Mu jnacari chi jnacare bajai chi
chojnirame Dupade: “Ugúsi ti que.”
english: But the young man, the first man, said to God: “I was over there.”

ayoreo: Mu chi Dupade chojnira: “¿Gotique gu a uje bajéngome yu? Ise ga acho yãrengué pore adie uje ojningame uaque diríca, ‘ca acho jne.’
english: But God said to him: “Why are you ashamed? I think you must have eaten fruit from the tree from which I forbade you to eat. I said: “Don’t eat it!”

ayoreo:
Ga jnacari chi chojniga: “Berané
casé chequé ome yu, aniri, tagu barengué pore adie diríca que, enga chisi ome
yu to que, ga agu to que, mu yiba tu anira, berané, bapesudé ica, chequé.”
english: Then the young man said: “That woman that you gave me, well, she ate that fruit first and then she gave some to me, and I ate it too, but it was because of that woman you made for me.”

ayoreo:
Enga jec u uje ore chichaga
ñane, jnacari chi chichaga chequé uje chi tagu gu, chequé. Mu Dupade chi
chojningame chequé to: “¿Gotique gu a? uje agu diríca. Agu que poria adie uje
badute yuruode diríca uje ojningame ua diríca, ‘ca acho poria adie jne uje a
atoyoi jne ujetiga acho poria tuaté adie.’”
english: And then they started blaming each other. The young man blamed the woman because she ate the fruit first. So God asked the woman: “Why did you eat the fruit? You ate it even though you heard me say, ‘Don’t eat the fruit from that tree or you will die.’”

ayoreo:
Mu chi chequé chi chojnirame
Dupade: “Dicoré u que uje chãra yu enga jé agu to poria adie. Dicoré chãra
yúdode enga jé agu poria adie que.”
english: But the woman said to God, “It was the devil that deceived me and that is why I ate the fruit from that tree. The devil just kept on tempting me until I finally ate the fruit.”

ayoreo:
Chi ore ichaga ñane. Chi ca ore
chiraja cuchárique uné diríca iji taningane mu Satanas chãra ore enga jé u uje
ore chiraja paringaquéode, ore chuságaja cuchade uje ore chaguhéta ñane gáyode ome
cuchade.
english: They blamed each other. They were still innocent in the beginning,  but Satan deceived them and then it was that they learned from him all about deceiving and blaming others for things. And that is why God then punished them. And that is why God’s punishment affects us also just as it did the first people that God made, up to this very day. God said to Adam: “You didn’t do what I asked you to do. Because you disobeyed me and ate the fruit from that tree, harmful plants will come up all over this earth. From now on you won’t have free access to food. But you will have to work hard to make things grow to have food to eat. You will work so hard that your forehead will sweat. And only then will you have food to eat. And you will have to fight the harmful weeds that want to choke out your plants.”

ayoreo: Enga jec u uje Dupade chi Dupade cho dajuñame ore. Enga jec u uje Dupade ujuyaquéode udore u uje yoquibade u, yocai jnani bajade, Dupade ipesudode ore, jé u jec aja dirode unojo gajnerĩ. Dupade chi chojnirame Adã, anire: “Que be yuruode gotique diríca. Uje que be yuruode uje bagu poria tuaté adie, yãrengué pore adie, a jnumi tuné cucha gajnaréngone suéji jne. A ca agupo babosode bisideque jne rĩ. A agu cuchade to jne gu betotiguei ujetiga qué ejna baguesi bajaque. A agu cuchade to jne, mu a qué gajnetui déji bacuruté bajaque, benochai.  Jec ujeta aque to,” chojnirame jnacari. Dupade chi chojninga: “A apésu bejnatígo iji erámi tuné, mu a bajuya ua ome bejnatígo ujetiga apésu. Jé bajneque erámi isatígo jne.” Dupade chi chojninga: “A ojnatígo suéji erámi te jne.”
english: Thorny plants will come up all over the world.

ayoreo: *Comai – Zapoco, Bolivia – 1960**Marina de Morarie churúsãre Comai uruode unojo*
english: *Comai – Zapoco, Bolivia – 1960*

ayoreo: Yocacaranguipis iji dejai tudé ome yoquijõrai Dabidé (Ecarai) ojñane uje tṍraji yoquijorane Guidaigoso ore idai. Enga yacaranguipise uje irajágo uje e Guidaigosode uacagúsoji Dupade Uruode jogadode to. Jecucha icatecãcoi ome uac ujetiga aníri, adúcho Dupade Uruode to ujetiga angáchoi Dupade Uruode ujetiga anguretáyo Dupade to. Irajágo uje a ca ũré enga arajayopise uacarasade cojñoque ore acajnaquéode, uje ore irajatigade ome ayoréode uruode qué garósi. Mu ore ipotigádi jé u yoquipotigádi. Jec u jnese ujetiga yingongo Dupade Uruode ome uaque. Ipotágo ujetiga yingongo Dupade Uruode ome uac ujetiga arajáyo to. Yoquipotigádode u ujetiga arajáyo uje cho jetiga yocurúo gotique Dupade Uruode. Gusu uje ingongome uaque uje yirajágo uje gusu Dupade Uruode uje ũré. Ayorérique que urúo gotique. Mu Dupade urúo.
english: I am thankful tonight for the news about our friends, the*Guidaigosode*that our friend Davídé (also called Ecarai) has shared with us – news coming from the*Guidaigosode’s*village. I am also thankful to know that you*Guidaigosode*now attend where Dupade’s words are taught. And I’m thankful to hear that you now have a place to learn about Dupade’s words. We always pray for you that you will hear Dupade’s words and pay attention to them and believe in Dupade also.

ayoreo:
Icatecãcoi Dupade ome uac
ujetiga beyoi Dupade Uruode to. Ipota jeta yimo uaque iji dire uñeque ujetiga
yingo Dupade Uruode ome uaque to. Ujetiga yatata yadode jnese uje aníri, jé
ũrepise Dupade Uruode. Mu Dupade ipotigádi jec u to jetiga aníri, ayoréode
yiyágo yocadode jnese. Ujetiga yiyágo yoquisijnaringuéone. Ujetiga yajnángo
Dupade ujetiga yanguretágo ujetiga yicoi uruode jnese.
english: We know you can hardly understand the teaching of the Cojñone, for their understanding of the Ayoré language is not very good yet. But what they want is what we want – and that is to tell you about Dupade’s words. We all want you to understand Dupade’s words. We want you to understand that what we tell you is not our own words, but Dupade’s words. We only want to tell you what we understand, that only Dupade’s words are true. They are not the words of humans. But Dupade’s words.

ayoreo: Irajágo uje ayorérique que yirajágo erámone unojo isocárique gotique uje pésu re icaite. Mu yirajágo uje ayorérique que ipesute erámone gotique. Déji Dupade Uruode uje chi Dupade ipesute cuchade unojo icaite iji erámone jnese, erámone jogosórone cucha iji taningane. Dupade Uruode chatata uje, iji taningane ga uje chi cáma ayorérique, cuchisórique déji erámone icaite, ga Dupade chi pésu erámone unojo icaite ome dajopié.
english: We pray for you to do Dupade’s words, also. I want to see you one day and to teach you about Dupade’s words face to face. I’d like to give you my testimony and assure you that Dupade’s words are true. Dupade wants all of us Ayoréos to stop our former ways. He wants us to stop sinning. He wants us to follow him and to believe in him, and to do everything he asks us to do.

ayoreo: Dupade chi pésu erámone unojo iji taningai enga chi Dupade chimate erámone unojo iji díningo uje chi chojmára iji yijmanu uñai. Dupade Uruode chatata uje iji taningai enga Dupade chi*chijire*erámone enga chi gusu ijnangununie icaite iji erámone unojo. Mu Dupade chi pésu ijnangununie ga chi chajuque pajei ga chi Dupade pésu, chajo ijnangununie aja uñane. Chi díreca ijnangununie petique. Dupade chi pésu cuchade jnese jetiga ayoréode jé ca yotigáco. Dupade pésu diri ujetiga ayoréode ore yisago cuchade iji ujetiga yisoco ujetiga yipéco cuchade, ujetiga yipéco uruode. Jé basaja Dupade pésu dejai te gu jetiga ayoréode ore yoco yocujñare iji ujetiga yimóngo.
english: *W*e know that no human person knows how the earth was made. But we know that human beings did not make it. It’s all in Dupade’s words, which state that the earth and everything in it was made by Dupade in the beginning. It tells us that in the beginning before there were humans or animals that Dupade made the earth by his power.

ayoreo:
Quigade ga Dupade chi pésu dírecaringuéi
iji taningai te ga pésu ijnangununie. Dejai ujetiga jé cãra re ome diri ujetiga
yimóngo iji, enga yoco yocujñare iji. Mu Dupade Uruode chatata uje chi gusu
yodode icaite iji jnumi iji taningai.
english: Dupade made this earth in the beginning, and in six days he completed everything. In the beginning, it says, Dupade saw that this earth was completely dark. So Dupade divided the darkness and pushed it together. And in the part where it had been daylight appeared. Dupade made everything so that we humans would not wonder how the earth was made. Dupade made daylight so that humans could work and travel around and serve Him. On the other hand, he made the night so that we people could rest and sleep.

ayoreo: Que yurúo gotique mu déji Dupade Uruode ujetiga yajico iji. Chi gusu yodode icaite iji erámone. Chi chaco jnumi gai, mu Dupade chi pésu yodode enga chajuguiji ñane iji. Jec u uje chajugunápo yodode iji erámi tenga, jec u uje Dupade chi chajuque yodode iji ñane enga yodode charidode chojmára. Anírone tiedie jogadode jé chojmára, gajñonie, tuijnane uje cáma tie jochade. Enga Dupade chi pésu jnupe cajnóri to. Enga Dupade chimate dapesudode unojo.
english: In the beginning, after making daytime and night time, they both occurred as a day. He separated the day so that at night we would sleep and rest. But Dupade’s words tell us that the only thing that could be seen in the beginning was water.

ayoreo:
E que yotique dépoji erámi jnese
rĩ mu Dupade chi pésu cuchade uné ore. Ujetiga ayoréode yimóngo ipesudode ga
ca yotigáco jec aja dirode unojo.
english: This is not just my words, but they are Dupade’s words for us to read. It says that there was only water long ago. It says that only water was seen on earth. It completely covered the ground, but then Dupade made the water split. And that is how He split the water that covered the earth with the intent of keeping it in one place. All the different water conduits – the rivers, the lakes, the pools, Dupade made water run into them, and they all flowed toward one place. And that is how Dupade made dry land appear. And Dupade finished these things that he did.

ayoreo:
Dupade chajo yodode aja ñane
enga uéchai uñai je qué déji dajogadode uje tie u uje yodode dacagode ga Dupade
jé ipesucho jogadode. Quigade enga jec u uje jnupe cajnóri, jnumipísi, chi
pijoque to. Dupade chimate tiedie, erámi jnupe cajnóri ore. Yoquiyaripi jnupe
gajnerĩ erámi tu ore.
english: Now the water no longer covers the whole earth because of these things that Dupade did. Even today, when we people see what He has created we don’t doubt who did it because we can see it still.

ayoreo: Ga Dupade chi aníri, chi chimo uje chi jé arec ujetiga pojníngui sue iji dapesudi jnupe cajnóri. Ga jec u uje Dupade, iji jnupe cajnóri tudé, Dupade chi pesapo poridie rĩ ujetiga sue iji jnumi. Pésu cuchade uje sue iji erámi tudé. Enga jec u uje iji jnupe cajnóri tuté enga uje erámi yoquiyaripi jnupe.
english: Dupade brought all the water together and the other part of it is still in the places, like rivers, that Dupade made for the water to enter. And then it was that the ground was dry, and the real earth could now be seen. Dupade completed the rivers, and the dry earth.  Our dwelling place, the earth, the world, was now made.

ayoreo: Ga Dupade chi pesapo cuchisode iji rĩ; cuchisode jnese uje déji erámi tuné. Dupade chi pésu. Dupade chi pota jetiga cuchárigo déji ipesudi tuté jnupe. Enga pésu cuchisode. Anóningacho Dupade Uruode. Dupade chi pésu cuchisode jnese iji erámi uje cuchisode diguiji. Jec u ayoréode yoquimone cuchiso ponáyo iji erámi jnese. Dupade chi pésu poridie enga poridie chi adie cuse ujetiga cuchisode tagu. Enga jé jnumi tupo to rĩ uje Dupade ipesudápo rĩ ujetiga ayoréode ore quigade dirome enga ayoréode ore aníri, yichajo to, jeonga ayoréode ore yipéco yoquejnatígo iji ujetiga yajo.
english: And Dupade saw it all and said that the dry ground was good for plants to bloom in. And then Dupade created fruit-bearing trees to come out of the dry ground. Everything that grows was made by Him. And that’s how it was that on the dry land of this world there was a dwelling place for us.

ayoreo:
Quigade enga jéc u uje Dupade
pésu cuchisode jnese uje chimate erámi tuné. Chimo erámi tudé ga chimánapo
cuchisode rĩ. Enga Dupade chi pota ujetiga ayorérigo déji to ujetiga ore nóna
ipesudode cuchiso jnese iji erámi.
english: Dupade also made the animals in the world, all the different kinds of animals. They were made by Dupade. Dupade wanted the earth he had created to be inhabited. So he made animals. Notice what Dupade’s word’s say: Dupade made all the animals that walk on the earth. And that is how it is that we people see all kinds of animals even today. Dupade made the trees to have fruit so that the animals could eat. And the ground he also made so that the humans who would come after the animals could work it and we could make our gardens, so we could eat.

ayoreo: Dupade chi chica jnumi urui. Enga chipocásu dachabei ga chi pésu ayoré tiji. Chi pésu ayoré tiji jnumi tuné ome datirai enga chimate ayoré adode jnese: jmanáyone, jmanecade, orotadode, edodie enga chi chisi ome ayoré pucuecaringuei tome. Mu quigade enga Dupade chubúchu dapesudi tuté ayoré ujunane ga Dupade chisi ome áyipie ueca tome. Enga jec u uje Dupade chi chimate ayoré bajai, ayoréode yocajnu jnacare.
english: After making all the animals, then it was that Dupade finished this earth. He saw this earth and the animals he had finished making. And Dupade, it’s said, wanted there to be humans also along with all the animals on earth.

ayoreo:
Jnani. Jnani tuté chi i tu Adã.
Dupade ipesute jnacare bajai tuné iji erámone iji imani erãpe, yoquiyaripi
tuné. Ayoréode uyoque jnese yocãrani tu Dupade ipesudi tuté jnacare, uje i tu
Adã. Dupade chi chubúchu ayoré tuté ujunane ga chisi ome áyipie tome, áyipie
ueca. Enga ayoréode yocúringai ayoré e u. Yocúringai áyipierãc e u jec iji
dirode udojo gajnerĩ. Enga jec u uje Adã chi nóna Dupade ipesudode cuchiso
jnese. Adã a pésu Dupade anírone imaningone cuchiso íode jnese. Enga íode jé u íode udore uje íode gajne iji dirode unojo. Enga quigade enga Dupade chi*chijire*dapesudode cuchiso jnese. Uje chi ore nóna ñane aja quedéjnane jorónga. Dupade imoi tu uje cuchisode, cuchiso choqui nóna cuchiso chequé. Enga chi ore nóna ñane aja quedéjnane. Enga chi ore cháta pininaquei taja ñane uje ore nóna ñane aja quedéjnane.
english: Dupade, it’s said, cut into the ground. He began to form the ground to make a man from it. It’s said, he made the man from this soil moistened with his saliva and he formed each member of the man’s body: his hands, his arms, his shoulders, his face, and it’s said he then gave him life. But afterwards Dupade blew into the man’s nostrils and put a living soul into him. And this is how Dupade finished making the first man, and that young man was just like us.

ayoreo:
Enga Dupade chi chojninga:
“Areque to jetiga jnacari anínique cuse to, atajasónique dojo ayoré ujetiga ore
catecã́ri ñane. Ujetiga ore nóna ñane aja quedéjnane. Ujetiga ore cho uñeque.”
Yingo jnese uje Dupade a pésu cuchisode enga pésu choqui, pésu chequé to
ujetiga anire, cuchisode chõre rac ujetiga quérã ujetiga irágu erámi. Jecute Dupade chisi ome yoque gatei ome dapesudi jnacare, yocai jnani bajai uje i tu Adã. Enga chi Adã chi mo. Jnacare bajai, Dupade imanique bajai, ayoré chi mo. Enga jec u uje Dupade chi chijnime Adã orotabitique. Jnacare bajai orotabidi tenga Dupade chi pésu chequé tiji. Enga jec u uje Dupade chi chimate chequé jeta nóna jnacare bajai enga jec u uje Dupade chi pésu chequé tiji orotabidi. Enga Dupade a chimate chequé tiji, enga jec u uje anire, jnani cho cuyuque to, niji to, iji aniri, damochádi.
english: He was a grown man and his name was Adam. The first young man was made by Dupade from the earth he had made, the same earth where we dwell. All of us humans have descended from the young man that Dupade made first, named Adam. Dupade blew breath into his nostrils and gave him a mind and made him a living soul. Our state of being intelligent human beings started in the beginning and continues to this very day. And from that time Adam was always concerned with all the animals Dupade made.

ayoreo:
Enga chi chimo chequé tiji, chi
chimo chequé tiji dejoi. Enga chi jnacare bajai chi irasé chequé enga Dupade
chojningame jnani bajai: “A bátajató, ayoré tuaté jne,” Dupade chojninga.
english: Adam was to make names for all the animals that Dupade created. And ever since that time we have terms for the different animals. And afterwards Dupade looked at all the animals he had made. They went around together, in vain. Then he saw that the animals paired up male with female. And they continued to stay together. And they were content to be with each other, and travel together. And they interacted together and were content to be with those of their own kind.

ayoreo:
“A jé átamája jne,” chojningame
chequé, “uje yipésu ua iji jnani orotabidi tenga badode a jec u jnacari, jnani
adode jnese jne. Ajnóraño ñane jne,” chojninga. “A jé ca adosóyo aja ñane jne,
enga yipota to ujetiga õcho uaque to ujetiga aquéraño to iji erámi tuné ga
uásejna uaque to jne.”
english: And Dupade said: “It would be a good thing for the man to have someone to help him also, someone like himself to talk to. And to go about with him. Someone to relate to.” I have told all about this, how that Dupade made animals male and female so that the animals could multiply in number and fill the earth.

ayoreo: Jecute Dupade isocai tuné uje Dupade chimate erámone unojo. Mu yajníngomá yoquiquigade rĩ. Dupade chi pota ujetiga que chimate cuchade bisideque. Chi pota Dupade iji taningai ujetiga imaningo cucha ucode aníningo cuse, gajnesóningo cucha.
english: Therefore, Dupade gave us, in posterity, to the first man, our forefather named Adam. Adam slept. The first young man, the first human Dupade made, that person went to sleep. Then, while he was sleeping, Dupade took a rib out of him. And from that rib, Dupade made a woman. In this way Dupade provided a woman to be the first man’s companion, the woman he made from his rib. Then it was that when Dupade finished making the woman, the man woke up and got up from his bed.

ayoreo:
Jecute Dupade chi pésu cuchade
jnese ujetiga unirisóningo cuse. Enga Dupade chi*chijire*yodi te mu yodi
que unirisóningo cuse gotique enga Dupade chi jec u uje pésu oyídode uje uniri
tu yodi. Cuchisode jnese uje cho daniri iji yodi chi Dupade pésu to. Jeque
oyídode jnese uje jé cãra rome ñane: arócojnane, cucujna gajnúone, jé u cuchade
jnese.
english: And it’s said he saw the woman who was beside him, he was very pleased with the woman. Dupade said to the first man: “This person is your helper.” That’s what Dupade said.

ayoreo:
Dupade chi pésu cuchiso udore
ujetiga cho daniri iji jnumi ujetiga imaníngo cucha unirisóningo cucha jnese.
english: “You will join him,” Dupade said to the woman. “I have made you from the rib that I took out of the man, and you will do everything the young man does. Love each other,” he said to them. “Get along and don’t hurt each other. I want you to multiply in number so that there will be many people on earth just like you.”

ayoreo:
Dupade chi quigade ga chi pésu
gaté unirisórone cucha to ujetiga chóji gaté yui enga u chugúperone jnese. Jec
u chugúperone to uje uásejna ga cãra rome ñane. Dupade pesapo cuchisode rĩ ujetiga
cho daniri iji jnumi.
english: This is how Dupade made this earth and the things in it. But let’s go back and I will explain something else to you. Dupade, it’s said, had a purpose for the things he made, he didn’t just make them for nothing. He wanted in the beginning for all the creatures he made to have the proper environments and all the things they needed.

ayoreo: Enga Dupade chi pesapo guedoside rĩ ujetiga guedoside uta chijñate ayoréode, cuchisode ore gai jnese, Dupade imani erãpe to iji dejai. Dupade pésu guedé ome diri ujetiga chijñate ayoréode gai iji diri, jeque Dupade pésu guedoside ujetiga chijñate erámi tuné, ayoréode ore gai iji dejai. Enga Dupade imaningone cucha aníri, unirisórone cucha jnese. Unirisórone cucha upúsu jnese. Jecute Dupade, jé yimóngo jec aja dirode unojo gajnerĩ.
english: And so it was that Dupade made every kind of environment (land) to have creatures dwelling in it. He looked at the water but saw that there were no creatures belonging to it, and so he made fish; the water was the environment for the fish. And Dupade created all the other creatures that needed water for their environment. Besides the various kinds of fish, he created: crocodiles, alligators, beavers, webbed foot mammals, all these types of animals and many more.

ayoreo:
Dupade imaningone unojo cucha
iji uruode u uje que taque Dupade gotique. Ũrepise Dupade. Dupade imani tu
tuté dire, jec u diri, dirode uje yimóngo iji dirode ujnojo uje Dupade pésu gu
jetiga ayoréode yimóngo. Yujnusinángo ipesudode cucha ga yirajágo uje Dupade
Uruode ũrepíso uje imóngo Dupade ipesudode cucha.
english: Dupade, it’s said, made these animals to have as their land the ground, so that the things he had finished making would all have their own environments.

ayoreo: Que ayorérique yoquipesute erámi tuné gotique. Dupade ipesucho. Dupade ipesudode. Ipesudi dejaque, jé u dejade uje yimóngo gajnerĩ uje ayoréode yimóngo jnese. Dupade imani tuté diri. Jé u diri te gajnerĩ uje imóngo cuchade to. Dupade ipesudi jnupe. Jé u erãpe. Jec u yoquijogosórone erãpe. Dupade ipesudedie pori erãpe gatoi jec u yoquimoi. Erãpe gajne iji dirode unojo. Dupade ipesudode cuchiso u yoquimone cuchiso jnese. Dupade imani tuté jnacare bajaque u ayoréode uyoque uje quigade dirome ga ayoréode yocãrapiesṍri jec u jnani tuté, chequé to rĩ uje chequé anire, chequé ore chõre raque ga yocãranone u ore.
english: Dupade, it’s said, later made things whose environment would be the sky, the birds that fly in the sky, all of them. There were many birds, but they were not alike. Dupade also made the animals who considered the ground their land.

ayoreo:
Ayoréode que yórango iji sañeque
gotique. Que yórangoji cucha uñeque gotique. Yocãrani jec u jnacare bajai,
Dupade ipesudi jnacare bajac uje chi i tu Adã icaite. Jeque ayoréode ore
yocãrapié chequé chi i tu Eba icaite. Chequé bajé tu, Eba, uje Dupade pésu.
Ayoréode uyoque yocaté tu. Jeque ayoréode ore yocai tu Adã, jnacare bajai.
Ayoréode ore uyoque, cojñone ore jnese, ore yocãrapi tu Dupade ipesudode
jnacare bajaque, ayoré bajaque ore.
english: Dupade made the moon so that it would provide light for humans and animals all of them, and for the whole world at night. Dupade made the sun for the day so that it would provide light for people during the day, just as he made the moon to shine on the earth and its people at night. Everything Dupade created was part of the environment that Dupade knew the creatures would need. There was every kind of environment. Therefore, Dupade and us have all observed these things up till this very day.

ayoreo: Enga quigade enga Dupade chi chijna ore aja dejnatique, yocai jnani bajade, Adã, Eba ore. Dupade chicha cuchade aja ore gai jnese ujetiga jé ca ore*fuerza*cuse ome dacuetique cucha. Dupade chi chicha ore aja dejnatique uje chi cuchade déji jnese. Dupade chi diji ore gari, dapesudode ayoré bajaque ore. Chi chicha ore aja dejnai te que. Chi chicha cuchádaja ore jnese, ore posode. Dupade imaningone cucha jnese ujetiga ore tagu ujetiga ore nína ránguiji ujetiga ca ore chicadígui Dupade uté. Enga ore doi uruode jnese iji taningai nanique. Ore doi uruode enga jé ca ore chimoc ujetiga ore doi urúrique mu ujetiga Dupade ipotigádi icha ome ore ujetiga angoninguite guesaque ore.
english: These were all things Dupade created by his words and there is no way that he would lie about it. Dupade is very truthful.

ayoreo: Fuerza – Etotiguei
english: Dupade created day, and it’s the same type of day we see even today, daytime was made so we people could see things. When we consider all the things Dupade made that still exist today, we know that Dupade’s words are very true for we are still seeing the things just like he created them.

ayoreo: *Comai – Zapoco, Bolivia – 1960**Marina de Morarie churúsãre Comai acajnaquéode unojo nanique*
english: We humans could not possibly have made this earth. Dupade made it. It’s Dupade’s creation. He made the night, the same night we still have during which we all sleep. Dupade made the daytime. The very daylight that makes it possible for us to see things even today. Dupade made the earth. This very earth. The earth that we humans have as our environment. Dupade created the tall trees whose heads we see above us that belong to the woods. The animals we see today were all made by Dupade. And the very first man that Dupade made, that’s us, for we have all descended from him and the woman that Dupade gave to make him complete. And all of us have been born of women; the women that have descended from the first woman have filled the earth with people and we continue filling it.

ayoreo:
Ga ajnusináño to gajneri, Jesucristo, Yocangarádejnai uruode ome yoque. “Ca amíningacho ore gajiné,” chojninga Jesucrísto, “jetiga ore cho ũrusode iji uaque. Ũrusode jé u ore uje que ore chiraja Dupade, Yocai. Ũrusode jec u ore.” “Satanas a chósiriji guesi tome
uaque ujetiga anguretáyo yu. Mu ujetiga ẽ́cacho yi ga Satanas a chósiriji
guesi tome uaque dirome. Mu gosi ujetiga etóguiji darajatigai dirome ga danguretaretigai
uté u jetiga chise uéchai,” chojninga Jesucrísto ome yoque.
english: Remember now what Jesus Christ, the-great-one-we-listen-to, said to us: “Now don’t pay any attention to those who call you crazy. They are the crazy ones, for they do not know Dupade who is our father. Truly, they are the ones without good sense.”

ayoreo:
Jecute yocacaranguipis ome
Jesucrísto uje que yugúcoi ojningane udojo cucha go. Que yugúcoji go. Que
yipótago erámi jogonórone cucha go. Chamáñaje e yiyógo jnumi gai gu. Jé
unejaque yocuniri.
english: “Satan will try his best to overcome those of you who believe in me,” Jesus said. “If you only mention my name, he will revolt against you. But whoever is sure of what he knows, his faith will help him to reach the other side of this trial.” That’s what Jesus taught.

ayoreo:
“A ore chejnáreque jne,”
chojninga. “A ore chejnáreque yacajnaquéode dirome mu jé adotáyo uáguiji
uacarajatigárigo. Ajnusináño uje ore eteque yatígo nanique to,” chojninga
Jesucrísto. “Ajnusináño jne uje uyu u uje que yipésu cuchárique nanique, mu ore
cho damajo yu. Moitapure dirome uje cucha bisideque uaque ome ore,” chojninga
Jesucrísto. “Mu ujetiga gosi chise ore uechai dirome ome danguretaretigai te ga,
uté gosi tu ujeta piquetai u.”
english: We should be grateful to Jesus Christ that we are not yet going through the things He told us about. We are not there yet. We no longer are interested in the things of this world, however. In fact, we have overcome the world. We belong to a different country now.

ayoreo: Yocacaranguipise ome Dupade gajiné iji deréjnai tudé uje que yicoi to gotique udojo. Que yejnaréjo yoque go. Cho jeta Dupade chãraque yoque ome cucha gajnaréngone yúode gu yocúringai ujñepetaque. Mu que qui tu go. Dupade uruode déji to uje chi sijnaque yoque gu. Idájapíse yóguiji Dupade. Idája yoque jnese mu Dupade gajneque yoque. Dupa Pujnuéngaisṍri gajneque yoque jnese, mu yipójo yocadode ome uté, enga que cucha ome yoque uje Dupade chijnóra yoque. Yojnico uje yanguretágo Dupade, mu yicagóji quedéjnai ome iji ujade. (Uyu jé u to. Chamáñaje sijnaque cuchape yu gu.)
english: “People of this world are going to suffer,” Jesus said. “What we teach will be convicting to them and they won’t like us for it, but be strong. And remember how they hated the things I taught, as well.” Jesus Christ said: “And remember that I didn’t do anything wrong, but still they mistreated me. How much more will they mistreat you in the future.” Jesus Christ said: “But whoever goes through this with faith in Me, that one will be saved.”

ayoreo:
Uacamóño Zaquéo gotique to uje
chi oriechoqui, sijnaque rĩ, mu*el Señor
Jesucrísto*u uje cojñone ore charatáyime ga uté chijna aja daguíjnai ga chi
jé anire, cojñoi chi chojninga: “Jé gotique gu a uje chiese pãraque Jesus múñi,
jé taguíji cojñoque sijnai uté gari.”
english: We’re grateful to Dupade in these days, that we aren’t going through the things Jesus told about; we are not suffering for him. It isn’t because we are such good people that Dupade is protecting us from those things. Rather, Dupade’s words tells us that we are all sinful. We are all far from Dupade. Even though we’re all far from Him, we do rightfully belong to Him. Dupade who is Almighty possesses us all, but we hide from him, and we don’t appreciate how much he loves us. We claim to believe in him, but we all turn away from him at times. (I include myself. For I am a great big sinner!)

ayoreo:
Mu Jesus chi chudute ore uruode
ga chi cho quique enga chojninga: “Que cho ujetiga yicai jnumi tudé gu ujetiga
ucué erámi jogosórone ore ujetiga uome ore, ujetiga paraque ore. Yucuegode
udojo u uje gajnareque ore adode,” chi chojninga Jesus. Jé jno doquidode údi
enga chojninga: “Yucuegode u erámi jogosórone ore uje sijnaque ore. Yucué
sijnangone ore iji erámi.”
english: Take for example Zacchias, who we don’t know personally, except that he was a thief, and very sinful. Remember how Zacchias took The-great-one-we- listen-to, Jesus Christ, to his house, even though the people resented him for it. One of the people said, “How is it that people claim that Jesus in so good, but here he is eating with a sinful man.”

ayoreo:
Yocacaranguipise gajiné uje Jesus
ute chimate yogaja re, uje chiraja yocúringane sijnaque múñi. Chimate bisidi. Enga
bisidi tu datoringai casicaite.
english: But Jesus heard their words and he explained what he was doing. He said: “I didn’t come down to this earth to look for people who were good and righteous! The ones I came to find are those who do bad things.” That’s what Jesus said to them. He went on explaining, “I’m looking for people on earth that are sinful. I’m seeking the sinners of this world.”

ayoreo:
Enga yujnusínango to gajiné iji deréjnane jnese uje Jesuse u uje chopo ore
quique rĩ enga chojningame ore ‘Jesus acadigode.’ Yocacadisṍri u Jesuse to
uje qué cho yoquiquique ome cuchade to iji deréjnane unojo to, uje uté
acadigode jé upo yoque rĩ. Mu adi jec u. Jesucrísto adi jec u uje cho
yoquiquique ome cuchade jeta yirajágo quigode to. Mu arecujaidie u uje chi
chojninga: “Cachi que yiraja ya, gusu Yai uje chiraja. Que yiraja
yiríringárique yúrique mu ujetiga uacapótayo gajiné jne enga a qué yo quique
uñéngome uaque jne,” chi chojninga.
english: We should be so thankful that Jesus brought us to himself even while we were yet sinners. He even had a remedy ready. The remedy was his death.

ayoreo: Ga jé u uje chatata deréjnai ujetiga di jne. Chojninga: “A chaguhéode déji erámi tudé jne. A adúcho pocaningane anírone jne – pocaningane ujnoméone iji data. Mu jé ca uacagutéraque cho jora ome pocaningane jne. Enga jé chi chaguhérigo déji erámi jne rĩ. Uje jnupe tãrachugode chi déji erámi rĩ.”
english: We should remember every day how Jesus was quick to explain things to those he called ‘his disciples.’ This is because he is also our teacher and we are his disciples. And he is treating us as such, He explains everything we need to understand, as well. This is what he said, referring to his return, “I don’t even know when I will return, only my Father knows. But if you want me to, I will explain to you some of the things about my coming back again.” That is what he said to them.

ayoreo:
Enga uje yinóninga udojo to enga
a déji erámi jnese. Déji erámi jnese udojo cuchade.
english: And then he went on to tell about what would happen when he returned. He said: “There will be hunger all over this earth. You will hear about wars – the news of wars happening in different places. But don’t be frightened because of those wars. There will also be famines and earthquakes on earth at that time.”

ayoreo: Qué upo rĩ uje chi uruode uñéngo tocade diríca. Que yiraja puniri uje chi chejna – que yiraja ujeta 3,000 u yugode iji cojñone. Enga yáyipie jĩrique déji diríca Jesus uru casodicaite ome yoque. Enga cojñone ica qué ore chujé ñane. Qué ore déji deréjnane unojo to. Tocade Jesus ojningane cucha.
english: And when we start to notice these things, they will be happening all over the earth. The whole earth will be involved.

ayoreo:
*Mu chojninga: “Ca chajuque
uacagutéraque jne. Chamáñaje uacanguretáyo yu gu.”
english: And some of the things Jesus predicted have happened only recently. I don’t know which country it was that was destroyed but I think there were 3,000 people killed there in an earthquake, and when that happened, I quickly thought about the words Jesus spoke so long ago. And not only disasters like this, but we’re hearing about people at war all the time. The things Jesus told about are coming to pass.

ayoreo:
Jé areque gajiné ujetiga yiroco
Jesucrísto yui tiji deréjnai tudé. Uacamóño uje que pijoque go diringai te que.
Chatatame dacadigode ore ga chojninga: “Que yiraja to go yiríringárique dire.”
english: But Jesus said: “Don’t let it frighten you. Because you have believed in me.”

ayoreo:
Mu ojningane déji uje chi
chojninga: “Yiríringai tu ujetiga cojñone jnese, menenéngone ore changureta yu.
Jeta ore chudute yuruode iji uacabijnanone. Ga yinoningaco rĩ uje e garósi
cojñone iji erámone uje ore doi Dupade adode. Ga yirajágo uje e idógosi Jesuse.
Yirajágo. Yirajágo, mu, ujetiga gosi cáma changureta Dupade ga gosi a cho
bisidéguiji udojo. Mu jec ũré yoque ome yocacajnaquéode udojo uje que yẽcaco
quitique go. Mu yocajnai u Jesucrísto uruode ome yoque.
english: It’s good for us to be looking for Jesus Christ’s return in these times. You know his arrival date has not been revealed. He told his disciples about this and he said: “I don’t even know the day of my arrival.”

ayoreo: Mu yujnusínango to gajiné uje chi deréjnai tudé chi yocajnúone u anírone, yocajnúi tu Noé. Jesus cho quique enga chojninga: “Noé urásade ore cha tangári Noé acajnaquéode nanique. Enga a ore cha yu to jne rĩ enga a ore cho cucha bisidéguiji yuruode. A ore chicadígui jeta yiríringai tu. A ore cha yu enga a ca ore changureta yu go.”
english: But there’s more he told. This is what they say he said: “My arrival will only happen when all the Cojñone and the Tribal people have had a chance to believe in me; when, from your mouths, they have heard my words.”

ayoreo:
A déji deréjnai tudé uje cojñone
jnese iji erámi ore chijna erámi jogosórone cucha ga que qué ore chucuepoi
Dupade gotique rĩ. Enga a ca ore chucuépoi daquetangónique gotique rĩ.
Chamáñaje ore chijnóra erámi jogosórone cucha gu. Ore chijnóra ga ore cho Dupa
iji erámi jogosórone cucha. Mu a pininaquei jogadi u ome yoque ujetiga Jesus di
enga chise yoque uje yiyágo yoquijnoraquéode ome erámi jogosórone cucha iji
deréjnane unojo. Uerate ujetiga yanguretágo
Jesus. Uyu ga yangureta Jesus iji deréjnane unojo ga ũré yu ome yanguretigai.
Que qué yipótagopo erámi jogonórone cucha rĩ, enga pijoque uje ũré yoque ome
yocanguretigade.
english: And we must take note of the fact that Jesus will be coming soon when there are few Cojñone in the world who follow Dupade. This we know. We know it, but when someone has not yet believed in Dupade, he considers all of this as unimportant. But what we are teaching is the truth, and we haven’t changed any of what Jesus said. But we are following the words that Jesus Christ spoke to us.

ayoreo: Que yimo ujetiga gajnárecho yacajnaquéode ome uaque. Mu Jesus arecujaidie u ome yoque ome panguretaretigai yui. Chojninga: “Adotáyo uaque. Uaque gosi anguretigai ajãpe marínga, jé omi tu. Omi tu dirome ujetiga uaque gosi changureta yu. Ajnóra bojode ayoré,” chojningapo Jesuse rĩ uje cho quique dacadigode, “Ajnóra bojode ayoré.” Que gusu ore, mu Jesus cho yoquiquique to, enga oquigode jé chojmára ome yoque jnese uje Jesus acadique yoque iji yoquijogadi dire udé to.
english: We must remember now that he said we would be like the countrymen of Noah. Jesus explained this, saying: “The people of Noah’s time stopped listening to his teachings. And they will stop listening to mine also and consider my teachings unimportant. They will forget all about my coming back again. They will have nothing more to do with me and will not put their trust in me at all.”

ayoreo:
Jecute gajneri, yicoi ga yijnórango
ñane gajiné. Yoco yoquihermáno iji ñane iji deréjnai tudé. Que taque Jesus go
ome uyoque, mu omi tu ujetiga yanguretágopíse. Omi tu. Jesuse diringai idógosi.
Idógosi Jesuse diringai. Ca yachógo yui. Ca yachógo Jesuse diringai yui.
english: The day is coming when people all over the earth will only be concerned with the things of the world, and no longer seek the things of Dupade. They will not look for a savior. That’s because they love the things of this world. In fact, they’ll make idols out of the things of the world and worship them as though they were Dupade. But for us, it will be a time of great joy when Jesus comes back and finds that we have stopped loving the things of the world.

ayoreo:
Uacamóño uje Jesuse que pijoque diringárique
mu idógosi, mu yirajágo uje Yocai Dupade u uje chiraja, enga gusu uté. Uje chi
cojñone chudute Dupade uruode jnese ga diringai e u. Jecute yicoi enga yico
Jesus ujnomei údi, enga ca yoco uja yocatatagode Jesus acho.
english: It’s good for us to believe in Jesus. I believe in Jesus more and more all the time, and I’m a true believer. Let’s no longer want the things of this world, that will show that we are sincere about our faith in Jesus.

ayoreo:
San Pedro uruode u udojo ome
yoque. Cho yoquiquique jeta ca yachógo Jesus diringai yui.
english: I don’t know if what I am teaching you is ugly to you. But it’s what Jesus said to us about becoming believers. He said, “Be strong. Even though your faith is small, it’s still good. It will be a very good thing in the future for the person who has put his faith in me and has loved his fellow-ayoréos.”

ayoreo: Enga chi íjnoque tiempo dirome. Íjnoque tiempo ujetiga qué ayorérique chojninga: “Jesuse, yanguretape ua to gajiné iji deréjnai tudé. Uje e bãri enga yimo ua ome yedodie gu.”
english: This is what Jesus counseled people to do. His counsel wasn’t only for the people then, but Jesus counsels us today, and his counsel is the same for all of us who are his disciples whatever our generation.

ayoreo:
Mu Jesuse chi chojningame uyoque
jne uje que yanguretágo to diríca: “Yisiome deréjnai casodé ga íjnoque anire,
ga yucué bei tiji darujna aoi to que, mu que yise go. Que yise bei go – que
yise bei gotique.”
english: Therefore, right now let’s start loving each other. Let’s be like brothers and sisters in these times. Jesus did not lie to us about anything, so it’s a good thing to really believe what he has told us. A very good thing. Jesus is coming soon. Jesus’ arrival is approaching. Let’s not stop looking for him. Let’s not be weary as we wait for Jesus to arrive.

ayoreo:
Jeoga uyu. Qué yo gajnúji yu.
Uje Jesuse ujetiga chajire darujna aoi ga yi que déji to ga a yojninga: “Cachi
yacate ayoréode diríca, yujode ayoréo ore.”
english: You know that the exact date of Jesus’ arrival isn’t known, but it’s soon. We know Dupade who is our father knows, but only he. He said that when the whole world has heard, then he’ll come. Therefore, let’s take the news of Jesus, and not stop telling about him.

ayoreo:
Dupade a chiraja yu. Chiraja uje
yo yiquetangóri iji yate arégode enga que yáyipie déji piquetangóri Jesucrísto
adode uje tẽra re ome yoque.
english: Time will run out. It will be too late then for a person to say, “Jesus, I will believe in you right now, today. For you have now returned and I have seen you with my very own eyes.”

ayoreo:
Que cucha uñeque chiqueta yoque
go – jeoga yocatígo u, jeoga yoquirajatigade u, jeoga picatecãraitigade u – mu
gusúpusu Jesus u yoquiquetangóri. A Dupade chuchẽre piagode gáyode iji dedai
ome gosi ujetiga uté cho daquetangóri iji Jesuse enga gusu iji Jesus. Ujetiga
yo Jesucristo anguretaisórone uñai iji yu gajiné, mu yãrai jé u yu ujetiga
taque yu.
english: But Jesus will say to those of us who hadn’t believe in him: “I gave you plenty of time, and now there’s no more time left; I have looked for your name in the book, but I didn’t find it. I didn’t find your name at all – I didn’t find it anywhere.”

ayoreo:
Mu que yãra uaque go.
Yacaranguipis ome Dupade uje yangureta Jesucristo to ga que yãra uaque go ome
yanguretaretigai. Yiraja uje Espíritu Sánto déji yu. Yipésupú ga ũrepíse uaque
to ome uacanguretaretigai ome Dupade Abi Jesucristo enga jé edúyo
uacanguretigai ome uté.  E gusu.
english: Let’s say I was that person. I’ll use myself as an example. If Jesus looked at the book and my name was not there, I would say to Him: “But I’ve taught people about you, I taught my fellow-ayoréos.”

ayoreo: *Dijaide – Rincón de Tigre, Bolivia – 1970**Marina de Morarie churúsãre Dijaide acajnaquéode unojo*
english: Dupade would know me. He would know that I considered my good works to be my savior, and that I wasn’t depending on what Jesus Christ did when he sold himself for me.

ayoreo:
**Dijaide catecã́ri Dupade:**

Dupade, yocacaranguipis ome ua iji deréjnai tudé uje yujnusínangopo ua rĩ, yoque Dupade.Bamo yoque jnese, yoque Dupade, iji jnumi tudé. Que bamojmárique cucha cus ujetiga ca chimo yoque. Mu icatecã́ri ua gajnerĩ ome yoc ujetiga yirajágo baruode quitígo to gajnerĩ, yoque Dupade. Que yirajágo baruode quitígo, mu garósosi uje yajneque, mu baruode que qui tu ujetiga batṍri yoc ujetiga yoquirajatigade jiei, yoque Dupade, iji baruode. A garósi yoquirajatigade iji baruode marínga, mu qué batṍri yoc ujetiga yicoi baroquéode.

Yicatecã́ri ua iji deréjnane
jnese ome yoque, yoque Dupade. Bamo uje uásejna poguiédode. Que ayorérique déji
siñeque ujetiga uerate atígome ua jnese, yoque Dupade. Jé cho yu, uje que
uerate yatígome ua jnese, mu yigaidi tu baroquéode, yoque Dupade. Jecute
yicatecã́ri ua ujetiga átaja yu gajnerĩ, yoque Dupade, enga yiraja yuru
quitígo iji yujode ayoréo iquei ome baruode quitígo yoque Dupade.

Yacaranguipis uje yise baruode, yoque Dupade. Yise uje yicatecã́ri ua jetiga yiraja baruode quicho. Mu yicatecã́ri ua iji dirode jnese ga diga bangome yu, yoque Dupade, ome cuchade quigode jnese ujetiga yajnésãco baruode quigode, yoque Dupade.

Enga yirajatigai ome baruode quérã iji deréjnane jnese, yoque Dupade. Átaja yu gajnerĩ jeta yiraja yuru quitígo ome ayoréode ore, yurasade ore. Uje que yiraja baruode quitique, mu yacaranguipise uje batatame yu iji deréjnane to ujetiga yajire baruode enga que yicadígapo ua rĩ. Yoque Dupade, yacaranguipis ome ua uje bangome yu uje bojninga: “Yibájoi ayoréode.” Enga bachája yiquei baruode jnese omitigade ome ore, yoque Dupade.

Yacaranguipis ome ua uje bamo uje íjnoque Satanase*poder*iji yu. Enga*ba-poder e*déji yu, yoque Dupade. Uje Satanase*poder*u uje gajnáreque. Chetaguipise baruode, yoque Dupade, ga idája Satanas, irógode iji ua, yoque Dupade. Jé idógosi barógode iji ua, yoque Dupade.Yacaranguipis ome ua gajnerĩ uje bangopísome yu barusarane uje bacháji yiquei, yoque Dupade. Átaja yu gajnerĩ, jeta yiraja yuru quitígo gajnerĩ iji baruode, yoque Dupade. Que yajéngome gotique múñi, Satanas u uje déji yajei ga yajéngome baruode diríca, yoque Dupade. Angopise baruode ome yu iji deréjnane jnese iji yiquigade, yoque Dupade, mu ujetiga bajéo jeta déji yajéode, yoque Dupade. Ca be yajéode iji gajnerĩ uje bajéngome yoque ujetiga jec ũré Satanas déji yajei.

Yiraja uje bangome yu, bacha
baruode aja yiquei, yoque Dupade. Mu que cho jetiga gusu yoque, Dupade, uje
bacha baruode aja yoquiquéode jnese, ayoréode uyoque, ujetiga yirajágopise
baruode quitígo ome yoque.

Atapísaja yu ujetiga yiraja
quitígo ome baruode ome banguretasórone iji yocurágue ajei.

Átaja Davídé to iji sañeque
ujetiga diapo jne iji guidai rĩ.

Yacaranguipis ome ua ome cuchade
jnese, yoque Dupade. Yicatecã́ri ua ome bajnacari Jesuse i. Amẽ.

**Yucué ujetiga Dupade Uruode quicho ome yu, ome ayoréode yocate gajnaréngone:**Yacaranguipise ome Dupade iji deréjnai udé uje Dupade chingo daruode aja yu. Domíngo bajai diríca uje yajengopísome Dupade Uruode iji quigade. Mu chise diri uje yitodo yujode Dupade anícho, quicho, Dupade uruode quicho, ujetiga yajnai jnumi mu que yise go. Que yise gotique. Yajire mu jé ate. Jé ate uje que yise diríca. Que yise Dupade Uruode quitíque go.

Mu jecucha yajire. Yucué ujetiga Dupade Uruode quicho ome yu ome ayoréode yocate gajnaréngone. Yocate gajnarétic. Yucué, yucué, yucué, yucué Dupade uruode múñi, jé ate. Mu iji ejo uje que yise gu, uje Satanase chãraque Dupade Uruode ome yu. Chãraque ga que yise iji Dupade Uruode go. Que yise ga que yajire Dupade Uruode go. Jé ate.

Mu yicatecã́ri Dupade iji yajei. Que yiraja uje yajéngome yu, mu Satanase u uje chãraque Dupade Uruode ome yu, enga que yise cucha dacatique iji Dupade Uruode ome yu, enga que yise cucha dacatique iji Dupade Uruode go jetiga yiji Dupade Uruode. Mu chise dire uñai enga dire uñai diríca enga yajire Dupade Uruode.

Yajna jnumi. Yojninga: “¿Jé dési rique Dupade Uruode quigode, jetiga Dupade Uruode quigode?” Mu jé ate. Que yise go. Enga e achopise. Enga e achopise uje yicatecã́ri Dupade.

**Jomoné catecã́ri yoque ome Dupade uruode nanique:**Mu aníri, ugúcoi campamento, ayoréode campamento iji Santa Cruz uéchai. Jomone catecã́ri yoque ome Dupade Uruode. Chojninga: “¿Davídé, arajáyo Dupade Uruode jnese to a?”

Mu Davídé chojninga: “Jomoné, que yiraja Dupade Uruode jnese go.” Mu ueradipise Davídé uruode ome yu uje chi ca chiraja Dupade Uruode jnese go.

Ga Jómone chojninga: “Davídé,
yocate gajnaréngone garani tu Satanas iji ujade. Jé garani u yoc iji ujade to.”

Enga Davídé irasiguipise Jómone. Chojninga: “Jomoné, jé arec. Jé areque Dupade áyipiedie ga ate sijnaque yu.” Enga Jómone chacate yoque*poquito*ome Dupade Uruode. Chacate yoque.

Mu dire uñai yucuegopoi Dupade Uruode rĩ. Yajningopo aja anire rĩ. Yajningopo yoquiquigade ome Dupade Uruode. Uje yicatecã́ri Jómone. Yojninga: “Jomoné, ¿Dupade Uruode quitígo déji rique iji erámi jetiga Dupade chetaguipise yoque?”

Mu Jomoné que chiraja quitique go. Que yirajágo quitique gu, uje Satanase chetaque ujetiga yicoi Dupade Uruode. Que yiraja quitique go. Davídé que chirajápo rĩ. Que yirajágopo gotique rĩ. Jómone que chirajápo rĩ. Que yirajágo gotique uje yicadíjoi Dupade Uruode gu.*Bijna iji yocatoidie*.

Mu yajníngoja ga yicagoi
eduguéjnane uje ore chiraja Dupade Uruode quitígo jeta ore chátaja yoque. (Mu
chise diríca ga jé yico. Yucuegopoi rĩ.)

Mu don Len u uje chiraja. Chiraja Dupade gueteguipísode. Chojninga: “Jé obiréjna cuchade jnese ome Dupade,” chojninga don Len.

Mu yojninga: “Davídé yucuegoi diríca udore mu que yijnicórique cuse. Uje Satanase áyipiedie déji yoque gu.”

Jé basa don Len chiraja. Chojninga: “Jé jiei cucha dacadode jnese,” mu chatata uje chi ome Dupade ujetiga gosi chujé dojórique ayoré, jeta chiraja Dupade, mu jé chujé dojórique ayoré uje chi cucha detéguipise u udore. Mu chi gajnui jec upo ujetiga uacuñeque pésu cucha gajnáretique ga gajnui jec u. Dupade chi chãraque. Ujetiga gajnui jec u ujetiga uacuñeque chujé dojórique ayoré. Mu adi jé u.

Cáma yisóco Dupade Uruode iji yoquiquigade. Cáma chacája yajei go. Que yise Dupade Uruode unétique go. Que chacája yajei. Que yiraja. Mu dirode udojo u uje que yajire Dupade Uruode uje Satanas qué chãraque Dupade Uruode ome yu. Charángome yu ga que yise Dupade uru quitique go.

**Yise udojo yajegaidi casodé Dupade áyipiedie:**Mu diga deréjna casodé e yise diríca. Yise. Ga yacaranguipis ome Dupade to. Chingome yu udojo. Chingome yu. Yajire urusarane. Yise udojo yajegaidi casodé Dupade áyipiedie. Yise iji uruode uje chingome yu.

Chojninga: “Be udojo.” Ga yajire
enga jé u. Uje Dupade chojninga iji daruode: “Uacuñeque a gajnáreque atígo
maringa, gosi chajnésãru yu ga yisapo uaque gosi rĩ. Acatecãchoi yu iji
deréjnane jnese.” Chojninga: “Ujetiga gosi pota,” Dupade chojninga, “enga a
yátaja uaque jnese. Que yetetique déji uaque go. Mu jé uaqueteque yu,”
chojninga uruode. “Ujetiga gosi cha yu enga gosi chajnésãrapo yu rĩ enga
yajnésãrapo gosi te rĩ.”

Chojninga: “Ujetiga irajágode
sue gai que, gosi catecã́ri yu ome dajegaitique cucha ga a yisi ome gosi. Enga
que toc jeta qué bijnapome uté gosi rĩ.”

Uje yajire udojo enga yacaranguipis ome Dupade acajnaquéode ome yu iji deréjnane. Chojningapome yu rĩ: “Ujetiga uaque gosi pésu, ga uaque gosi chojninga: Yangureta Dupade, mu gosi que changureta, enga gosi jé ãra u re. Chãra re. Jé basa gosi uruode uerácho uje chojninga: Yangureta Dupade, ga jec ũré ga que chãra re go.”

Idájapise Dupade Uruode to diríca, mu yacaranguipise uje chisi ome Espíritu Santo ome yu rĩ. Chisi ome yu ga jé que jnoji yu gotique.

Pajegátigai u uje Satanase déji yu diríca, ga yajéngome aníri, Dupade Uruode. Yajéngopísome Dupade Uruode, mu Dupade chijocase Satanas iji yajei, enga e déji yu aníri, Dupade Uruode iji quigade.

Enga yacaranguipísome to udojo
uje Dupade que etatique cuse uje cháta raja yoque. Que etatique cuse go.
Ujetiga yipótago yipeco cuchárique, enga yicoi. Ujetiga ca yipótago, enga ca
yicoi.

Uje Satanase déji yu enga,
diríca enga, ipota ujeta yiya. Ga jé yiyapúsu Dupade Uruode. Enga que
yanguretarása to ujetiga ga yãraque yu ome Dupade rĩ. Mu Dupade jé chajo raja
yu to uje Espíritu Sánto chiraja. Yiraja iji yajei Espíritu Sánto, ga que
yicadígui Dupade Uruode go. Yicadígui mu, que yicadígui. Dupade que chicadígui
yu to go. Ga jé déji yu.

Chingo daruode garósi, garósi, garósi aja yu. Yacaranguipis ome Dupade uje que cucha uajátique cus ome go. Uje chi uajádome ayoréode uyoque ga que uajádome Dupade go. Que toc ujetiga yipójo yocatique ome Dupade go.

Dupade chimo yoque jnese, yoquipoguiédode cucha, cucha gajnaréngone. Dupade chojninga: “Idájapise uáguiji yu, mu jé yijnóra uac.” Adi u yocáyipie dépoi aníri diríca, Jonás rĩ.

Jonás u uje chi quejna ome Dupade. Chi quejna ga chi jno. Chi pota jóroque ujetiga pógu re ome Dupade. Mu chi Dupade chimo enga jé urásu uje Dupade cho dajuñame suqué. Mu pésu udore gu ujetiga Jonás catecãrápoi ome rĩ. Ga chajnésãrapome rĩ. Jnusinapo date gajnaré casodé ga catecã́ri Dupade iji aníri, oyite cuchábi ajei. Catecã́ri ga Dupade chudúdapo rĩ. Uje jé déji anire múñi, gajño pioi ajei múñi. Cáma gajño pioi, mu e Dupade chosi dajuñame, mu gaidi u ujetiga chijnimapome rĩ iji aníri, oyite cuchábi. Dupade pota chijnimapo Jonás iji ate gajnaréngone rĩ. Ga chi jé u uje que toque anírami, suté anírami, Jonás. Que toque jeta qué ayorérique yimóngoporase rĩ iji oyite cuchábi ajei.

Mu chi chajnésãrapo Dupade rĩ.
Chajnésãru ome Dupade ga Dupade chichapoi aja diri rĩ. Ga jec u uje chi ca
chapo rĩ iji ejoi. Chacate cojñone iji anire, iji udore idáidatéode. Ga que
pesapo date gajnarétique rĩ, uje Dupade e chichapo iji dirie gu. Chimo re
diríca iji Dupade ujuyaquei ajei, mu chi ca chajnésãrapo iji daquigade iji ejo
rĩ.

Mu yocáyipie udojo u uje Dupade chisi ome yoque anírone, udore quigode ujetiga ca yiyágopo rĩ ujetiga yocajnui su aníre, Jonás, uje Dupade ujuyagarãque mu chichapoi diríe rĩ. Chichapoi diríe rĩ enga changuretapise Dupade iji ejoi. Enga jetapũré uyoc enga a ca yimóngo Dupade ujuyaquérique go, uje irasiguipise yoc. Irasiguipise yóguiji deréjnane, uje cho dabi iji yoque gu yocanguretaretigai iji Abi Jesuse.

Jetiga gosi pógu datique,
ayorérique, mu jé u uje pógu iji dajei. Chingo raja erámi, gaidi u gu ujetiga
ca ore chiraja. Mu Satanase jĩraque déji gosi. Mu ujetiga gosi pota jeta
chajniapója Dupade gai rĩ, enga bisidode déji. Bisidi déji. Bisidi u ujetiga
gosi chajnésãrapo rĩ.

Yacaránguipís ome Dupade uje
yise Dupade Uruode to rĩ. Diríca u uje yipota erámi jogonórone cucha ga yijos
eti ueracho. Mu Dupade chingome yu diríca, chingome yu iji daruode.

Cojñone iplátadie bijna. Ayoré,
cojñone origuidedie bijna. Gosi ujeta ugucharãque, ugúchade bijna, jé aja ayoré
gajnaréngone ore uguchade a bijna to jne. A cuchade bijna jnese iji erámi.
Cojñone ipotiguéode bijna. Udore cojñone ore jódode uje cojñone ore irasique, yote
pioi uje cojñone ore chóji enga ore chisi ome dajõrane a bijna to jne to rĩ.
Udojo u uje Dupade chingome yu. Uje que yise Dupade Uruode quitique, enga
yiraja uje udore bijna to ome yu, uje que yiraja cuchárique diríca, mu
quigade enga bijna ome yu enga yicadigui.

**Udore u uje chingome yu, Dupade:**Yuchẽre Dupade urusarane gai ga jé u uje yijnorapise udojo. Yijnorapíse Dupade uruode. Ga chatatame yu udojo. Cho ujetiga Dupade jé upúsu, mu jé u uje cho gojñame yu ome unojo. Cho quique uje ore chojninga: “Cuchade a bijna jnese jne.” Unojo yajiríringai. Que cucha onique chi ca déji jnumi go. Que cucha onique cuse go jeta yijnóra udore, cuchade iji jnumi tudé.

Mu chi cucha omi déji ayorei iji
de ujetiga qué*tiempo*déji ome
yocuñeque, enga gosi changuretape Dupade. Jetiga gosi ca pota Dupade ga, mu
Dupade tibidi gosi. Tibidi iji deréjnane jnese. Dupade que chetaque yoque go
ujetiga ayorérique catecã́ri. Que Dupade chetaque yoque go.

Mu guetei chojmára iji yoc.
Guetei u yocate gajnarei. Chi ipéco cucha gajnarétique ga ayoréode uyoque. Ga
Dupade e chajire quedéjnane ome yoque. Adi jé u ayoréode uje yajico quedéjnane
ome cuchade, cucha gajnaréngone. Chi ujetiga ipéco cucha gajnarétique ga Dupade
e chajire quedéjnane ome yoc.

Jé basa, Satanase a irasique
yoque uje ipéco cucha gajnarei. Mu uje qué yipéco cucha gajnarétique, enga Dupade
chingome yoquisocade ome yoque. Uje yipésu yate gajnarétique ga Dupade Uruode
déji yajei enga yiraja yúringai máraque ome Dupade. Mu Dupade e chajire ta.
Jé chajire quedéjnane. E cho dáyugu. E cho dáyugu.

Jé basa Satanas irasique re uje e chijócãre Dupade Uruode iji yocajei. Jé basa Dupade chetaque yoquisijnaringuéone, mu chijnóra yoque. Jé basa gosi ujetiga Satanas uruode déji ajei, ga Satanase chajire quedéjnane ome uje uté gosi cha adode. Mu Dupade jé basa, irasique gosi uje cha Satanas adode.

Mu Satanase chucuepoi dasocáringome
uté gosi rĩ, chucué, chucué, chucué, mu Dupade surupúsu gosi gai. Suru
gai. Enga Satanás que chise dasocárique ujetiga gosi uajádiji Dupade Uruode.

Jé basa gosi ajirítigade chi
gare. Ca gusu Dupade Uruode uje gosi chajire, mu ajirique Dupade to. Changureta
Dupade enga changuretapo uruode rĩ, enga chajo raja dajirítigade udore
gatuaque.

Satanase chise ga jĩrique
chacája. Chise dabi tiji ga jĩrique chijócãre Dupade achietigade iji ajei,
Espíritu Santo. Mu que toc jeta qué cha. Mu qué Dupade chacapo ajei rĩ. Udore
u uje Dupade chingome yu udore uje chi Satanas, Dupade ore chijócãre ñane iji
uñane ajéode.

Satanase a déji yajei. Ga a e chijócãre Dupade omítigarique jõ. Dupade idája iji yu. Dupade que gajneque yu. Que edo chajire yu. Dupade uruode chi qui u udojo. Uje chingome yu iji arode. Enga yajirapo Dupade rĩ enga Dupade chajirapo yu rĩ. Chajire yu.

Yicatecã́ri Dupade. Jecute Satanase e serẽrája iji yajei. Adi u uje Dupade chajnésãra iji yajei te rĩ. Dupade Uruode quigode u udojo iji daruode. Yacaranguipis ome Dupade uje yise qui; Dupade u uje cho gojñame yu.

Explicó cómo podría estar limpio de nuevo. Porque Dupade también me dijo que cuando echa a Satanás, se va. No solo hace esto por mí. Dupade regresa para ayudarnos a todos. Y cuando regresa, se queda conmigo. El Espíritu Santo en mí es fuerte entonces. Y cuando el Espíritu Santo está en mí, él echa a Satanás fuera de mí y lo hace irse. Y Satanás siente lástima de sí mismo, porque volvemos a Dupade. Y eso es lo que sucede: hay una pelea en nuestros corazones entre Dupade y Satanás.*(Gálatas 5:17)*

Yipótago Dupade ga Dupade chijócãre Satanase tiji yocajei uje ipótago Dupade gu. Jé basa, ipótago Dupade que, mu ipótago Satanas enga chijócãre Dupade enga gusu Satanase iji yocajei.

Ga udojo u uje Dupade chingome yu cuchade udojo. Uje yicoi uruode, Dupade áyipiedie quitígo, enga Dupade chátaja yoque. Mu que yise diríca. Mu e yise iji deréjnane udojo. Cachi u uje yijnorapise acajnaquéode ome yu. Acajnaquéode que cho jeta gusu yu. Dupade Uruode chojninga: “Idájapise uáguiji Dupade jnese.”

Ajiri u uyu. Ajiri u ayorérique.
Chajire ua iji quedéjnane. Chi ca déji siñeque jeta Dupade ca churúsãre yocate
gajnaréngone. Churúsãre yocate omitigade. Jetiga yocatígo, mu e chicháji
darusãrai. Jeta yatique gajnáreque ga e chicháji. Mu yicatecã́ri ome yodi ajei
ga chĩro uqué case enga chicadígui. Chojninga: “E yĩro. Acadígui to. E
acadíjoi.”

Dupade e chĩro gu. Cho jetiga
gosi ca pésu cucha gajnaretique diríca. Chĩro. Tṍra darujna aoi. Chĩropise.
Ga Dupade pésu ga yicadíjoi. Dupade Uruode déji jnese ome yoc jetiga gosi
chajnésãra Dupade, ga Dupade chĩro gosi ate gajnaréngone. Enga tṍra darujna
ao casé. Tṍra darusãra casé aja guesi. Bamo uje Dupade Uruode e chátaja yu.
Espíritu Sánto e dépoi yu rĩ iji deréjnane unojo. Dupade chingome yu daruode.
Chingome yu uje chi ca cucha onique déji jnumi. Que cucha onique cuse go. Chi
gusu omi tu Dupade. Chimopise yoque uje icatecãcoi jnese. E gusu udojo ome
yocate gajnaréngone uje udore u uje Dupade chátaja yu. Garósi yirajatigade, mu
yacaranguipise ome Dupade uje chingome yu dajnéone unojo.

Uje Satanase déji yajei enga
charanguipise Dupade Uruode ome yu. Yicadígui. Satanase chãraque Dupade Uruode
ome yu, enga que yise quitique go. Que yise quitique, mu diga yise iji quigade.
Uje querénga yise deréjnane to Dupade Uruode. Mu Dupade chingome yu udojo. Uje
chicháji yiquei.

Ga u udojo cuchade. Ujeta apota
ga bei udojo quigode to. Ujeta ca apota ga ca bei. Uje Satanas uru qui tu uje
chingo yoc uje gajnáreque Dupade Uruode. Jé basa Dupade chetaque Satanase.
Satanase chojninga: Cucha bisidécho Dupade Uruode.”

Mu Dupade chojninga: “Jé uyu u
uje yujnué cuchade gáyode jnese. Gusu yu.”

Cachi Satanase urúo uje ore botãre ñane ome daruode. Uje chi ore
chisipise dabáyode, mu jé ũré Dupade. Jé basa ga ãrapataquechoquí Satanase uté. Jé gusu udojo.

**Puruode quigode:***Poder*– Ujopié*Ba-poder –*Tu poderPoquito – GarósiTiempo – Guedé jogadi

*Dijaide – Tobité, Bolivia – 1970**Marina de Morarie churúsãre Dijaide acajnaquéode udojo*
english:
*Bamo yoque jnese iji jnumi tudé***Y**

**Dijaide****talks to****Dupade****:**

*Dupade*, we’re thankful to you today as we remember you again.*Yoqui-**Dupade*, you see all of us here on this earth. There is nothing that you can not see, nothing prevents you from seeing us. So, I pray to you right now,*Yoqui-**Dupade*, for us to understand your words. We don’t understand your words, though I have a little understanding of them, but you do not require that we have great understanding of your words.Even though our understanding may be little, you still use us to serve you.

**No one follows you perfectly:**

I pray daily for us,*Yoqui-Dupade*. You know that there are many hidden things. There’s no one anywhere who follows you perfectly in every way. It’s the same with me, for not all my ways are beautiful to you, and yet I want to serve you,*Yoqui-Dupade*. Therefore, I ask you to help me right now,*Yoqui-Dupade*, to understand your words as I stand here in front of my fellow Ayoreos to teach them from your words.

**Dupade helps us to find things in his Word:**

I’m thankful that I have found what I was looking for from your Word. I found it, after talking to you about it and asking to understand it. And I talk to you every day,*Yoqui-**Dupade*, that you might show me from your Word what I need to review.

May my knowledge of your words be increasing every day,*Yoqui-**Dupade*. Help me right now to find the right words to explain your words to my people. When I don’t understand your words, thankfully you tell me the meaning every time, and I do not forget how much you have helped me.*Yoqui-**Dupade*, I thank for inviting me and telling me: “Let’s go to the Ayoreos.”. And then you put before me all the good things from your word to share with them.

**Dupade’s Power prevents Satan from controling us:**

I am grateful to you that you have shown me that Satan’s*u**jopié*cannot control me now that your*Ujopié*is in me,*Yoqui-**Dupade*. Satan’s*u**j**opié*is ugly. He despises your words,*Yoqui-**Dupade*, and Satan and his angels are far from you. Your angels are close to you,*Yoqui-**Dupade*.

I’m very grateful today that you have explained clearly to me the writings from your Words and have brought them to my attention. Help me today to know how to explain your Words,*Yoqui-**Dupade*. Even though I am not ashamed of your words now, it was Satan inside of me in the past that made me ashamed of them.Now that my past is behind me, I want you to tell memore andmore of your words every day,*Yoqui-**Dupade*, if it is your will for them to be in my heart. But if you are ashamed of me, if it’s true that Satan is still inside me, then don’t do what I ask.

**Dupade helps us to understand and to apply his Word:**

I know that you have told me your words and brought them to my attention,*Yoqui-**Dupade*. But it’s not as though you are only concerned with us, for you have brought your words to the attention of all of us Ayoreos, so the we might understand well what your words mean.

Help me to understand your word as I teach your believers gathered here today.

Help Davidé who is away that he will soon be back in our village with us.

Thank you,*Yoqui-**Dupade*, for all these things. I am talking to you in the name of your Son Jesus. Amen.

**I began to search****Dupade’s Words****for what it had to say about our sin:**

I thank*Dupade*today that he has finally shown me what his Word says about sin.

But last Sunday I was discouraged after teaching*Dupade’s Words*. And then, one day, I started worrying about what I was going to teach my fellow believers about what*Dupade’s Words*said about sin. I kept trying, but I couldn’t find anything about it, nothing at all. I read some more but with the same results. Just like I didn’t find anything before, I couldn’t find anything now.

But I kept reading. I was looking for what*Dupade’s Words*had for me about why we sin and do ugly things. I looked, I looked, I looked, and I looked, searching*Dupade’s Words*, but with the same results as before. Satan was keeping me from finding anything in*Dupade**W**ords*. Since I couldn’t find anything, I stopped reading*Dupade’s Words*completely. That’s how it was.

**I prayed to find what Dupade’s Word tells us about sin:**

But I talked to*Dupade*in my heart. I didn’t understand why I felt ashamed; but it was Satan who was preventing me from finding what*Dupade*had for me in his Word, and what he wanted me to teach in the coming meeting. But the next day I began to study*Dupade’s Words*again.

I was so burdened. I said: “Where are*Dupade’s Words*about sin? Perhaps there’s nothing in*Dupade’s Words*about why we sin?” I couldn’t find anything about it. I was tired of seeking it, and I was tired of praying about it.

**Jómone talked to****us****about****Dupade’s Words****:**

But we were in an Ayoré camp on the other side of the city of Santa Cruz. Jómone spoke to us about*Dupade’s Words*. He said: “Davidé, do you and Dijaide know all of*Dupade’s Words*?”

And Davidé said: “Jómone, I don’t know everything about*Dupade’s Words*.” It was really comforting to me to hear him say that.

Then Jómone said: “Davidé, I think our sin originates with Satan sometimes, and sometimes from ourselves.”

Davidé was really pleased with what Jómone said. He said to him: “Jómone, I think you’re right.*Dupade*’s thoughts are good, but I’m a sinner.” And Jómone continued teaching us from*Dupade’s Words*for a little while. He taught us well.

The next day we started studying*Dupade’s Words*together again, returning to the subject of sin. I was talking with Jomoné and I said to him: “Jómone, where in the world is it in*Dupade’s Words*that talks about*Dupade*disapproving of us?”

But Jómone didn’t know where it was. We didn’t know where to look, because Satan didn’t want us to discuss*Dupade’s Words*. I didn’t know where the passage was or if it existed at all. Davidé didn’t know where it was either. None of us knew where to find it, not even Jómone. I don’t know why we all forgot everything we knew about the word of*Dupade*. It was as though our heads had emptied themselves of everything we knew about*Dupade*.

But we went back to looking for it and talked with missionaries about it who knew*Dupade’s Words*very well. (We finally did find what we were looking for, but meanwhile kept searching.)

**Don Len said: “Everything is known to Dupade:”**

Don Len knew where it told about the things that*Dupade*really hated. He said: “Everything is known to*Dupade*.”

But I said: “Davidé and I were looking for that passage yesterday, but we couldn’t pull anything out of*Dupade’s Words*because Satan had clouded our minds.”

But don Len, on the other hand, knew about it and he said: “There’s a lot about what*Dupade*disapproves of.” And then he went on to say, that when a person that knows our*Great Leader*kills someone,*Dupade*really hates that, but not just the sin of killing*.*Whatever we do as believers against our fellow man is sin and*Dupade*hates it just as much as he hates the sin of killing. There is no degree of sin.*Dupade*hates all classes of sin.

We were not yet able to go here and there in*Dupade’s Words*. Since I couldn’t understand it, I wasn’t very pleased with*Dupade’s Words*at that time. But that was when Satan was still trying to get me to not read the Bible. Because he was keeping me from*Dupade’s Words*, and I couldn’t get a thing out of it.

**One day I found what I had been looking for:**

I finally found it! And I’m thankful to*Dupade*that I did. He showed it to me while I was reading his word. I found what I was seeking about*Dupade*’s will for us. It was right there in his word and he showed it to me.

He said: “Here it is.”

I looked, and, sure enough, there it was. And this is what*Dupade*said in his words: “Though your sins have been ugly, when you turn back to me, I’ll take you back. And you may pray to me every day.” He said: “Whoever wants my help, I will help him. I do not hate you, but you have hated me.” His word says: “If someone has stopped following me, but turns and comes back again, I will return to him also.”*(James 4:8)*

If something is too hard for a person, but that person asks*Dupade*for help,*Dupade*will help him. And whatever*Dupade*gives us is ours forever and will never be lost.*(**James 1:5)*

**When I learmed that we can return to Dupade after we sin, I was happy:**

When I understood that I could return to*Dupade*, I was very thankful to him for showing me this Scripture, and for teaching me every day. The word of*Dupade*tells about those who are sincere and those who are not. Some of us say, ‘I believe in*Dupade*,’ but they really don’t, and are only deceiving themselves. A person can deceive himself. Others say the same words but are sincere. When they say, ‘I believe in*Dupade*,’ they really do believe in him. A person like that is not deceiving himself.

What was written in*Dupade’s Words*were hidden from me before, but now that*Dupade*has sent his*Ujopié*to help me, they are no longer hidden. And the*Ujopié*that*Dupade*gave to me, will never leave me.

When Satan was at work in me it made me ashamed, and I felt very ashamed as I read*Dupade’s Words*. I was even ashamed to go to meetings, but*Dupade*uprooted Satan in me, and*Dupade’s Words*took his place.

And I’m so thankful that when*Dupade*is with us, he doesn’t find fault with us. There is no condemnation at all. And there is freedom – if I want to go to you, I go. If I don’t want to go to you, I don’t go.*(Romans 8:1,2)*

When Satan was there with me, I was ready to give up. I was ready to quit going to meetings or reading*Dupade’s Words*. I was almost ready to think I’d been fooling myself, and ready to have nothing more to do with*Dupade*. But*Dupade*stuck with me, because his*Ujopié*, the Holy Spirit, understood what was happening. I knew that the Holy Spirit indwelt me, and I couldn’t abandon*Dupade’s Words*. I forgot them, but at the same time, I didn’t forget them. And*Dupade*didn’t forget me. He stayed with me.

**Dupade loves us and continually is working in our hearts:**

He kept bringing his Word to mind gently but consistently. I’m so thankful that there is nothing too hard for*Dupade*. Things are hard for us humans, but not hard at all for*Dupade*. And one thing is certain, we cannot hide what we do from*Dupade*.

*Dupade*sees all of us, the things we hide from others, our sins.*Dupade*says: “Even though you are far from me, I still love you.” And that makes us think about Jonah, doesn’t it?

**When Jonah admitted he had sinned, Dupade rescued him from the big fish:**

Jonah was running away from*Dupade*. He started running and kept going. But it was all in vain, he couldn’t hide from*Dupade*. Since*Dupade*could see him, all he accomplished was to be punished by*Dupade*. But*Dupade*did it so that Jonah would communicate with him again. And while in the belly of the big fish, he admitted it was wrong to disobey*Dupade*and to run from doing what*Dupade*wanted him to do. And when he started talking to him,*Dupade*was ready and willing to hear what he had to say.

Jonah said, “Out of the belly of*Jnaropié*I called to you and you heard my voice.” Well, it wasn’t*Jnaropié*, but he was suffering*Dupade*’s punishment as people suffer in*Jnaropié*, and then dear Jonah could do nothing for himself, none of his fellow humans could see his plight and help him.

And after all that, Jonah probably never stopped following*Dupade*again. He taught people in a large city about*Dupade*. And he never went back to disregarding*Dupade*’s will for him after*Dupade*spared him and took him out of the belly of the great fish. He had experienced what it was like to be punished by*Dupade*, and he never wanted to experience it again.

**Even though Dupade punishes us, when we repent, he forgive us:**

I think*Dupade*told us what happened to Jonah so that when we see what he went through, and how*Dupade*punished him, and then spared him, we will stop rebelling, so that we won’t have to experience what Jonah experienced. After*Dupade*spared him, Jonah had more faith in*Dupade*. And if he spared Jonah, how much more will he spare us and love us who, through his Son, have been made his children. His love for us is eternal.

Let’s say someone hides what he is doing, anyone, and he keeps it inside him. Then he goes on about his business, so no one will know. But Satan quickly takes advantage of him. But, if he wants to come back to*Dupade*, there’s “medicine” for that. There is a way to do that. And the medicine is to turn back to*Dupade*.

I’m so thankful to*Dupade*that I have once again found His Word to be meaningful. Before I did, however, I wanted the things of this world. For me, they were very appealing. But*Dupade*showed me from his Word the truth. And this is what he said to me: “Whoever wants me, must not want the things of this world.”*(1 John 2:15)*

Nothing lasts in this world: people lose their money. The clothing we wear gets old or is lost. Rich and poor lose their things. Everything in this world will one day be lost. The things we covet will be lost after we get them. The liquor that people like and give to each other to drink will ruin them. You can’t hold on to anything in this world, and that is what*Dupade’s Words*tells us. Even the things that we know, we can forget them, and then they are lost to us.*(1 John 2:17)*

**This is****what****Dupade****showed me.**

When I opened his written word, I saw that this is what I really loved. I really loved*Dupade’s Words*. It tells me the truth about everything. When I read it, I don’t hear*Dupade*speaking, but somehow, he tells me things. He explains them to me. And one of the things I’ve come to understand is that: “Everything earthly will end.” Everything I read now, has meaning. I understand now that there is nothing good about the things of the world that I should love them.

But there is good in every person and there is still time for each of us to believe in*Dupade*. Even though a person doesn’t want*Dupade*,*Dupade*is inviting him to come to him. He calls to him every day.*Dupade*doesn’t refuse anyone who talks to him.*Dupade*doesn’t hate any of us.

There is one thing he does hate about us, however. What he hates is our sin. We are sinful people and we all sin. And*Dupade*must look away from us for that reason. It’s like what we do when we see something very strange, very ugly, we look away from it. It says that when we sin,*Dupade*looks away from us.*(Habakkuk 1:13)*

However, Satan is happy to see us sin. But when we do something wrong,*Dupade*lets us know that what we do is sin. When I do something wrong, then*Dupade’s Words*inside of me tells me I’m guilty in the eyes of*Dupade*. But*Dupade*looks away. It makes him sad. He is sad when we sin.

But not Satan, he gets happy when we sin, because he’s managed to make us disregard*Dupade’s Words*that are in our hearts.*Dupade*hates our sin, but he still loves us. On the other hand, when Satan’s words are in a person’s heart, Satan looks away from that person when he stops doing what he wants him to do. But then,*Dupade*is happy, he’s happy because that person has stopped doing what Satan tells him to do.

But Satan looks for a way to tempt that person again, he looks, and looks, and looks, but*Dupade*won’t let him near that person. He prevents him from tempting him. Satan can’t find a way to tempt him when the person is strong in*Dupade’s Words*.*(Psalm 119:11)*

A person must focus on two things: not only to focus on*Dupade’s Words*, but also to focus on*Dupade*himself. He must believe in*Dupade*and believe what his Word says and stay fixed on both*Dupade*and on his Word always.

Whenever Satan comes to a person, he enters suddenly. He looks for an entrance and quickly comes in and tries to take away the joy that person has in his heart – the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit. But he cannot make the Holy Spirit’s joy stop, because*Dupade*is also in the person. And this is what*Dupade*has shown me from his Word: Satan and*Dupade*are always trying to make the other one leave a person’s heart.

When Satan gets inside me, he tries to take*Dupade*’s goodness away from me. And*Dupade*seems far from me.*Dupade*doesn’t control me. He takes his eyes off me. This is what*Dupade’s Words*says. That’s what I’ve read in its pages. But when I turn my eyes back upon*Dupade*, He watches out for me again. He looks at me.

**When we talk to Dupade about our sin, the Holy Spirit is stronger in us:**

I talk to*Dupade*, and Satan’s control over me weakens. That’s how it is when*Dupade*returns to me. This is what*Dupade’s Words*teach us. I’m thankful to*Dupade*that I found out how it works; it was*Dupade*himself that told me this.

He explained how I could be clean again. For*Dupade*also told me that when he casts Satan out, he leaves. Not only does he do this for me.*Dupade*comes back to help all of us. And when he comes back, he stays with me. The Holy Spirit in me is strong then. And when the Holy Spirit is in me, he casts Satan out of me and makes him leave. And Satan feels sorry for himself, because we return to*Dupade*. And that is what goes on: There is a fight going on in our hearts between*Dupade*and Satan.*(Galatians 5:17)*

When we choose*Dupade*, then*Dupade*cast’s Satan out of our hearts because we want*Dupade*. But the reverse is also true, though we wanted*Dupade*before, but now choose Satan, then he takes over in our lives instead of*Dupade*.

And this is what*Dupade*helped me to see about these things. When we follow*Dupade*and his plans,*Dupade*helps us. But that is what I couldn’t do before. But in these days, I have found out how to do it. I must love what*Dupade*teaches me. It’s not as though his teachings were only for me. They’re for all of us.*Dupade’s Words*say: “Once you were all far away from me.”

*Dupade*sees me, and he sees each individual person. He knows what you’re doing all the time. There’s none of us anywhere whose sins have not been written down by*Dupade*. He writes down the good things we have done in his book also. If I’ve done something wrong, it’s written down. But if I’ve talked to him about what is in the deep water, he tells me those sins have been erased and forgotten. He says: “I’ve erased them. So forget them, also. We no longer remember them.”

**Not only does Dupade forgive sin, he forgets it:**

When*Dupade*has forgiven someone’s sin, it’s as though that person had never sinned at all. The sin has been erased and*Dupade*has thrown away the paper they were written on. And he helps us forget the sins we have committed.*Dupade*’s Word is all there for us, so that whoever we are, we can return to*Dupade*, and*Dupade*will forgive all the bad things we have done.
