ayoreo: Yacaranguipise rĩ uje yugúsiji udé yujode Dupade anguretasore ore ajei to iji Cochabamba udaque. Mu uje yirasiguipise yu uje que yicadiguipise Dupade. Que yiramurase Dupade iji yáyipie iji yajei go (*yo guardo mi Dios en mi corazón*). Yacaranguipise rĩ uje Dupade a jé oite yu aja udojo diríca, Cochabamba, ujetiga – uje pésu yininaquei tome dabi Jesucristo.
english: *Que yicadiguipíseDupade*

ayoreo: Dupade a jé iroque yu aja de ujetiga yicáji udé ga jé ca cuchárique chosi tiji yu. Yacaranguipise rĩ uje yacoté e aregue uje yatecãraitigade u gu. Dupade chapúsa dasocade ome yu ome cucha dacadode udojo uje yejna yiguesi ome Dupade uje yicatecã́ri.
english: I’m so grateful that I can be here in Cochabamba with fellow-believers in*Dupade*like you. But what I’m happy about is that I haven’t forgotten*Dupade*. That I haven’t put him out of my mind or my heart. I thank*Dupade*that he brought me to Cochabamba so that I could rejoice with you in His Son Jesus Christ.

ayoreo:
Uje
yicatecã́ri iji dirode jnese, iji dejade jnese, iji yiguíjnai, iji udé rĩ. E
yacaranguipise gajnerĩ uje yacoté e areguepo rĩ. Enga que toque jeta je
yiramurase Dupade iji yajei gotique iji deréjnane iji yoquiquéode, u dire
uñéngo dirome.
english: I’m sure it is*Dupade*that has sent me here to help her and has kept me safe as I traveled. I thank Him that, in answer to my prayers, my wife is doing better.*Dupade*has helped me in so many ways as I’ve been praying fervently to him.

ayoreo: Yacaranguipise rĩ uje Dupade chisi ome páyuquetotiguéi tome yu ome daruode to, uje ¡cho jeta yirajarase cuchárique! Yiraja gu Dupade, u ujetiga yiraja cuchade udojo gu; Dupade u uje chisi ome yu páyuquetotiguéi, páyipiejna. Jecute yiraja cuchade udojo – Dupade atuatigade udojo to. Mu yacaranguipise gajnerĩ uje yáyuquetotigué udé que jnoji yu to gotique. Yacaranguipise uje yirasigápo Dupade Uruode to rĩ.
english: I talk to Him daily and every night as well – in my house, and now, here in Cochabamba also. I’m so thankful right now that my wife is getting well again. There’s no way I will forsake*Dupade*or take him out of my life, not now, nor in the future, never.

ayoreo: Uje yadode jecucha gajnárecho to diríca, mu Satanás u gu; mu yujnusina Dupade ga yicatecã́ri iji deréjnane jnese, ga Dupade doi yáyipie quigode. Nóna yu aja yajesúode, ga Satanás que pesupúsu cucha datérigo ome yu go.*No me ha engañado*– Dupade jécucha chajnésaru yu aja re uje chiraja uje que yicadígui.
english: I’m so grateful also that*Dupade*has given me wisdom for translating his Word – as if I had education enough for such a task! I know it’s only because of*Dupade*that I know how to do what I do; it is*Dupade*that gives me wisdom and inspiration. That’s how I can understand these things of*Dupade*that I must translate. I really thank him that the wisdom I have remains in me and never leaves me. I’m also thankful that he’s given me a love for*Dupade’s**Word*.

ayoreo:
Enga Satanás
iroi ga jĩrique chijócãre iji yajei. Dupade iroi Espíritu Santo jĩrique
chijócare Satanás irógode iji yajei. Ga jec u uje yáyipie jĩrique chachẽrapo
Dupade gari rĩ iji quigade, uje e Satanás íjnonguiji yajei.  Ga yirasique yu iji quigade uje cho jetiga ca
cucha gajnarétique dépoi yajei rĩ uje yiraja udé uje Espíritu Santo e chise yu
gu.
english: I did some really bad things in the past, but it was because Satan was at work in me; but when I remember*Dupade*, and pray every day,*Dupade*responds and does what I asked him to do. He goes with me everywhere, and now Satan cannot do the awful things he once did. He can’t deceive me.*Dupade*is always bringing me back to himself for he knows I have not forgotten Him.

ayoreo: Jecute que yáyipieraque jnopome cuchade udojo iji Dupade. Ujetiga cucha gajnarétique chosi ti yu, ati jec u uje yatecã́ri Dupade. Uje que toque jetiga yáyipieraque jnopome Dupade rĩ. Que toc jeta jé yumuchásapo yu rĩ aja Satanás adode. Yacaranguipis ome Dupade uje yimo Satanáse ipesutedatéode uje ueracho ga pipotiguei gajnesṍri Satanás u uje chingome yu.
english: And he casts out Satan’s demons from my heart.*Dupade*’s*Holy Spirit*is the one who casts Satan’s demons out of me. And then, my mind grabs hold of*Dupade*’s presence again since Satan is not inside me. Oh, how happy I am to have all the ugliness gone that was inside me, and I know it is because the Holy Spirit has found me.

ayoreo:
Chingome yu
iji uñane. Chojninga: “Apésu, apésu, ueracho, 
a ijõre ua jne,” jeoga, “a ataque ua jne.”
english: And now there’s no time when I’m not thinking about the things of*Dupade*. And when I’m tempted to sin, I quickly tell*Dupade*about it. That’s because my thoughts can never again go away from*Dupade*. It isn’t possible for me to turn back to doing what Satan wants me to do. I thank*Dupade*that I can recognize that the beautiful things Satan tempted me with came from him.

ayoreo:
Mu yujnusina
uje Satanás u ga yetaque. Yojninga ome cuchade uñane uje Satanás chiroque yu
aja uje chiese ataque yu: “Úngare, que ataque yu go. Que ataque yu go ome
Satanás iroquéode, cuchade iji jnumi tudé. Que yipésu Satanás adode go. Yiji
yigaidi Dupade Uruode.”
english: Sometimes Satan still shows them to me. He says: “Do it! Do it! It’s beautiful! You’ll be o.k.!” Or, he’ll say: “You’ll be rich!”

ayoreo:
“Que uerácho
go,” yojninga iji yajei, ga jec u uje Espíritu Santo chise yu ga chisipise yajei
iji ñane. Uje yetaque Satanás gueranone cucha ome yu.
english: But I recognize that it is Satan talking, and so, I refuse to do it. I say about those things Satan tries to get me to do to make me rich: “No, it won’t make me rich at all. Nothing Satan wants me to do in this world will make me rich. I won’t do what Satan wants me to do. I’ll keep seeking to know*Dupade’s Word*.”

ayoreo: Jecucha yujnusina yu uje disi yu nanique ga Dupade chirodipise yu. Disijate yu ga que yagu yibotique. Que yagu yibotique iji deréjnane, dirode jnese. Íjnoque yiguidedie. Íjnoque yibosode. Íjnoque yiparátadie gãratique. Mu yicatecã́ri Dupade iji yúringai disijat.
english: “Those things are not beautiful,” I tell myself, and then the Holy Spirit comes to me and sometimes I feel him inside me. That’s what happens when I refuse the things Satan wanted to give me.

ayoreo:
Yicatecã́ripisi
Dupade ga chisipise yajei. Ga jé u uje chátaja yu. Que gusu iji yúringai disi
go, jé aja udojo gajnerĩ, dirode udojo. Uje yisipise yajei ga que yanguretigai
que ajãpe go, cuchadaté iji yajei yanguretaretigai.
english: I’m always mindful of how it was when I was a little boy long ago and*Dupade*was watching over me. I was an orphan and had no food to eat. There were days when I didn’t have enough to eat. I didn’t have clothes. I didn’t have food. I didn’t have any money. But, I used to pray to*Dupade*in my state of being an orphan.

ayoreo: Ga jec u uje yatecã́ri Dupade ome cuchade uje sué yibai. Sué yibai ga que togome yu jeta yimate yu aja uechade cucha dacate gajnaréngone, Satanás ipesudode ome yu; mu yujnusina Dupade uje chi ca cucha uajátique déji ome ga yicatecã́ri ga yojninga: “Dupade, bamo yu uje ayoré gajnareque yu, ga que daijna yu gotique rĩ. Que yipesutique cuchadaté gotique to. Cucha bisidégape yu. Jé basa ga cuchadaté uhua uje bajnué cuchade gai jnese. Jecute yutṍri ua ome udojo cuchade uje que yijnime arínique Que cojñotique chijnime arínique rĩ iji erámi tudé. Enga ajócãre iji yajei.”
english: I prayed fervently to*Dupade*, and he came into my heart. And he helped me. Not only in my state of being an orphan, but also now. I examine my heart, and find that my faith is not little anymore, but much larger.

ayoreo: Yosi ti yicatecãraitigade ga jec u uje Espiritu Santo déji yajei. Ga yirasique yu iji quigade uje cho jetiga ca cucha gajnarétique dépoi yajei rĩ uje yiraja udé uje Espíritu Santo e chise yu gu. Jecute chijócãre Satanás iji yajei. Ga cho sere yáyipie to tome cucha dacate gajnaréngone udojo.
english: This is how I talk to*Dupade*about things that are too big for me. Some trials are so big that I can’t get through those ugly things that Satan brings to me. But I remember that*Dupade*said that nothing is too hard for him and then I talk to him and I say, “*Dupade*, you see that I am just a weak, common person and not in anyway a**daijnai*. I’ve never done anything powerful in my life. I’m not worth anything. But, on the other hand, you are greater that everything else. Therefore, I’m calling upon you about these things that*I can’t pull out the meat from*. There’s no-one in this world who can*pull the meat from them*. There is not a*cojñoi*in the world that can do it. So, take this out of my heart.

ayoreo: Yujnusinapo dirica rĩ uje – iji udé – uje yicatecãri diríca. Yojninga: “Eti bajéo, Dupade, mu yajéo gajnerĩ, que yajéo iji yajei tudé, yajei bisidei, yajei ayoré tudé.  Ba-espíritu Santo áyipie gajnerĩ iji yajei tudé. Babi áyipie gajnerĩ iji yajei ujetiga uacajéo ga yacoté ejnarei íjnongape gajnerĩ iji udé. Bamo uje yujnusipise yabode gu uje yicatecã́ri ua jeta átaja cucha udojo.”
english: This is how my prayers go, and then the Holy Spirit takes over my heart. And I’m so happy afterwards when I sense these ugly things are no longer in my heart now that the Holy Spirit has reached me. He has kicked Satan right out of my insides. And my mind is at peace and I’m free from those ugly temptations.

ayoreo: Que yicadígapoi Dupade Uruode quigode rĩ uje chi chacate dacadigode nanique ga chi chojninga: “Boyo ga beyoi cojñone iji erámi, ayoré ponáyone ore ga acacho ore.” Enga acadigode chi ore tangaripísi. Chi ore jno ga ore jnome erámi.  Chi chojningapome dacadigode rĩ – a chi udojo ore tangaripísi. Chi ore tangaripísi dacadisṍri acajnaquéode uje chi ore pota jeta ore tangaripísi gu.
english: I was praying yesterday when I remembered this, and I said, “If it’s your will,*Dupade*, for I don’t want my own will. My own human will is a*for-nothing-thing.*Your Holy Spirit’s will is inside me. May your Son’s will that is inside me take away my wife’s sickness, if it’s your will. You can see how much I miss my children as I talk to you and ask you to help them with things.

ayoreo:
Chi
chojninga: “Boyo gajnerĩ, acayoi erámi.” Ga chi ore jno, ga ore chacate
cojñone iji erámi. Cho ore quique ome cucha dacadode. Cho ore quique ujetiga ca
ore tangári cojñone ore guetetigade ome raque.
english: I’ll never forget*Dupade’s**Words*that he taught to his disciples and said to them: “Go and bring all the people, all kinds of tribal people and teach them.” And his disciples obeyed him. They took off and went out into the world. He also said something else to his disciples – and they listened to him. They really listened to their teacher’s lessons because they wanted to hear what he had to say.

ayoreo:
Ga chojninga:
“Boyo, acayoi erámi, acacho cojñone, ayoréode, menenéngone ore iji erámone
jnese jne. Ga gosi ujetiga cho doré iji yuruode iji udé jnumi – ujetiga chise
Yapade idai to iji guéode*enga po gosi oré jne*.”
english: He said: “Go right now, go into the world.” And they left, and they taught the people in the world. They explained things to them. They told them not to pay any attention to the people that hated them.

ayoreo:
Ga chi
áyipiedie u udaji ome dacadigode ore. Ga chi ore chisite rágaja erámi ome dacuegode
cojñónguiji erámi. Chi Espíritu Santo nóna ore iji erámi. Ga que toc jetiga qué
cuchárique cho na ore. Cojñone pojna ore. Ore chaquesu ore údode, mu que ore
timíninga ore go. Ore gaidode u dacadisṍri casé áyipiedie gu.
english: He said: “Go, go into the world, teach the*Cojñone, the Ayoréos, the Menenéngone,*all of them all over the world. And whoever considers my word to be their pay as they go, when they reach my Father’s city in heaven, they will be given a reward.”

ayoreo: Ga que jnoji yajei to udojo, uje ujetiga yacãco yógaja udore ore, jnacare bajade udojo gotigo ore, uje chi que ore timíninga cucha dacate gajnaréngone. Jec areque gajnerĩ ujetiga yacãco yógaja udore ore jetiga ca yimíngaco yocujode ayoré jetiga ore chetaque yoque gajnerĩ iji Dupade iguíjnane. Jeta ore chetaque yoque maringa, jeta ore cana yoquibai maringa, jé areque jeta ore cana yoquibai. Mu yicatecãcoi Dupade ome ore gajnerĩ. Uje cáma ore chiraja gu. Jecucha conferenciadie iji guidai, disiode ore cana iji conferenciadie. Mu cama ore chiraja Dupade gu. Jec arec jeta icatecãcoi Dupade ome ore to uje cáma ore chiraja cucha dacate omío.
english: And this was his plan for his disciples. And they scattered themselves about and went looking for the people in the world. He told them that the Holy Spirit would accompany them in the world. And that they would lack nothing. He told them the people would bawl them out. They would whip them, but to not pay attention to what they did to them. They must seek to do the will of their teacher, no matter what happened.

ayoreo: Piquetangore Dupade áyipiedie gu. Jé basa gajnaréngome jnanione uyoque uje yirajágo uje cucha dacadode doso ome ayoréode uyoque, doso pipojnaquéode, doso páyuguhebátigade, doso uje ayoréode ore pojna ñane, doso ome ayorérigo uyoque. Mu jetapuré Dupade uje paraque. A dosopise cucha dacadode, yocate gajnaréngone ome, uje que pota jóroga ujetiga yocatígo gajnarengui jorógome ñane. Ipotigádi u ujetiga yijnórango ñane iji jnumi jóroque. Mu Satanás u uje pésu ayoréode yocujuredie ome ñane iji jnumi tudé.
english: And none of this leaves my mind – how that we should follow the example of those first disciples who did not pay attention to the ugly things they suffered. So, let’s all follow their example, even when our fellow people hate us who go to church. Even though they hate us, and laugh at us, let’s just let them make fun of us. But, let’s pray for them. They don’t yet know what they are doing. We always have meetings in our village, and the children laugh during the meetings. But they don’t yet know*Dupade*, and that’s why they’re disrespectful. It’s good for us to pray for those who don’t yet know what appropriate behavior is.

ayoreo: Mu uduco Dupade Uruode rĩ uje cho yoquiquique, anguretasórone uyoque, ujetiga yidotágo yóguiji yocadode to uje omio. Yidotágo yóguiji yocanguretaretigai ome Dupade. Mu ujetiga yiraco udore ore uje que ore changureta cuchárique, ore áyipiedie gaidipise pisijnaringuei ome dojode ayoréo, ga yijoco ati ayoré cuchape yóguiji, jeta dacajneque yóguiji.
english: *Dupade*’s plans are always redemptive. But we humans don’t like it when things hurt us: scolding hurts, worries hurt, being bawled out hurts. We humans feel the things that happen to us. But if it hurts us, how much more does it hurt*Dupade*who is so good. He feels bad about our ugly ways, and though he doesn’t want us to do them, we do bad things to each other. He wants us to love each other here on earth, but we don’t obey him. But Satan is the one that makes our relationships with each other to be troubled.

ayoreo:
Mu que yajico gaté uje Dupade guetetigade u
ujetiga yoco eduguéjna iji yoque, yijoco yoque ome cuchade. Ga que yajico
yocujórique ayoré yui. Ga yoco ajningarane aja quedéjnane ga yijoco ati
yujnuéngo yocujode ayoré gai.
english: But, it’s only when we listen to the counsel of*Dupade*’s*Word*that we believers can continue doing the right things. We must be firm in our faith in*Dupade*. But when we start imitating those who have no faith, those who look for ways to influence other people, we mistakenly think we’ll be important if we give in to them.

ayoreo:
Mu que Dupade
áyipiedie quigode – que u go. Áyipie qui tu jetiga ca yocáyipidie jnoji iji
deréjnane. Ga yicoi áyipiedie quigode. Yocáyipiedie ca jnoji gajnerĩ iji
Dupade uje cuchadaté tu Dupade iji dirode udojo.
english: And we don’t consider the fact that*Dupade*doesn’t want us to consider ourselves above others in importance, and he doesn’t want us to refuse to help our fellow man. He doesn’t want us to be angry with others and think we’re better than they are.

ayoreo: Cucha dacadode jetiga Satanás atígo sué yoquibai gajnerĩ, yicoi ga yatecãcoi Dupade gajnerĩ. Yingongome gajnerĩ jeta yanguretágo ga Dupade chijócãrape gajnerĩ iji yocajéode. Ga que chisapo yoque rĩ, Satanás. Yirajago uje Satanás u uje pésu ayoréode yocujuredie ome cuchade. Cuchade jnese. Chingongara cuchade aja yoque iji jnumi tudé jeta ipótago ga ca yangácoi Dupade ome guerãtígo cucha.  Satanás ujopierãque to.  Satanás u, mu Dupade u uje ujopie sué gai. A jé areque gajnerĩ ujetiga yicoi iroquéode gajnerĩ. Jé ca atique gajnaréngome yoque iji deréjnane udojo, Dupade.
english: This isn’t*Dupade*’s will for us at all. His will is for us to never stop thinking about him. He wants us to do what he wills. Let’s not allow our minds to leave him, for*Dupade*is the important one these days.

ayoreo:
Yacaranguipise
uje Dupade chátapísaja yu iji erámone udojo. Que chicadígui yu. Que yicadígui
uruode rĩ uje chojninga: “A yijína ua aja erámi eruei. A bojórigo cho damajo
ua maringa, cojñone iji erámi ore cho damajo ua maringa, jé yugúsi bari. Jé
yugúsi bari iji deréjnane jnese.”
english: When Satan tries to get control of us, let’s start praying to*Dupade*! If we tell him that we believe in him,*Dupade*will cast Satan out of our lives. And Satan will have to leave us alone. We know that Satan is always bothering us in every way he can. He shows us earthly things so that we’ll desire them and forget what*Dupade*has given us. Satan is powerful also, but*Dupade*’s power is far greater than his. So, it’s a good thing to serve only*Dupade*. He’ll never do anything bad to us.

ayoreo: Jecute que toc jeta jé yicadigui. Yáyipie gaidode u unojo uje que yiramurase Dupade iji yajei. Yatecã́ri iji dejade jnese. Que yiramurase rĩ uje*yugúsiji*cojñone ujade, iji yujode ayoré ujade rĩ. Uje yajire cojñone iji erámi, yosõre ore to, mu ome Dupade Uruode mu que toc ujetiga yécãre ore atique.  Gusu Dupade Abi ujetiga chécãre ore áyipie quitique. Ore chisi re ome Jesucristo to ga churu ore ate gajnaréngone. Ga jec u ujetiga chojmára ore iji jnumi.
english: I’m so grateful for all the ways that*Dupade*has helped me here in this world. He has never forgotten me. And I have not forgotten his Word that says: “I will go with you till the end of the world. Your fellow men might persecute you, the people of the world do you wrong, but I will be with you always.”

ayoreo:
Gusu yuruode
iji dejai tudé mu ca yicadíjoi Dupade gajnerĩ ome ñane, ayoréode, cojñone
uyoque uje yanguretágo, ga yocáyipiedie jé jogádiji deréjnane jnese ome ñane
jetiga yicatecãcoi. E gusu yuruode.
english: There’s no way I can ever forget Him. My will is to never set*Dupade*aside. I want to pray to him every night. I won’t set him aside when I’m among the*Cojñone,*nor when I’m among the*Ayoreos*. When I’m with the*Cojñone*in the world, I’m sorry for them, but according to*Dupade*’s*Word*, it’s not possible for me to change them. Only*Dupade*’s Son can change the direction of their hearts. When they give themselves to Jesus Christ, then he washes away the ugly things they’ve done. And then, they are joined to Him, one with Him.

ayoreo: *Dijaide – Cochabamba, Bolivia – 1970**Marina de Morarie churúsãre Dijaide acajnaquéode*
english: That is all I have to say tonight, but let’s never forget*Dupade*when we are together, whether*Ayoreos or Cojñone*, all of us who believe in Him – let’s keep our minds upon Him all the time, remembering to pray. That is all I have to say.

ayoreo: Garósi to uje yajire Dupade Uruode to iji guedé jnoringai, mu yajirigode jé u Danielné (Uroi) ajirigode to iji durase. Ta jé chojmára yocajiritígo, mu cãra re, mu jé chojmára uje Dupade Uruode u.
english: *Garósi to uje yajire Dupade Uruode to*

ayoreo: Mu yoquirajatigade garósi ga jé tiógarapísaja yocacajnaquéode ome uaque uje Dupade Uruode qui u ujetiga garósi yoque marínga, Dupade jé déji yocari. Garósi yoquirajatigárigo marínga, yisígo yocujade ayoré iji, enga cho jétiga jiei to. Dupade que pota jetiga ca uruode déji iji deréjnane. Chisi ome yoquirajatiguei tome yoque gu uje yicoi Uruode to. Chisi ome yoquibijnani, yocatoi, yoquirajatigai, yocangoninguinone, yoquijmainie, yocuajatiguei, pininaquei; chisi ome cucha omone ome yoque gu jetiga yijnángo ome Uruode. Que chisi ome yoque bisideque go. Mu Satanas déji to uje chĩro udore iji yocujade.
english: I’ll read just a little of Dupade’s words, but it’s the same Scripture that Daniélné read this morning. We read the same passages from Dupade’s words but get different meanings from them.

ayoreo: Mu yocacaranguipise ome Dupade uje jé yijnángo Dupade ẽracáidode cucha ome yoque uje Dupade que chachía cuchade gotique iji dapesudi uyoque ayoré. Socaseti cho darúja yoque iji anire, uje Espíritu Sánto di.  Espíritu Sánto chi di jne enga chojninga: “Espíritu Sánto u ujeta chátaja uaque ga chisi ome uaque pirajatiguei, pirajatiguedatérique.”
english: Though our knowledge is little, it’s only right that we teach you because Dupade’s words say that even though we are few, Dupade is in our presence. Even though our understanding is limited, we give to our fellow people what we do understand, and the little becomes much. Dupade does not want us to ignore meetings where his words are taught, because when we get together to hear his words he gives wisdom to us. He gave us our lips, our heads, our understanding, our hearing, our hands, our strength, our joy. And he gave us these good things to use when we share his Word. He didn’t give them to us without a purpose. But Satan is also here among us to erase Dupade’s words.

ayoreo:
Jecute déji yoque gajneri,
Espíritu Sánto, uje chisi ome yoque ome yoquirajatiguei. Yirajágo cuchade jnese
ome yocatoi, yoquirajatiguei, uje Espíritu Sánto ẽracáidode cucha. Mu uñane
déji cojñone uje que ore chijna go. Dupade chisi ome ore bisideque ore
irajatiguei. Enga que cuchárique tṍrapísiji go. Ore chijna ome Satanas
atuatigade uje Dupade que pota go. Satanas que chijna Dupade ẽracáidode cucha,
mu jé basa ga yocuñane ga yijnángo Dupade ẽracáidode cucha.
english: But we thank Dupade that we are using Dupade’s gifts so that God won’t lose what he’s given to us humans that he created. Perhaps he will bless us now and cause his power, the Holy Spirit, to come upon us. He told us that his power would come, and he said: “The Holy Spirit is the one that will help you and give you knowledge and understanding.”

ayoreo: Dupade irasique udojo cuchade udojo uje que chachia. Ta irasiguipise yoc uje que chachia yoquirajatigai gotique. Dupade chojningame cojñone uje cho darúja ore: “A yisi ome pirajatiguedaté ome uaque jne.” Mu i tu ‘consiencia,’ uje Dupade chisi ome yoc. Mu jé unejaque rĩ. Mu Dupade ẽracáidi ‘*sabiduría*’ ome yoc ujetiga yirajágo  ga ‘*sábio*’ uyoque. Irajataque yoc. Ujetiga yipéco yocáyipierigui ome Dupade enga ‘*sábio’*uyoque. Ajéode u ujetiga ‘*conciencia*’ déji yoque jnese. Mu Dupade ẽracáidapo rĩ. Uacamóño cojñone to uje ore irajatigai sué yoquibai te mu, que ore sué yoquibai go. Déji yoque to jetiga yipéco.
english: Therefore, he is with us, the Holy Spirit, the one that gives us understanding. With our heads we know all things and have understanding because they are gifts of the Holy Spirit. However, there are people who have his gifts, but they do not use them. Dupade gave understanding to them in vain. And nothing comes from his gifts to them at all. They use their gifts to accomplish what Satan wants, things that Dupade does not want. Satan does not use Dupade’s gifts, but some of us do, and we use them for Dupade.

ayoreo:
Jeta yipótago ga Dupade chisi
ome yoc ‘conciencia.’ Jé unejaque ‘conciencia,’ jec unejaque ‘ciencia’ rĩ. ‘Ciencia”
u cojñone ipesutedatéode uje Dupade chisi ome ore. Dupade urugaite yoc ome
cuchade ujnojo uje chi ipotágo gu. Ujeta yipotágo ga yijnángo. Yocacaranguipise
ome Dupade gajneri uje yuruode garósi mu iji guedé jnoringa tudé mu jé irasique
re Dupade.
english: Dupade is happy when his gifts are not lost. He is very pleased with us when we use his gift of understanding, that he has not lost what he’s given us. Dupade has said to people when he blesses them: “I’m going to give you great understanding.” But it’s called*cognizance*and Dupade gives it to us. But there’s something different, but also a gift that Dupade gives us, that is called*wisdom,*so that we can be*wise*. When Dupade gives us wisdom then we are called*wise people*.*Knowledgeable people*. When we decide to follow God we are wise. Dupade wants all of us to have cognizance – that comes from him. You know that there are people smarter than we are, but they are not better than we are at all. There are things that we can do that they cannot.

ayoreo:
Irajágo uje irasique re uje
garósi yógome múñi, jé urúo uje chojninga: “A uñeque gasu iji damochátique,
enga dasoranéraque uñeque marínga, jé yátaja gosi. Jé yátaja jeta gosi tangári
yu.”
english: If we want him to, Dupade will give us cognizance or conscience. There is a difference between “conscience” and “science.” Science is the knowledge Dupade gives some people to do and discover great things. Dupade will bless us with these gifts if we want them. When we want them, then we will use them for Him. We thank Dupade this afternoon that despite my words being few he is pleased with them.

ayoreo:
Yocacaranguipis ome ujnojo uje
yirajatigai garósi mu que Dupade chachia yoquirajatigai gotique. Jé yisíngome
yocujode ayoré yoquirajatígabi enga Dupade irasique re enga chojninga:
“Barajatigai garósi marínga, jé angome bojode ayoréo uje uacanguretáyo.”
english: Even though there are few of us, we know that he is pleased with us. His word says: “Even though a person might be alone in bed, or alone on a trip, I will help that person. I will help him if he listens to me.”

ayoreo: Jé basa a gosi irajatigai jiei, mu que cuchárique tṍraji uje que chacate yoque gu. Que changureta Dupade ga cachi cuse. Gosi ta irajataque mu que cuchárique tṍraji. Irajatáguíji diríe uné mu, Dupade chajire quedéjnane ome. Dupade chetaque iji dajei uje que changureta gu.
english: We are very thankful for these things, that though my knowledge is little, Dupade has not lost that knowledge and understanding that he gave. The little knowledge that we have we give to our fellow-ayoréos and God is please with us. He said: “Even though your knowledge is small, you have shared what you believe with your fellow ayoréos.”

ayoreo:
Dupade chojninga: “Ca o ore
acadisõre cuchábi iji ua. Dupade cho puyáguiji enga puyac ujeta o oracadisõre
cuchábiji ua. Jé ca o eduguéjnai iji ua.” Uacamóño uje eduguéjnane to uje que
ore cho ore acadisõre cuchábi iji re. Ore chiraja uruode quigode gu. Que ore
cho eduguéjna iji re go. Ore chiraja uruode quigode uje puyaque ga que ore doi
ujnojo go. Enga jec arec jeta yicoi ujnojo to ujeta yanguretágo Dupade. Jé ca
yoco oracadisõre cuchábiji yoque. Jé ca yicoi eduguéjna iji yoc.
english: On the other hand, someone might have great knowledge, but it accomplishes nothing if he doesn’t use it to teach others. If he doesn’t believe in Dupade, his knowledge is also worthless. Someone might we intelligent, but nothing comes from it. Today, he might be acclaimed as intelligent, but Dupade looks away from him. Dupade has nothing to do with him because that person does not believe in him.

ayoreo:
Yocacaranguipis ome unojo Dupade
Uru garósiode. Jé gusu yatatagode, Dupade Urúo, ome yoque iji guedé jnoringai.
Mu que yiraja jeta uacuñeque déji duruñeque jeta gosi pota enga chope quique
Dupade Uru garósirigo to ome yoque.
english: Dupade’s words say: “Don’t think you are a great teacher. This is a wrong attitude, according to Dupade. Don’t lord it over people.” You know that when someone is a real leader, he doesn’t brag about being a great teacher. He knows what Dupade’s word’s say about wanting to be the big boss, and he understands that it is something we should not do. And as believers, we should obey Dupade. We should not want to be greater than others. We should be a teacher not a boss of those we teach.

ayoreo: *Dijaide – Tobité, Bolivia – 1970**Marina de Morarie churúsãre Dijaide uruode udore*
english: We are thankful for this little teaching from Dupade’s words. This is all I have to say to us this afternoon. But maybe someone else is here who would like to share a little of Dupade’s words with us, and if so, feel free to do so.

ayoreo:
**Ecarai catecã́ri Dupade:**Yoque Dupade, gusu ua uje baraja cuchade. Jecute yacaranguipis ome ua gajnerĩ ujetiga jé socaseti baruode jũrua iji yocajéode, yoque Dupade. Enga yejnángo yógaja baruode, yoque Dupade. Mu átaja yóguiji guidai, chequedíe, jnanione, uyoc, ujetiga yanguretágo, yoque Dupade. Enga orei yocanguretaretigai tiji yocajéode enga ca yucutágome ujetiga yicatecãcoi ua, yoque Dupade. Yoque Dupade, yicatecã́ri ua ome cuchade jnese uje yajo uje gusu ua uje posõraque ayoréode, cojñone yóguiji erámi ome betotiguei. Dupade, cuchiyi tu ua ujetiga basi ome betotiguei tome yoc. Dupade, átaja yoque gajnerĩ enga yuchẽco yocáyipiedie angoninguinone gáyode ome baruode iji gajnerĩ, yoque Dupade. Yirajágo uje baruode chojninga: “Gosi ujetiga cutame yuruode, jeta cutame yu iji ujade a yipogáji gosi te jne,” baruode chojninga.*(Apocalípsis 3:16)*

Jecute yicatecã́ri ua ujetiga apésu yoque iji ne gajnerĩ jne enga yoquicatecarãitigade unéraja bajei ujetiga ca cucha gajnarétique déji. Jé basa baruode chojninga: “Ujetiga gosi chimesa cuchade aja ñane enga a yipogai jne jetiga chimesa cuchade.” Jecute yicatecã́ri ua ome cuchade jnese ome bajnacari Jesus i.  Amẽ.

**Ecarai chacate yogome Pablo icatecãraitigade ome Dupade:**Que yajire Dupade uruode gotique ujetiga yingo uaque, mu yajiri u Dupade ojningane cucha. Ojningane u uje Dupade anguresṍri jnani bajai San Pablo u uje chojninga uje chi catecã́ri Dupade ome re ujetiga Dupade chiqueta ejnaréngone. Mu chi catecã́ri iji dirode ga gadióguiji deréjnai údode.

Mu diga Dupade chudute icatecarãitigade. Chajnai uruode, enga chojninga: “¿Gotique gu a uje jecucha bacatecã́ri yu?” chojninga. Cachi que Dupade chisi ome etotiguei ome gotique.

Ujetiga uaque gosi chejnáreque re jne, jeta yocuñeque u uje chejnáreque re, mu Dupade ajegaite gosi, yocuñeque, ejnaréngone gu ujetiga gaidi tu ujetiga ca chajirápo erámi jogonórone cucha iji erámi uné.

Jecute San Pablo chojninga: “Yoque Dupade, ¿gotique que bei yicatecarãitigade ome ua uje yicatecã́ri ua gatuac jetiga aqueta yu?”

Mu chojninga: “¿Gotique gu a uje cachi ca yijnoraquei cucha gose ome ua?” chojninga.

Uacamóño uje cojñone chujérase Pablo, mu Dupade chorei quedéjnane ome ore, Pablo. Mu chiraja unojo enga chojninga: “Jec ũré. Ise ga Dupade chiqueta yu jeta chãraque yu ome cojñone. ¿A yiyárase Dupade enga yicarási quedéjnai ome ore?”  chojninga.

Mu jé ũrepise Dupade uje a ayorérique ijõre aja Dupade uruode iji danguretaretigai. Mu iji dáringai ayoré ijõre enga jé cha dáringai ijõre ome Dupade uruode. Que chise Dupade uneratiguérique go.

Jecute aniri, San Pablo, que changureta Dupade to gotique iji taningane. Enga a chirase re; a chijnóra dáringai ayoré ijõre nanique. Ujétiga changureta Dupade iji dáringai ayoré ijõre enga jé Dupade chigarúrasu iji.

Jé uacamóño uje Dupade chetaque Pablo isijnaringuéone uje doi Dupade anguresórone, ga chejna Pablo edodie. Gaidi tu jeta changureta Dupade. Mu quigade enga jé etoque mu Dupade guetesórone ore chaquesu údode enga chejnáreque ore aquesupídode urõsoringuéone uje chiraja uje que Dupade gotique. Mu chojninga: “Ise ga, jec ũré,” chojninga, “uje a ijõre yu, enga a yirasirase yijóningane ome Dupade. A yirasigode urásu erámi jogonórone cucha, ga e yiya Dupade enga yicai quedéjnane ome,” chojninga.

Mu Dupade uruode que uruode qui u gotique ome yoc ujetiga ijõre yoque. Ujetiga ayoré ijõre ua enga angureta Dupade iji báringai ayoré ijõre, enga jé ca etaque ua ome Dupade. Mu jé ũrepise Dupade uruode unojo ome yoc uje a yocuñeque a yirase yúringárique ijõre enga a jé yiya Dupade. Yicai quedéjnane ome.

Mu Dupade uruode que qui u go. Dupade uruode qui u uje chojninga: “Ujetiga bajogate báringai ayoré ijõre enga a jé ca ajé Dupade uruode iji báringai ayoré ijõre,” chojninga. Enga a ejnaretaque ua iji Dupade uruode gánique enga jé ca etaque.

**Ecarai chacãra dacajnaquéode aja Ezequías icatecãraitigade ome Dupade:***(2 Reyes 20:1-11)*Ocoi Dupade anguresórone unojo ore uje ejnaretaque ore. Eduguéjnane uñai u uje chojninga: “Dupade á, que yicatecã́ri ua gotique jeta aqueta yu, yicatecã́ri ua gu ujetiga yitoi gajnerĩ,” chojninga. “Ujetiga yise bedai te ga, que u gotique ujetiga yitoi gajnerĩ iji jnumi udé. Ajnusina gu uje,” chojninga, “uje que yãraque yejórique ome yurasade ore nanique. Ajnusina yu uje jecucha yisi yurasade ore iji yibose gajnaréngone,” chojninga. “Que yicatecã́ri ua ujetiga aqueta yu,” chojninga.

Mu cho dáyugu re. Doi daguíjnai ajidé ga moji. Chujé raja daguíjnai ajidé. E chapu re gu. Mu Dupade a gaidi tu, ome yajéode, ujeta ca chirase rome Dupade rĩ. Chi doi ga chuningame eti Dupade uruode chise enga chojninga: “Bacatecarãitigade chise yu iji gatajnoquei,” chojninga, “mu a ca yirógape pitoringai taja ua gotique,” chojninga, “a qué yorepe sequeredie tiji bei te enga jetiga sequeredie chejna yirídai tiji bei te jne,” chi chojninga.

**Dupade tangári yoquicatecãraitigade ome re mu pésu dájegaidode ome yoque:**Enga jé ũrepise uje a yocuñeque potapísa cucháriguiji Dupade. Dupade ca ajéode u, ga ca Dupade chisi ome yoque go. Que chojninga gotique: “Dupade, aqueta yu. Yi baroquéode jne. Dupade á, ãro yejnaréngone iji yúdode. Enga yi baroquéode.” Que uruode u gotique. Chojninga: “Yoque Dupade, yitoi gajnerĩ,” chojningame, “ujetiga jec ũré uje yangureta ua,” chojninga, “ajnusina yu gajnerĩ jeta ibágui jnaropie,” chojninga.

Mu Dupade chosõre icatecarãitigade ome, enga doi ga qué chécãrapo sequeredie iji yui te. Sequeredie chi a gagájni iji ayoréode yúode. Mu qué chorepe sequeredie iji yui tenga que úringai jnacare chirajápo gatajnoquei te rĩ enga que gaidi tu ujeta Dupade qué chĩro ejnaréngone.

**Ecarai chacãra dacajnaquéode aja Maria, Moises urẽja:***(Número 12:1-4)*Moises nero chojninga: “Achopise uje yocurasade ore tangárosi yajábi uhua,” chi chojningame Moises. “Iyajmaque ga yingo yurasade enga que yangácopoi ua jne rĩ,” chojninga.

Mu jec ũré ujetiga Dupade anguresóri déji ujetiga ore chacho. Ujetiga ore nína jnumi jeta ore chetaque yocuñeque, Dupade anguresónique, Dupade uruode jnungónique, ga a ore chacho acajnaquéode. Ore chacho uje ore urasade ore tangári. Mu jé socaseti Dupade chimo goto etétigade, jeoga gosi etétigade.

Jecute Moises nero chojninga: “Yachopis uje yurasade ore doi Moises iroquéode,” chojninga. Mu Dupade chimo, chudute uruode ome uraque dajai enga doi enga chumute pugorodie iji gai.

Dajai doi ga chichai guesi tiji uraque darengúone. Gusu iji ijnangununie.

Ga jnusina uje chi chojninga: “Yuninángorone
a e ipesute yejnaréngone,” chojninga. Yuninángorone doi ga tṍra yu aja guesi
tiji ore iguíjnane. Chi chajuque ijnangununie aja anírone, dachojmaringane.

Mu ujetiga jec ũré jeta yanguretágo Dupade ga ca acho Dupade ujetiga acatecã́ri. Jé ca barúrique chamáña aja Dupade. Dupade uruode chojninga: “Jé ca urase jeta o Dupade iji ua,” chojninga.

Jecute una cho darúja dajai te, mu Dupade cho darúja uaté. Doi datéringane uje gusu ijnangununie ome mochádode.

Enga yoque gosi ta adi tu dirome ujetiga gosi cho jmiti-jmiti ome Dupade, enga ga gosi chajuque Satanas uniri jogonórone cucha anírone, dequejnarécho.

Ujetiga angureta Dupade enga,
yirajágo uje yanguretágo Dupade. Mu Dupade uruode qui u uje angureta enga jé ca
bei ga adojnóngápo Dupade quigánique rĩ.

Jecute Dupade uruode u uje chojninga: “Gosi ujetiga dojnóngaja yi te ga dojnóngaja yu ome cuchade yui te ga, uté gosi tu uje chejnáreque re jne,” chojninga.Jecute yingo uac unojo uje jé uacamóño uje e gare uje yingome uaque, jnanione uje ore chejnáreque re, ome Dupade Uruode.

**¿Gotique gu a uje Dupade que chisiome yocutaidode cucha ome yoque?**San Pablo u uñai. Uqué eduguéjnai upo uñai rĩ, uje chejnáreque re ga catecã́ri Dupade mu que Dupade chetaque atique enga Dupade qué chorepo sequeredie gaté iji yui rĩ.

Jé basa uyoque ga a yocuñeque
catecã́ri Dupade gajnerĩ ome dejnarétigo. Catecã́ri ome dapotigátique cucha
mu Dupade que ajéo ga que chisi ome gosi go. Mu gosi e dojnóngaja Dupade uruode
ga Dupade chojninga: “Yirajade u uje qué yirodirase,” chojninga.

Dupade chi uqué icatecarãitigade gajnui tu quesei tome Dupade. Uje uacamóño uje qué yocuñeque catecã́ri ome cucha ueradéjnai jeta Dupade chisi ome yoque mu Dupade chojninga: “Úngare, ise ga yisi ome ua enga aníri, a arasirase ua aja guesi gaté rĩ,” chojninga.

Ga Dupade gajnúi tu jnanione uje disiode chijna queséode. Ore datedie charángome ore uje que ore chiraja dasocáringome. Ise ga ca déji siñeque chequéraque jeta ca chaquĩ́ra datígome quesei docoritique.

Ayoré chijna quesei ga goto chojninga: “A qué chaquesu ua gajnerĩ.”

Mu abi que tangári enga chaquesu dajmanéro. Chaquesu datique ga daté chajotase jnumone. Chojninga: “Bamo uje yingo ua diríca. Que adute yuruode.”

Enga Dupade a uruode jé u to.
Dupade a chisi ome gosi ipotigátique cucha ome gosi jeta gosi ipotigátique
cucha ueradátique enga cha Dupade enga, Dupade chojninga: “Yiraja casode.”*(Salmo 106:15)*

Jé uacamóño San Pablo. San Pablo a Dupade chisírasome etotiguei tome enga a charásu Dupade enga chojningarása: “Yiraja casodica uje cha yu,” chojninga.*(2 Corintios 12:7-9)*

Jecute yingo yoc ujetiga yujnusinángo uje Dupade que jĩrique chisi ome yoquipotéode cucha ome yoque gotique. Que jĩrique chisi ome yoquipotéode cucha ome yoque uje qué chinóninga yoc ujetiga jec ũré enga yanguretágo. A gosi, yocuñeque, catecã́ri Dupade ujetiga chojninga: “Asi ome yiguidérigui ome yu ga ueradéjnai. Jé asi ome parátadie ome yu ga ocoi.”

Mu Dupade timíninga icatecarãitigade aja ajidedie. Ujetiga jé ũré gosi ome jeta doi Dupade ojningane cucha, ga Dupade chisi ome gosi ipotigádode cucha ome. Jé basa gosi te enga a gosi todo aníningo, daguidé áimeringui ome Dupade uruode ga que chutai Dupade iji cuchárique. Que chutai iparáta. Que yutai jeta chisi yibotígo jeta chisi ome yiguidedie ome yu, mu Dupade chimo uje udé chijna iyoite ga, jé a chojninga: “A yisi ome jne,” chojninga.

Jé basa gosi a doi Dupade iroquéode mu que gaidi u ujetiga doi Dupade iroquéode, mu gaidi u ujetiga Dupade chisi dagúcharigo ome. Enga Dupade chojninga: “Ise ga yisi ome ipotiguéode cucha enga a jnorase dádi,” chojninga. “Ise ga yisi ome gaidode cucha enga chãra re ome yu,” chojninga.

Jé uacamóño uje ocoi Dupade
anguresórone iji erámi iji gajnerĩ, diri unojo, uné ore cho jmiti-jmiti ome
Dupade uruode. Ore chacho uje Dupade, ore catecã́ri Dupade ome ore ipotéode
cucha mu Dupade que chisi ome ore. Mu Dupade chiraja uje cachi ore icatecãraitíga,
cachi ore utaquéo. Enga que chisi ome ore go.*(Santiago 4:3)*

Jé uacamóño uje ore chojninga: “A déji toniñeque ome Dupade.” Mu Dupade que chicadígui go. Ocoi Dupade anguresórone uje ore cha Dupade, mu Dupade que cha ore go. Ocoi to uje ore cho dabotígaja Dupade uruode enga ore tagu, enga ore chajo raja uje ore chajire Dupade uruode. Jé ũrepise ore anguretaretigai te gu.

Jé uacamóño uje anire Dupade iminingate danguresórone aja erámone. Timíninga danguresórone aja jnumi gu ujetiga chajire ejode. Jecute yingome yoc ujetiga anóningacho to ujetiga apota cucháriguiji Dupade enga acatecã́ri bayé Dupade. Enga aya bojningane uje bojninga: “Dupade taque. Taque uté ga que chisi ome yipotigádode ome yu.”

Mu jé ca aya bangome ua ome Dupade. Jé chajire jeta jec ũré ua aja bapotéode cucha ome Dupade iroquéjnai. Yadi u nanique uje yipota jeta yi Dupade iroquéode, mu chise gójni diri uje yiji Dupade uruode.

Enga Dupade uruode qui u uje chojninga: “Gosi
ujeta cutame yuruode, uté anguretaretigai areredaté iji ajei tenga e chósi ti ome
yuruode,” chojninga.

**Ecarai cho dojñame jnanírique uje choru cucarani enga chijna Dupade uruode iji dajmáinie:**Mu jé uacamóño uje ugúcoi jnumi, Satanas uniri te ga, ueradi tu ujetiga Dupade anguresórone ore nóna ñane aja quedéjnane. Déji erámi uje Dupade anguresórone ore cṍra. Mu ore chaguepo rĩ uje ore jnusina danguretaretigai iji Dupade.

Jecucha yingome uac uje cojñoi
jnome gatajnoquei uje Dupade anguresórone ore cho quic uje ore chojninga: “Ese
cucaranone udore enga ese guesi enga ca amóji gajnerĩ,” chi ore chingo. Mu
chise gójni ta enga íjnongosi ga etarudabode íjnongosi ga doi ga moji ta. Doi
ga noñome Dupade uru casodica iji jnumi.

Mu Dupade uruode qui que u gotique jeta yocuñeque mo ga noñome Dupade uruode iji daquigade. Gosode que chajnésare daquigánique.

Chi Dupade anguresṍri tuqué
jno enga chise ore gaminone. Jé jnusina uje ore gaminone u unojo. Moi ga noñome
Dupade uruode iji jnumi. Chise toniñeque enga chojninga: “A yi ome Dupade
uruode iji durasé.”

Que chajniapo gai te rĩ. Mu Dupade uruode que qui u gotique jeta yijíñangome Dupade uruode enga diyajmaque jeta yajni to gai iji toniñeque rĩ.

**Ecarai chacate yogome Satanas etingaquéode quigode:**Chise gójni ore gaminone ga chojninga: “Ore gaminone u unojo uje yujé caratai tiji diríca.” Todo caratedie ga chajniapo aja ore quigade rĩ. Mu Dupade uruode que qui u go ujetiga yitodógo caratedie.

Caratedie u Satanas anirone etingaquéode ujetiga Satanas chetingare gosi te ga chicha caraté taja gosi iquei. “Caratedie chãraque Dupade idai yui. Que yicai to jne,” chojninga. Mu gosi que timíninga caratedie ome ore patarane ga gosi chajo raja uéchade enga chise gójni uechamitodie. Enga unojo u uje irajágo uje Satanas etingaquéode porójna iji jnumi.

Enga yocuñeque a timíninga unojo Satanas etingaquéode unojo. Uaque gosi e chicadígui Dupade uru casodicai iji cucarani ójodie.

Jeta angureta Dupade enga
etingare Dupade uruode to. Jé socaseti yiquérango iji jnenga yingongo Dupade
uruode aja ñane iji de jnumi iji yoquidai.

Mu yacaranguipis ome Dupade uje Dupade uruode iji darujna aode ojningane cucha déji jnese. Yimeserane cucha déji jnese rĩ. Ujeta uaque gosi chetaque datígome Dupade enga angári Dupade uruode iji darujna arone ga jiei enga a ese jne Dupade guetétigade iji. A ese Dupade imeserane cucha jne. Enga a ango bayé Dupade enga Dupade chĩro basijnaringuéone. Dupade uruode chojninga: “Gosi ujetiga pocaningane, cojñone, ayoréode ore yuguébeque, mu que yi ga yojninga: ‘Acai uaiti. Yetaque ua.’*(Juan 6:37)*

Dupade chi jé chĩro ayoré jocaratade ore isijnaringuéone. E chĩro pitoringai tiji dei te rĩ. Jecute yirajágo uje Dupade uruode udojo ga que cuchárique sue gai go. Jé uacamóño uje chi Satanas chingo raja Jesuse gai uje toi iji aniri, uje ore chijóchame. Mu que jnusina gotique uje Jesus chojninga: “Yitoi to marínga, a díningo gadióguiji yúode ujetiga yiquecapo jne rĩ,”  chojninga.

Ga unojo u uje yirajágo uje Dupade jé sue cuchade unojo gáyode. Jé sue basijnaringuéode gáyode. Jé sue erámi jogonórone cucha, Satanas uniri jogonórone cucha gáyode. Jé ca ojninga ujetiga apésu cucha gajnáreque datérique, mu jé ca ojninga, “Ajengopísome Dupade. ¡A jé ca Dupade chĩrorase yisijnaringuéone jõ!”

Uje jec uje Dupade chimo Satanas iji ujnacari itoringai iji ujnacari ode enga ecapo rĩ. Chi Jesuse uté jnome guesi enga jé chicai jnaropie ujetiga qué chingo Dupade uruode aja ore iji uté rĩ. Chise jnaropié te uje chicai yoque. Ga chi guejnanie dire guejnanie ayoré gare iji jne uje Dupade chimo ore uyoque iji erámi uné. Ga unojo u uje yirajágo uje Dupade sue cuchade jnese gáyode jnese. Ca ojningame bapesutique cucha daté: “Dupade, ise ga ca ãro jóroque uqué enga que yingo ua.”

Quene yĩri iji ayoréode, uje ayoréode marínga, ore ájnigai, ore ajningaméone e déji ore ajei te ga, que ore doso yu ome anire gotique, cojñoque idaidatei, pocaningárique cucha. Ore ajéode u jeta ajningamei e sue dagaitique cucha gai.

Ga cuchiyi tupúsu Dupade uje etingaquéode, uje chetingare re ome ica enga ocoi. Jé uacamóño uje Dupade anguresórone urasade chi ore déji yote quedéjnane. Chajni to uñéngo. Jé ore chajuque gu diri eruei iji. Ore doi pocaningane enga que ore umajósorone sue ore gánique gotique. Ore chajuque arenie mu que ore cajnóriji gotique. Ore chajuque anírone erãpe tarégode, mu que ore cajnóriji gotique uje Dupade jé sue cuchade unojo jnese.

A yiguíjna cutérone yúode ome ore yui, enga Dupade jé chamorase cutérone ore gáyode. Yiguíjna dosipéode ome ore yúode enga Dupade jé chore dosipéode iji ore. Enga yirajágo uje Dupade jé sue cuchade gáyode ome ore jnese. Sue pocaningane gáningo. Sue pisijnaringuéone, poriquéode ore.Jé uacamóño uje chi Dupade jé chi chetágapo uje chi ipesute cucha datéode rĩ, mu Dupade doi ga chiqueta ga jé chichapo iji diri rĩ.

**Ecarai chacãra dacajnaquéode aja cheque uje i tu Dorcas:**Chi chequéraque déji uje i tu, mu que yiraja i. Cojñone uruode qui u ayoréode yocuruode qui uje ore chojninga Erámoro (Dorcas). Chi doi ga urasade ore jnusi uje toi. Ore jnusi ate goningane. Ore jnusi picaniratiguéode. Mu Dupade doi dajopié ga chutõri darógode uñai tome enga ecapo rĩ, enga yirajágo uje Dupade sue cuchade enga sue pitoringai gai. (Hechos 9:36)

**Ecarai chatata jnacari adode ome yoque uje i tu Eútico:**Chi jnacanique basíji guiguíjnai porójo iji toniñeque, mu Dupade chiquetápo ome dajopié te ga, jé u uje ecapo rĩ.*(Hechos 20:6-12)*

Enga unojo u uje yirajágo uje
Dupade jé sue cuchade gáyode jnese. Gosi ujetiga ca pota Dupade chĩro gosi
isijnaringuéone, jé ore uru patáringane enga gosi chojninga: “Dupade cachi
urúo. Jecute que chĩro yisijnaringuéone go.”

Mu gosi tu uje que cha dasijnaringuéone ga uté gosi jé chajo raja dasijnaringuéone, dangoneparitigade ome Dupade, enga Dupade que pésu cucháringome gotique.

Dupade uruode u uje Jesus chojninga: “Gosi ga tibágui yu enga ca yojninga: ‘Acai uaiti jne. Que yipota uaque gotique. Sijnaque uaque gu.’” Dupade que chojninga go.

Dupade uruode chojninga: “Piotiguei tu uje gosi chingo dasijnaringuéone aja yu,” chojninga. Uje yingome uaque uje Dupade uruode qui u ujetiga yátago Dupade Uruode aja ñane. Ua ujeta etoque ua iji Dupade uruode enga ango ua aja Dupade anguresórone jeta ore chátaja ua. Ore chijnapo ua aja Dupade rĩ.

Gusu unojo uje yo quique Dupade
ojningane ome yoque iji durasei, mu yirajágo uje Dupade uruode que íjnoque
gotique jeta gosi pota Dupade uruode. Enga gosi pota chĩro dadode jeta ate
gajnarétigo ome yoc, déji jetiga Dupade chátaja yoc.

Qué déji San Pablo pijnani uje
chojningame Coríntiogosode: “Uacarasade gotígo ore cha dasijnaringuéone jnenga.
Que yugúsi uacari to mu e yĩro ore isijnaringuéone ome Dupade ujopié.” E gusu.

*Ecarai – Tobité, Bolivia – 1960**Marina de Morarie churúsãre Ecarai uruode*
english:
**I want your Word to be hot inside me**

*Yigaidi tu jeta baruode jũrua iji yajei Ecarai*

**Ecarai prays:**

*Yoqui-Dupade*, you are the only one that knows everything. Therefore, I’m grateful to you today that you can make your word to be*hot*inside us. And then, we will give ourselves completely to you,*Yoqui-Dupade*. But help us here in our village, the women, the men, all of us, to have faith in you,*Yoqui-**Dupade*. Increase the faith inside us so that we might not be lazy in our prays.*Yoqui-Dupade*, I pray about everything – about the food we eat because of your generosity to*Ayoreos**, Cojñone*all of us everywhere on earth where you show yourself strong on our behalf.*Dupade*, you are the only One that gives your strength to us.*Dupade*, help us to open the ears of our hearts to your Word. We know that your Word says: “If a person is lazy about studying my Word, about serving me, I will spit him out.”*(**Revelation**3:16)*

That’s why I am asking you to work in our hearts right now so that when we talk to you, our prayers will be refreshing to you with nothing ugly in them. However, your Word also says, “If a person mixes other things into his prayers, I will vomit them up.”

Therefore, I ask all these things in the name of your Son Jesus. Amen.

**Paul****’s****asks****Dupade****to heal him****:**

I don’t just study*Dupade*’s*Word**s*to tell you about them, but I study them to know what*Dupade*says. And what I’ve learned about God’s believer Paul, who lived long ago, is that he prayed for*Dupade*to heal him. And he talked to*Dupade*for three whole days.

But it happened that*Dupade*heard his prayers. He said: “Why do you keep on talking to me about this?” It’s just that*Dupade*wasn’t going to give him strength.

If one of you is suffering, if any of us is suffering, but that person has been chosen by*Dupade**,*whoever that person might be*,*he might be suffering because*Dupade*is allowing it so that he won’t look back at the things of this world.

Therefore, Paul asked: “*Yoqui-**Dupade*, why don’t you answer my prayers, for I’m continually asking you to heal me?”

But*Dupade*said, “Why is it that just my love is not enough for you?”

You know that the people almost killed Paul, but*Dupade*removed Paul from them. When Paul remember this he said: “That’s right. I think it was*Dupade*who saved me so that he could protect me from the*Cojñone*. How could I stop following*Dupade?*And how could I have rescued myself from them on my own?”

*Dupade*is faithful, and he wants a man to be healthy spiritually by reading his Word and believing it. But sometimes when a man is healthy physically, he stops being concerned about his spiritual health and stops reading*Dupade Uruode.*Then he doesn’t find the beauty of*Dupade.*

That’s how it was with Paul, he didn’t believe in*Dupade*in the beginning. He was proud; and loved being a healthy person. If he could trust*Dupade*while being a healthy person, then*Dupade*would have kept him in good health.

Now you know that*Dupade*hated Paul’s sin of going after Christians to kill them, so he took away his sight. He did that so that he would believe in*Dupade*. Afterwards, however, Paul was strong again, but those that hated*Dupade*began to persecute Paul – they whipped him so harshly that Paul suffered great pain, but he knew that it wasn’t*Dupade*doing it, it was men. He said: “I think it’s true that when I am healthy, I might be happier about my health than about*Dupade.*I might be happier about earthly things, and might stop following*Dupade,*and go away from him,” this is what Paul said.

But*Dupade*’s*Word*doesn’t say too much about our being healthy physically. If you are healthy, continue to trust in*Dupade*, and don’t refuse yourself to*Dupade**,*but serve him. But*Dupade*’s*Word**s*are very true that say to us: one of us might be happy about his health and then stop following*Dupade*and turn away from him.

But*Dupade*’s*Word*doesn’t teach us to be that way.*Dupade’s Word*says this: “If you are healthy, don’t let being healthy kill your need for*Dupade Uruode*. And if you get sick while obeying and serving God, don’t worry about being sick, God will take care of you.”

**Ecarai uses****Hezekiah****’ prayer****to illustrate his teaching.****(2 Kings 20:1-11)**

There are many who believe in*Dupade*that are sick. One of the kings of Israel said: “*Dupade*, I’m not asking you to heal me, but I’m asking to die right now,” he said, “that I might reach your city. Please remember,” he said, “that I didn’t keep my people away from me. Remember how I always shared whatever food I had with them,” he said. “I’m not asking you to heal me, I only ask you to remember the things I did for others.”

He felt sorry for himself. He went to a corner of his house and went to sleep. He put himself into the corner of his house. He lost all hope of recovering. But I think what*Dupade*wanted was for him to tell*Dupade*he wasn’t happy. But when he least expected it,*Dupade**’s*words reached him and said: “Your prayers have reached me in heaven,” he said, “but I’m not going to send death to you at all,” he said, “I’m going to extend years to your life, and the years will*finish off my foot,*” he said.

**Dupade****hears our prayers, but answers them according to his will****,****not ours****:**

And it’s true that we might greatly desire that*Dupade*give us something. If it’s not*Dupade*’s will, however, he doesn’t give it to us. Someone might say: “*Dupade*, heal me and I’ll serve you.*Dupade,*erase the sickness from my body and I’ll serve you.” That might not be what he says at all. He might say, “*Y**oqui-**Dupade*, I’m dying,” he says, “if I truly believe in you,” he says, “then remember me when I go to*jnaropié,*the place of the dead.”

But*Dupade*was moved by Hezekiah’s prayers to him and changed the amount of years he would live. It might be four years more for some people. But he increases the years and the person won’t go to heaven as a young man, even thought he hasn’t asked*Dupade*to heal him.

**Ecarai****uses what happened to Moses’ sister Miriam to illustrate his teaching:****(Numbers 12:1-14)**

Moses’ sister said: “I’m so tired of our people listening only to what you have to say, little brother,” she said. “For a long time, I’ve been telling my people that we shouldn’t listen to you any more.”

And to tell the truth, there are still*Dupade’s*believers that people are fed up with. They seem to like hating us who believe in*Dupade*and who take*Dupade*’s*W**ord**s*to other places. They tire of our teaching. And they’re tired of their fellow believers who listen to us. But*Dupade*is aware of the women who hate us, and the men who are against us.

Therefore, Moses’ sister said: “I’m so fed up with my countrymen doing everything Moses tells them to do.” But*Dupade*saw her and heard her words against her brother and he sent a skin disease upon her and she was covered with sores.

Her brother made her leave the camp. And she was alone in the darkness.

And she remembered, and she said, “It is what I did to startle my brother that has caused this sickness. Because of my harsh behavior, he threw me out of the camp where people dwell.” She crossed through the darkness in her aloneness.

If you truly believe in*Dupade*don’t grow weary of talking to him. Don’t use ugly words about*Dupade*.*Dupade’s**Word*says: “Don’t ever consider yourself to be*Dupade.”*

When his sister cursed her brother,*Dupade*cursed her. But worse, he sent her into the darkness all alone to sleep.

And it could happen to any one of us in the future if that person abandons*Dupade*, he might find himself walking back and forth, facing the things that are in Satan’s land, sick and suffering.

If we believe in*Dupade*, we will know that we believe in*Dupade*. But*Dupade*’s*Word*explains that if you believe in him, don’t show contempt for*Dupade*behind his back.

Therefore,*Dupade*’s*Word*warns us: “Whoever makes fun of or dishonors my name or belittles what I am going to do in the future, that person will suffer for it.”

That is why I say these things to you, and I’ve told you about two who suffered according to*Dupade’s Word.*

**Why doesn’t****Dupade****give us what we ask****for?**

Paul is a good example of this. Another good example is the king that was suffering, and he talked to*Dupade,*and*Dupade*didn’t disapprove of what he’d done, and he extended additional years to his life.

And then there’s us: one of us might talk to*Dupade*about his sicknesses. He might tell*Dupade*what he wants, but if it’s not*Dupade*’s will, he won’t give it to that person. But then, that person speaks badly about*Dupade**Words**,*and*Dupade*says: “I knew what would happen, I was just waiting for that,” God would say.

*Dupade*says, a person’s prayers could be likened to a sharp knife. For you know that if someone prays for*Dupade*to give some beautiful thing to him,*Dupade*might say, “No, I think if I give it to you, you’ll be too pleased with it,” he will say.

And*Dupade*is like a man whose children have knives. Their mothers don’t want them to have knives, because they don’t yet know how to use them. I don’t believe there is a mother anywhere that hasn’t warned her child about sneaking a knife!

A person picks up a knife and a woman might say, “It’s going to cut you!”

And a child that won’t listen to his mother ends up cutting his arm or another part of his body! And when that happens, his mother scolds him and says: “You know I warned you about that yesterday, but you never listen to me!”

And that is what*Dupade*will say. If*Dupade*grants a person’s prayer and gives him the pretty little thing he wants, and then the person stops following Him,*Dupade*will say, “I knew you would do that.”*(Psalm 106:15)*

You know how it was with Paul. If*Dupade*had given him strength and he would have stopped serving*Dupade*then*Dupade*would have said to him, “I already knew that he would stop following me.*(2 Corinthians 12:7-9)*

Therefore, I’m telling us to remember that*Dupade*isn’t quick to give us all the things we want. First, he wants to see if we really believe in him. Someone among us might pray and ask*Dupade**:*“Give me some really beautiful clothes, and lots of money.”

But*Dupade*would examine carefully*all the corners*of that prayer. If*Dupade*sees that the person making the request is sincere, and that it will help him to do what*Dupade*wants him to do, then*Dupade*will gladly give it to him. On the other hand, someone may be teaching*Dupade*’s*Word*in old clothes even though he fears it might give the wrong impression, but he goes on preaching and doesn’t ask*Dupade*for anything – for clothing, or money, or food.*Dupade*sees he uses whatever he has, and*Dupade*says, “I’m going to give it to him.”

And then there is another kind of person that serves*Dupade*, but his motives are not pure. He only serves*Dupade*to have things provided for him. And*Dupade*says: “I think giving him the things he lusts after, he will take them and go on his way, or he’ll find excuses for not having time to serve me.”

You know that there are many of*Dupade*’s believers these days that have stopped following*Dupade*and no longer have anything to do with Him. They say they are tired of asking*Dupade*for the things they want and not getting them. But*Dupade*knows they are just trying to get things from him, they talk to him but just to ask for things, and he doesn’t give them what they ask for.*(James 4:3)*

You know how people say: “Oh,*Dupade*’s so far off!” But*Dupade*never forgets. Many believers have stopped following Him but*Dupade*never gives up on them. There are still many believers, though, for whom*Dupade*’s*Word*is food, and they partake of it faithfully, always reading his*Word*. Now, whose are the ones that are true believers.

We know that*Dupade*is always watching over his believers all over the world. He is careful of his believers and watches all about them. Therefore, I encourage us to be careful to pray to our*Father**Dupade*when we have needs. And stop saying, “*Dupade*lies. He says he’ll answer us, but he doesn’t give me the things we want for ourselves.”

Never stop coming to*Dupade*. He looks to see how sincere you are about the things you want, and if they will help you do the important things he has for you to do. That’s how it was long ago when I wanted to serve*Dupade*, and the day finally came when I did what his Word told me to do, and I’m still preaching his Word.

This is the meaning of*Dupade**’s**Words.*He says: “Whoever is tired of my*Word,*his faith is cold inside of him and he neglects my*Word**.**”*

**Ecarai tells the story of a man carrying****Dupade****’s Word****in his hands as he climbs****a mountain:**

You can see that while we are here on earth we are in Satan’s land, and that is why we need to be united as believers in*Dupade*. Here on earth, believers fall into sin. But they stand up again when they remember their faith in*Dupade*.

I have often reminded you about the story of a person that was on the way to heaven, and*Dupade*’s believers counseled him and said: “On this journey, you will come to high mountains. Be sure you don’t go to sleep on the way to the mountain peak.” But when the person finally reached the mountains and started to climb, he got so tired his legs shook, so he lay down and went to sleep. And when he got up to begin climbing again he left the*Word of**Dupade*on the ground where he had fallen asleep.

But the*W**ord of**Dupade*doesn’t tell us to go to sleep on our way to heaven, or to lose sight of*Dupade*’s*Word*.  The things we did in the past are in the past and we can’t go back.

It’s said that this believer in*Dupade*left and reached a landmark of theirs. Then he remembered that he went to sleep and left*Dupade Uruode*on the ground. He had gone a long way and he said, “In the morning, I’m going back for*Dupade Uruode.*

But he couldn’t go back again. That’s because*Dupade*’s*Word*doesn’t tell us to forget it and after a long time go back to it wherever we were when we left it.

**Ecarai talks to us about****Satan’s****temptations****:**

When the Ayoreos reach a landmark, they say, “This is where I killed a jaguar in the past.” And they fear that place because of the lions and tigers and they turn back and don’t stay there. But*Dupade*’s*Word*never tells us to fear the lions.

The lions are Satan’s temptations – he puts these lions of temptation in a person’s path. “These lions prevent the way to*Dupade*’s city. I’m not going to get there,” that person says. But if a person ignores the teeth of the lions, looking past them to his destination, he will, as intended, reach it. By this we know that Satan’s temptations are many here on this earth.

And when one of us pays attention to these temptations of Satan’s, it shows that he has forgotten*Dupade Uruode*somewhere on the mountain top.

If you believe in*Dupade,*you should try to read*Dupade Uruode.*There’s a possibility, that if we do that, we will grow in the knowledge of it, and be able to share it with others right here in our earthly village.

How thankful I am that throughout*Dupade*’s*written Word*we find all kinds of things. All the things I want, or need are there in his*Word*. If one of you is concerned about your spiritual life, you should really listen to*Dupade*’s*written**W**ord*and then you will learn about what*Dupade*disapproves of. You’ll also learn about the things*Dupade*approves of. Then you can talk to your*Father**Dupade*and tell him your sins and*Dupade*will forgive them.*Dupade*’s*Word*says: “If you are persecuted by*Cojñone*, or by*Ayoreos*be assured of this, I will never say to you, ‘Go away, I hate you.”*(John 6:37)*

*Dupade*says he will forgive those who persecute others. He even forgave those who killed him. Therefore, we know this about*Dupade Uruode,*there is nothing greater than*Dupade*’s*Word.*We know that Satan gave himself credit for Jesus’ death and for his burial. But he didn’t remember what Jesus said, “Even though I die, after three days I will live again.”

Because of this we know that*Dupade*is greater than all these things. He’s greater than your sins, as well. He’s greater than the things of this world and greater than all that pertains to Satan in his realm. Even if your sins are very ugly, don’t say: “I’m too ashamed to go to*Dupade*. I don’t think*Dupade*would forgive the kind of sins that I have committed.”

*Dupade*saw Satan when His son died, and when he was buried, but his Son lived again. And while in the grave, Jesus left the grave and went to*jnaropié**,*the place of the dead, to tell*Dupade’s Words*to those who were there. He reached*jnaropié*before coming out of the grave to come to us humans, who*Dupade*was concerned about. The days he was among us*finished off two**ayoréos**.*By this we know that*Dupade*is greater than everything else. So, don’t say, “*Dupade*, I don’t think you can erase my sins, and that’s why I’m not confessing them to you.”

I’ve just returned from being with some Ayoré people. Even though they are all Ayoreos, they are filled with anger, and they spoke angrily to me in the city of the*Cojñone**,*where people are always fighting. They think that their anger will overcome those that oppose their endeavors.

But the one who is most powerful is truly*Dupade*, and he proved himself in many ways. Once*Dupade*’s believers came to a river. The water parted, and they crossed over at the close of the day. They were at war, but their enemies could not overcome them. They traveled through a sandy land, but they were not thirsty. They went through a desert but didn’t die of thirst because*Dupade*was greater than all these things.

They looked forward to having honey and*Dupade*provided it. They looked forward to game, and*Dupade*sent game their way. And we know that*Dupade*could overcome every obstacle for them – even wars, even sin, even thievery.

You can see that*Dupade*would be disgusted with the terrible things they did, but then he would save them and place them once again in the light of day.

**Ecarai shares****Dorcas****’s****t****estimony:**

There was a woman who’s name I can’t remember. In the language of the*Cojñone*and in Ayoré her name meant*Deer.*When she died the people missed her. They missed the good things she did for them. They missed her kindness and generosity. But*Dupade*took his power and sent one of his servants to her and she came back to life.  And we know from this that*Dupade*is greater that all things, even death. (Acts 9:36)

**Ecarai tells about Eutychus:**

The Bible tells us about a young man that died when he fell from a window high up in the building. But*Dupade*healed him with his power, and he lived again.*(Acts 20:9-12)*

I tell you this because it shows just how powerful*Dupade*is. Whoever it is that won’t confess his sin to*Dupade*because he thinks*Dupade*could not forgive such sin as his is lying when he says: “*Dupade*says he forgives, but he doesn’t forgive sin as great as mine.”

But if someone doesn’t want to give up his sin, and goes right on sinning and disobeying*Dupade*, then,*Dupade*won’t do anything for him.

*Dupade*’s*Word*tells us that Jesus said: “Whoever comes to me I will never send him away. I’ll never say: ‘Go away. I don’t want you, because you are a sinner.’”

*Dupade*’s*Word*says: “Now is the time to tell me your sins.” I want you to know that*Dupade*’s*Word*explains that we should help each other. If you are strong believers in*Dupade*’s*Word**,*you should go to*Dupade’s*believers for help, and they can bring you back to*Dupade.*

This is all I have to explain about*Dupade*’s*W**ord*this morning, but we know that*Dupade*’s*W**ord*is always available to whoever wants to hear it. And whoever wants to be forgiven for the sin he has committed against us,*Dupade*will help us to help him.

We have the words that Paul wrote to the Corinthians that say: “If your people have stopped sinning, then, even though I am not with you in person, I forgive that person’s sin on the authority that*Dupade*has given me.”

Dupade – God

Yoqui-Dupade – Our God

Finished off my foot – Fifteen

Jnaropié – Place of the dead

Finished off two ayoréos*–*Forty

Cojñone*–*People not Ayoreos

Dupade Uruode – The Scriptures, the Bible

*Ecarai – Tobité, Bolivia – 1970’s.*

*Transcribed and translated to English by: Maxine Morarie.*

ayoreo:
**Ecarai catecã́riDupade:**Yoqui-Dupade Dios, yacaranguipis ome ua, ome baruode iji deréjnai tuné. Átaja baruode aja yoque gajnerĩ ga quérã iji yocúdode gajnerĩ, Yoqui-Dupade. Ujeta jec ũré yanguretágo ua enga baruode déji yocajei, Yoqui-Dupade.

Yacaranguipis ome ua ome baruode
uje chingo cuchade ome yoque jnese, que cucha amínique cus jeta ca chingo
yoque.

Jecute yicatecã́ri ua iji
gajnerĩ. Yingo yu aja ua ujetiga átaja yoc iji gajnerĩ, enga
yicatecãraitigade unojo chise ua gajnerĩ, ujetiga que cucha gajnáretique déji,
Yoqui-Dupade. Jé basa ga cucha gajnáretique déji enga yicatecã́ri ua ujetiga
ãro. Enga ichapise yicatecãraitigade ome ua, Yoqui-Dupade. Uje baruode chojninga: Dupade Ujopié chátaja yoque. Uje
que yirajágo yocuru quitique ujetiga yicatecãcoi Dupade, mu Dupade Ujopié*Espíritu Sánto*doi enga catecã́ri Dupade
ome yoque.*(Romanos 8 :26-27)*

Átaja baruode aja yu gajnerĩ
iji deréjnai tuné, Yoqui*–*Dupade,
guedé jnoringai tuné, Yoqui-Dupade. Ujeta yiraja quitígo.

Yoqui-Dupade, icatecã́ri ua gu
uje que ayoréode, jnanione, jé u cojñone uyoque jnese que yirajágo cuchárique.
Mu, Yoqui-Dupade, uhua u uje bẽrate pirajatigai tome ayoréode, banguresórone
uyóguiji eramone. Jecute yicatecã́ri ua ujetiga asiome báyuquetotiquéi tome yu
ujeta yiraja baruode quigode.

Yoqui-Dupade, yacaranguipis ome
ua iji gajnerĩ uje jé ijõre yoguiji Tobité uje yoquibosode ocoi iji Tobité.
Chamáñaje utagóji ua gu, Yoqui-Dupade. Chamáñaje ingongo yóguiji ua gatuaque
jetiga arodipise yóguiji guidai. Que gusu iji ne gajnerĩ uje yingo yoc aja ua,
Yoqui-Dupade, jé u iji dirode uje qué chicáji yoquíode, sequeredie uje qué
chicáji yoquiquéode. Que yirajágo baríringárique gu. Jecute qué yingongo yógaja
ua iji dirode jnese.

Dupade, icatecã́ri ua gajnerĩ
ome dínique dirome ujetiga soca seti ocoi yóguiji baruode jeta yingongo yógaja
ua, Yoqui-Dupade. Mu que gusu jetiga yicatecãcoi ua jé iji dirode jnese, Yoqui-Dupade,
jeta asi ome petotiguei tome yoque. Asi ome picaniratiguei tome yoque. Aroque
yógaja baroquéode ome baruode. Yicatecã́ri ua ome cuchade
jnese ome bajnacari Jesuse i. Amẽ.

**Ecarai e chacate ore:**Yajirigode u Dupade Uruode que, uje Dupade Uruode chingo yoc uje chi ayoréode uyóguiji erámi jnese uje yipéco yocajéode ome cuchárique yúrique enga unere yui ome yoque, enga chi yocadi tu ome Dupade Idai ujetiga Dupade ga di iji jnumi.

Jé u uje Dupade Uruode chingo
yóguiji ne uje Isaías chingo yoque nanique, jnani bajai, uje chi a chimo Dupade,
churúsãre Dupade ojningane cucha. Jecute chingome yoque. Chojninga: “A cojñone
iji erámi jnese, ayoréode iji erámi jnese, a ore pésu dajéode ome Dupade Idai yui
te jnenga. A ore chojninga: ‘¡Yico, ga yicagoi Dupade Idaidatei!”Que ore chĩ́care Dupade Idai gotique.
Ore chĩ́care ‘Dupade gajnepídi erãpe’ aja.*(Isaias
2:3)*

Uacarajáyo erámone uje erámi tu anírone ayoréode uyoque yocuniri casodicaite. Mu que ore chiraja dacajnai ome Dupade uniri uje ore chacáji erámi uje ayoré casodicaite ipéco yocajéode ome pibosode jogadode. Uje ore pésu dajéode uje ore chojninga: ‘¡Yico a. Yicoi. Yiraja cucha pibosode pijóningai. Yoquejnaréngone que déji to é!’

Ga unojo u uje chi ayoréode,
cojñone uyóguiji erámi chi yécãco yógaja uné erámi tuné uje yucuegoi
yoquiquetangónique. Mu que erámi tu unojo gotique uje jnani bajade chucué,
udore uje ore chijnóra Dupade. Ore ucuei tu Dupade Idai. Dupade uniri u uje
ore pota chécãre rágaja nanique. Enga Dupade uniri u uje yécãco yógaja to
iji dire uñeque.*(Hebreos 11:9-10)*

**Unere yui jeta yico gaté enga ugúcoji Dupade iguíjna gateminóri ajei:**Jecute yingome uaque iji durasei uje Dupade Uruode uje yécãco yocatígo ome Dupade adode. Que Dupade isocade u yoquisocade, que jnani bajade ore adode u Dupade adode, mu yajico Dupade Uruode enga Dupade Uruode u uje yécãco yocatígo. Ga uné guedé jnoringai te ga, yajire Dupade Uruode uje chingo yoque que. Chojninga: ‘Yinínapúsu yu diríca uje ore chojninga: ‘¡Yico a. Yicagoi Idai!’

Que Dupade Idai u gotique mu Dupade
iguíjnai. Déji aníri Salmos. Mu ise ga ayoréode uyoque yocuruode urásu ujetiga
jec ũré yoquirasique yogome eduguéjnane ore ipesudode cucha.

Uje ore chimáta rágaja iji
cojñone gari nanique ga a yoquirasiguirase ujetiga yojníjo: “Yirasique yu uje
ore chimate re aja cojñone úringane aníri purása.” Mu unojo que uruode qui tu
go.

Salmos urusarane uruode qui tu
uje chojninga: ‘Yirasique yu uje ore chiroque yu aja Dupade Idai yui.’

Enga yocuruode urásu to.
Ayoréode uyoque ujetiga yoquirasique yoc ujetiga jec ũré yanguretágo Dupade ga
yoquirasique yógome aníri, ore ojñane ome Dupade Idai yui. Ise ga jé ojningai
tu ayoré tu yu, que uyu gotique mu uhua uje baraja Dupade, uje bangureta Dupade.
Chojninga:*Yo me alegré*. Yirasique
yu.*(Salmo 122:1)*

Mu jec u uje ayorérique a
chirase daquitique erãpe enga irasique re ome yui. Irasique rome daquerãpe yui.
Jecute unojo uruode u uje chojninga: ‘Yirasique yui uje ore chiroque yu aja Dupade
Iguíjnai yui,’ chojninga.

Ga unojo jec u uje gajnui jec u
uté ica Isaías uje chingome yoque uje chojninga: “Punirone iji erámi údode a
ore chamáta ñane enga ore chojninga jne: ‘¡Yico e, ga yicagoi erámi uje ueradi!’”

Erãpe ueradi tu, mu que ore
chingome jeta erãpe ga adi tu uné uje yiraja cutérone, yiraja yocade, yiraja
cucha pibosode, mu Isaías chingo yoque uje chi gajnui tu cucaranone. Ga chi
gajnui jec u uje aníri ujetiga a Dupade uniri turásu cucarani utá.

¡Jõ, a yicorási iji ta ga yinínango
yui! Ga ore chacãra uje a yugúcorási ta ga ore chojninga: “¡Yico, yicagoi
cucarani iji te ga!”*(Isaías 2:3)*Ga ore nína yui uje ore chiraja uje íjnoque dequejnaréngone iji gu.

**Eduguéjna cuchape Dupade, enga uniri cuse, enga a cho darása iji yoguiji daniri uté:**Ga chi gajnui jec u uje ayoréode uyóguiji erámi yibicoi ñane nanique. Uje Dupade Uruode chiroque yogaja Dupade Idai yui ga ore chojninga: “¡Yico á, Yicagoi Dupade Iguíjnai to á!”

Enga unojo u uje Dupade Uruode
chingome yoque uje chi gajnui tu uje ore chojninga ‘Dupade*reino*.’ A Dupade uniri u jõ.

Que yiraja*reino*gotique mu uacarajáyo uje cojñone uruode. Yirajágo enga
uacarajáyo quigode.*Reino*unojo ore
uruode u uje Eduguéjnai Uniri u. Gajnepite Erãpe tiji dejode enga jé uté u.

Jecute chojninga, gajnui u Dupade*Uniri*iji ta, cucarani uje que
dequejnarétique déji ójo. Uje yoquirasique yui uje yoquiqui tuté enga que yico
unerotiguérique. Ga u uné uje yingo yoc uje ise ga yocuruode urásu to jeta jec
ũré yanguretágo Dupade.

**Cucha bisideque Dupade unire ueradi ome otigayasórone:**Jeque ca yanguretágo Dupade enga deroco Dupade Idai yui ome yoque. Yojníjo: “Iyieja yui jetiga Dupade di iji de jnumi. Iyieja yui jeta yicoi Dupade Uruode iji domíngo.” Chamáñaje yetájo Dupade Uruode gu. Chamáñaje que yanguretágo Dupade ga cucha bisideque ga gajnui u uje que yirajágo jnani ga yoco cucha bisidécho iji.

San Pablo chojninga: ‘Yocacajnaquéode
unojo cucha bisidécho, pũrusoringuéone ome anírone, Dupade guetesórone ore iji
yoque.’*(1 Corintios 1:18-25)*

Enga ise ga jec ũré, a que
yipótago Dupade ga iyieja conferencia yui jeta yicoi Dupade Uruode gajnerĩ.
Iyieja yui ga iramurase Dupade Uruode iguíjnai tiji yajei. Enga gajnui jec u
uje jecucha cucha bisidégode u ga que yirajágo quitique ga suru
yocangoninguinone bisidec.

**Unere Dupade Uruode yui tome udore ore uje angoninguinone guesája:**Mu gosode uje changureta Dupade ga unere Dupade Uruode yui tome ore ga udore ore uje angoninguinone guesája, ore áyipie angoninguinone guesája to. Ga unojo u uje a yocajnúone u unojo uje Isaías ojningane u uje chojninga: ‘Punirone iji erámi jnese, ore doi ga ore chojninga, ‘¡Yico á! Yicoi Dupade Idai te gá…’

‘¡Ajína yógaja to á!’ a ore
chojninga, mu que uruode upúsu mu yocuruode, ayoréode yocuruode qui tu. Uje
yijínango taja yui ga yirójo ñane aja. Yirajágo uje jecucha pibosode irágu iji
uaque ga que ayorérique betáji. Ga unojo u uje ise ga jec ũré yu, uje Dupade
anguresórone iji uaque, yiguíjna yui jeta eso Dupade Idai.

Jé basa ga Dupade guetesórone
ore ga, iyieja yui jeta Dupade di ga ca ore chisapo dacaniratiguérique iji de
jnumi, jec aja jeta Dupade chajire ore adode aja quitígo.*(Hebreos
9:27)*

**Tito cho dojodeDupadeanguresóño ore quique:**Yingo uaque gu uje yocacajnaquéode ucode iji durasei u ujetiga aníri, Títo, Pablo ujnacarabi, que ujnacaripísi mu iroi, gajnui jé u ore ujnacare. Uje chingo anírone cojñone ujetiga ore cha date bajade.*(Tito 2:11-13)*Ore cha dasijnaringuéone ome dabijnanone ga ore changureta Dupade ga ore gajnui jec u to ujetiga ore chécãre re aja to ome Dupade ojningai ome guidai uje ore chojninga: ‘¡Yico á! ¡Yicagoi Dupade Idai te á!” Ga ore chiraja Dupade anguresõre ga ore chiróque ñane aja Dupade Idai yui.

**Que yuchẽco yocangoninguinone gáyode, ga deroco Dupade Idai yui tomeYoque.**Jé basa enga a ca yuchẽco yocangoninguinone gáyode ome Tito, San Pablo, ore gajnúone jnanío udore ore acajnaquéode jne, enga ore chiroque jõroque yógaja Dupade Idai yui. Que yudúco oracajnaquéode ga ore acajnaquéode unojo que chacája yocáyipiedie. Que yuchẽco yocangoninguinone gáyode ga deroco yui tome yoque. Enga ore acajnaquéode unojo cucha bisidécho ome yoc. Ore acajnaquéode unojo cháji yoquibai tuné. Chamáñaje que yanguretágo Dupade ome úringai piquetangore gu.

Mu yuruode qui jé upo rĩ uje,
ujetiga jec ũré bangureta Dupade. Yocuruode u unojo, uje chi ore chojninga,
cojñone ore iji erámi: “¡Yico á! ¡Yicagoi Dupade Idai yá!” Ore ichorájnai uje
ore irasique Dupade Idai yui.

Jé basa ga ore nína yui uje ore
changureta Dupade. Enga ore chiroque ñane aja enga jĩrique ore chóji gaté ome Dupade
Idai yui.*(Hebreos
10:24-25)*

Jé basa ga udore ore uje ore
chiroque ñane aja Dupade Idai yui, a ore chojninga: “¡Yico á! ¡Yicoi!” Ore
uruode unojo chojmára iji jnumi, enga ore nóna Jesus aja gatajnoquei te.

**Que uñeque jnusi ñane ujetiga yiquihíjnojo aja Dupade gari iji neguidai:**Yingo uaque que uje uacarajáyo uje pujnusiétigai jogadi tu ujetiga yoco jmíti-jmíti ome ñane. Jé basa ga a yiquihíjnojo aja Dupade Idai te jnenga, iji Tobité tudaque, ga que yimóngo pujnusiétigárique gotique iji ne guidai. Ijínango ñane aja. Imóngo ñane aja gatajnoquei jnese ga que ayorérique chojninga: “¿Ité, ore casodicaite déji rique a? ¿Iapai casodicaite ore déji rique a?” Que uñeque jnusi ñane ujetiga yiquihíjnojo aja Dupade gari iji ne guidai.

Jé basa ga a que yanguretágo Dupade
jnese iji ne jnenga, yocúringane pujuyaque jogadi u ome Dupade.*(Juan
3:36)*

**A yojníjo jne: ‘Yijínango to Jacób ore Angarádejnai Dupade jne.”**Mu yirasique ujetiga yocadi tu jne aníri, jeta yocuruode u uné, Isaías ojningane, jnani bajaque, ore uruode unojo. Que ore urúo unojo mu chi cojñone uyoque yocuruode ome ñane uje chi yojníjo: “¡Yico á, ga yijínango Dupade aja Jacob casé, jnani bajai, ore Angarádejnai! Uje chi di jne ome Isaías edominone uje chimo Dupade uje Dupade chiqueta raja!”

Enga chi ore chojninga: “¡Yico á!
Yijiínango to Jacób ore Angarádejnai Dupade uje e di! Yijínango to
Yocangarádejna Dupade. Yocangaráni tu uje e di tiji yoque.” Chi ayoréode yocuruode
a u unojo to ujetiga Dupade di jne iji ne jnumi.

**Que bisitique déji jeta chinoningase Dupade ujuyaquéode:**Jé basa ga a uruode unojo ujetiga ore chojninga: “Dupade e di que’.”

Uje que ore changureta to enga
ore chichaga erámi taja ñane. Ore
chichaga erámi taja tijode ga pénu ome ore. Uje urasade e ore jnome, e ore cho jmíti-jmíti ome
ñane gajnerĩ.

Jecute ore chojninga: ¡Yico á!
¡Yicoi! Yijínango Dupade to, Jacob, jnani bajade ore Eduguéjnai, ore
Angarádejnai, ore Iquetangóri. ¡U arĩe, e di!” ore chojninga.

Enga ore nína yui uje e di gu.
Enga a yinínango yui to uje Dupade u Yoqueduguéjnai to. Dupade u
Yocangarádejnai to. Dupade u Yoquiquetangóri to.

Mu a ca yanguretágo to Dupade
enga, a iyieja jõ Dupade diringai yui tome yoque. Iyieja jõ Dupade ujuyaquei
yui tome yoc. Que toc jetiga qué ayorérique doi enga tãre aja ga cho pejna aja Dupade
diringai. Uje ayoré casodicai chi ore tãre aja, cho pejnac uje todo ore ojñane.
Qué cho pejnac, qué ore chiqueta uáguiji, mu que ayorérique doi Dupade
diringai, Dupade ujuyaquei ga tãre aja, que cho pejnaque gotique rĩ. Que
ayorérique doi ga chubúchu Dupade ujuyaquei ga chiquetápo uñeque iji ome
dañáningo. (Su soplo.) Chamáñaje Dupade ujuyaquei tu uqué. Que doi ga cucha
uñeque chĩro. Que qué doi ga cucha uñeque chinoningase Dupade ujuyaquei.

Uje jecucha yingome uac uje
dequejnaréngone uje ayorérique daijna dequeserane, ga chubúchu ga que uñeque
chiqueta daguiotique iji Dupade ujuyaquei. Uñeque ujetiga doi ga tãre aja
yúrique ga Dupade*Ujopié*suqué chicai
quedéjnarique ome yoque. Dupade*Ujopié*tu.

**Dupade Ujnacari diringai gajnui u chequé icamasugué:**Chi gajnui tu chequé icamasugué. Uje chequé ica basíji ga que ayorérique doi ga chubúchu ga tãre aja yotígome ga que chaquirãpo ica tiji ajei gotique rĩ. Chamáñaje diringai tu. Que ayoré chinoningase, jeta uaté chojningame jeta chiraja disiode: “Ore idoningayasónique jetiga qué yica chapo ga chejnape guedosíriguiji yajei to rĩ.”

Ga Dupade diringai chi gajnui u unojo uje chi di ga
diringai tu, ga que ayorérique doi ga chinoningase.
