That is why Jesus told his disciples: “Go into all the world,” he said. “but don’t take a lot of clothes with you,” he said, “just your shoes,” he said. “Don’t take any money with you when you go into the world to take*Dupade*’s words to the people of the world. Just trust me for what you need.”*(Matthew 28:18-20)*

This is so that we might know if you want to go with us – and if you do, just tell our Father*Dupade*. He will forgive your sins. Trust*Dupade*as I present you to him while you are still alive. On the other hand, I might die, and other believers will tell you about this. But you could die before hearing their teaching.

I know that all of us will die, and no one anywhere will remain alive forever. When a person comes to the end of his life, that will be the end for him, and he’ll never experience another kind of life.

Therefore, the Bible says, Jesus says: “Whoever believes in me, I will give him life. A new kind of life that is everlasting. A life that will never have an end.”

I know that when you are young you’re happy, and you look forward to going hunting, or to look for honey, or to play around with girls, or to eat from your own garden. And you think your life is never going to end. But we don’t know when*Dupade*is going to arrive.

I know if you’re a young woman, you’re happy, too. You play around and mistakenly think that’s all there is to life. But there’s more to life when you decide to trust in*Dupade*. If you want*Dupade*’s state of being the only one you believe in, that becomes your life from then on.

On the other hand, others of you will think all is going well for you, but when*Dupade*’s Son returns he will punish you because Satan has been your friend. When you chose to do what Satan wanted you to do, rather than believe in*Dupade*, you chose sadness, for one day, you will reach the land that*Dupade*hates. Therefore, I have this to say to you: If you haven’t put your faith in*Dupade*yet, do it right away while there’s still time.

You have heard that Jesus is going to come back again, and when he does, there will be no more time. It will be like when someone tells his fellow-person something and that person believes what he says. That’s because he sees the one who told him this. These are*Dupade*’s words: If you want to believe in him, then do it right away. If it’s night-time or day-time do it right then before we leave. If I were talking about myself, that I had not yet taken off it wouldn’t be important, but I’m talking about*Dupade*’s Son’s arrival for he is coming. If he doesn’t come tomorrow then we’ll not yet be leaving, I won’t be leaving yet. We could leave on a Sunday or a Wednesday. You probably know when Wednesday is. But we haven’t left yet. I don’t know when it will be that we leave for*Dupade*’s city. This is all that I have to teach us. And now I will talk to*Dupade*for us.

**Ecarai****talks to****Dupade****:**

*Yoqui-**Dupade*, I am talking to you tonight because I’m very happy that you hear what we say. When we prayed to you for rain, you poured it down upon us,*Yoqui-**Dupade*, and I want to thank you for the rain you poured out. You did that, so the men would be happy with their crops and we could be well-fed and healthy,*Yoqui-**Dupade*. You did it so that they would talk to you and thank you that you love us. You showed us your love when you brought rain to us. And now they are happy that they are eating the produce from their crops. Therefore, I am talking to you right now and asking you to watch over us, those of us here in Rincón. Help us right now and give us health and strength,*Yoqui-**Dupade*. Give us love, for we talk to you in Jesus’ name. Amen.

Dupade – God

Yoqui-Dupade – Our God

*Ecarai**–**Rincon de Tigre, Bolivia**– 1960’s.*

*Transcribed and translated to English by: Maxine Morarie**.*

ayoreo: San Juan ojñane u uje chingo yoque uje*Dupade**Uruode*pésu re nanique ga jnani, enga cho yoque.*(Juan 1:14)*
english: *Dupade Uruode pésu re nanique ga jnani*

ayoreo: Enga*Dupade**Uruode*uté u Jesute uje pésu cuchade jnese iji taningai; ga*Dupade**Uruode*jé jogate erámi bajac. Ga*Dupade**Uruode*jé u*Dupade*to. Erámi taningane u unojo uje*Dupade*pésu erámi, enga San Juan chingo yoque, chi: “Cuchade uje déji erámi jnese que ipesute re go.*Dupade**Uruode*ipesute cuchade jnese iji erámi. Jecute yingongo uac uje*Dupade**Uruode*ipesute erámi jogonórone cucha. Que cuchárique cuse ujetiga ca ipesute.*Dupade**Uruode*ipesute cuchade jnese.” *Dupade*chi utocaidi u Daruode (uje Jesute u) uje pésu cuchade. Enga*Dupade*chimate cuchade gu datocaidi iji erámi. Utocaidi u Jesute, dajnacari.*Dupade*chi pésu cuchade enga utocaidi u uje chimate cuchade. Ore chingo yoc uje pucuecaringuei garani tu uté to. Mu ijnangununie, pisijnaringuei pota sué gai mu que toc ujetiga sué*Dupade**Utocaidi*gai uje que sué*Dupade*gai gu. Que cucha uñeque sué*Dupade*gai uje*Dupade*ipesute cuchade jnese enga que cuchárique sué gai.
english: John tells us about when*Dupade*’s*Word*made himself into a man, just like us.*(John 1:14)* And*Dupade*’s*Word*was Jesus, the one who created all things so long ago.*Dupade*’s*Word*existed before earth was made. And*Dupade*’s*Word*was also*Dupade*. In the beginning*Dupade*created the earth, and the book of John tells us about that. He says: “The things that are in the world didn’t just make themselves.*Dupade*’s*Word*made everything in the world. Therefore, we are telling you that everything on earth was made by*Dupade*’s*Word*. There is nothing that he did not make.*Dupade*’s*Word*made it all.”

ayoreo: Chi*Dupade*iroque bajai u Juan uñai nanique aja erámi ujetiga chatata direcaringuei ujnomei ome erámi unirisórone ore. Que direcaringuepísi u Juan uñai uté nanique, mu di gu jeta chingome direcaringuei gosi aja erámi unirisórone.*Dupade*iroque Juan uñai uté ujetiga erámi uirisórone ore changureta direcaringuei Jesute ome uté ojñane enga piquetaque ore.
english: *Dupade*, it’s said, used His*Word*who is Jesus when things were made.*Dupade*created the world by means of his agent (Messiah) that he sent into the world. The one*Dupade*sent to do this was his Son Jesus.*Dupade*made things and his sent one finished things. And life came from him also. But darkness and sin wanted to overpower*Dupade*’s agent, but it wasn’t possible to overcome*Dupade*’s sent one, because*Dupade*can’t be overcome. Jesus is*Dupade*. And sin couldn’t overcome*Dupade*’s power, for it couldn’t overcome*Dupade*. Nothing can overcome*Dupade*because he created everything, and nothing in his creation can overpower him.

ayoreo: Mu uje Jesute e di enga chingo raja darasade ore enga Jesute urásarigo chi ore chetaque nanique. Mu uñéngo chi jé changureta enga udore ore u uje*Dupade*cho dabi iji ore. Enga jé u ore ojningane uyoque to uje yanguretágo iji diríe udé.*Dupade*cho dabi iji yoque to iji diríe uné gajnerĩ uje yanguretágo Jesute. Ujetiga uaque gosi changureta Jesute ga*Dupade*cho dabi iji uaque gosi to uje changureta Ujnacari. Jé urúo nanique.*Dupade*chojninga: “Ujetiga gosi changureta Yujnacari enga yo yujnacari iji gosi to.”
english: *Dupade*sent another John into the world first to tell the people of the world the news about the Light. But that John was not the real light, but he came to tell people who that Light was.*Dupade*send the other John so that earth dwellers would believe in Jesus who was the Light, because of what he told them, so that they would be saved.

ayoreo: Yacaranguipísome*Dupade*uje yocuñane changureta*Dupade*ga*Dupade*cho dabode iji yoque to. Ujetiga Jesute ore diapo jne rĩ ga yijínango ore aja oridaidaté gateminóri to. Jesute chi qué pésu yoquidáningo iji gaté jecute cáma diji yoc. Chi yijínango Jesus aja gaté gajnerĩ ujetiga di, udore ore uje ore changureta Jesute.*(Hebreos 11:2,3,6)*
english: Despite this, however, when Jesus presented himself to his countrymen, his countrymen wouldn’t accept him. But those that believed in Jesus, they were the ones that*Dupade*adopted, and that’s how it is with us who believe in him today.*Dupade*adopts us, too, when we believe in Jesus. If one of you believes in Jesus,*Dupade*adopts you because you believe in his Son. That’s what he promises.*Dupade*says: “If anyone believes in my Son I will consider that one to be my son also.”

ayoreo: *Ecarai – Tobité, Bolivia**–**1960**Marina de Morarie churúsare Ecarai acajnaquéode udojo nanique*
english: I am very grateful to*Dupade*that some of us have believed in*Dupade*and he has made us his children. When Jesus comes back again we will accompany him and go to his great city in heaven. Until he returns, Jesus is busy getting a place ready for us. We will go with Jesus to heaven when he comes for us, all of us who believe in him.*(Hebrews 11:2,3,6)*

ayoreo: Yingo Jesute acajnaquéode aja yoque to gajnerĩ uje Jesus chi qué dési jnumi nanique enga chacate darasade. Chacãra dacajnaquéode aja chugúperone. Jecute Jesus chojninga: “Chugúperone que chutãre to, mu Dupade u uje chisi ome ore posode ome ore. Cajicho to chugúperone,” chojninga, “que chutãre mu jé tagu dabosode. Angureni u Dupade, uraque dapesungṍri.”
english: I am going to tell us right now about how Jesus taught his people when he was on earth long ago. He used the birds as an example. And this is what he said: “The birds don’t sow seeds, but*Dupade*provides food for them. Observe them,” he said. “They don’t sow seeds, but they’re eating food. The one they trust to provide for them is*Dupade*, their creator.”

ayoreo: Chacãrapo dacajnaquéode aja cucha pojnánguenie rĩ ga chojningame dacadigode, darasade ore: “Cajicho to cucha pojnánguenie. Que origuidéraque cuse mu Dupade pésu ore ga ueradei ore. Ore iguidedie gajnui u ore úringai uerate.”
english: He also used flowers as examples and this is what he told his disciples and his countrymen: “Just look at the flowers. They don’t wear clothing to make them look beautiful; it’s just how*Dupade*made them. Their beauty is their clothing.”

ayoreo: Jecute Jesus chojninga: “Ingo uaque jetiga ca oyo uacáyugu uaque ujetiga ca yoquibotique cuse gajnerĩ, jeoga ca yoquiguidérigui cuse.”
english: Then Jesus said: “I tell you this so that you will stop worrying about not having food, or not having clothing.”

ayoreo:
Jesute
acajnaquéode quigode u uje chi Dupade chóji gaté ome yoque jeta cháta cuchade
aja yoque. Jecute yanguretágo Dupade.
english: These things that Jesus’ taught about the birds and the flowers show us how willing*Dupade*is to provide the things we need for us. So trust him for what you need.

ayoreo:
Chi Dupade
chojninga: “Beyoi yiroquéode bajaque.” Dupade iroquéode que etócho gotique ujetiga
yicoi ga yiquíjnongosi gánic. Dupade ojningane u ujeta yicoi pate omone to.
Pate omone u ujetiga yiyágo yoquisijnaringuéone ga yicoi Jesute, Dupade ore
acajnaquéode.
english: *Dupade*says to us: “First obey my commands.” It is not hard to obey*Dupade*, and it will never make us tired.*Dupade*is saying that we should be doing good things. It’s a good thing to stop sinning, and to start doing what Jesus, and our father*Dupade*, teach us to do.

ayoreo:
Chi yocajneque
Jesute ojningane udore cucha gajnerĩ. Jesute ojningane cucha u uje chi ca
yimóngopo chaguhérique, ãréraque ome yoque gajnerĩ. Jecute Jesute chingo yoque
unojo aja yoque jeta yanguretágo Jesute ga yiyágo cucha gajnaréngone,
pisijnaringuéone, poriquéode, jé aja cucha gajnaréngone jnese, uje Dupade
chetaque, ga yicoi pate arégode to iji gajnerĩ.
english: The things that Jesus taught are for us today. What He tells us is that we won’t be hungry, and we won’t be thirsty anymore. The reason He tells us this is so that we might trust him and stop doing ugly things like stealing or anything else that*Dupade*hates, and instead, doing all the good that we can do.

ayoreo:
Jecute Jesute
chingo yoque iji unojo dacajnaquéode. Chãcara dacajnaquéode aja cucha
pojnánguenie gu uje chi ca jmanéraque cuse ga que charigueta damánique, que
chiro daguidé ueradérac. Enga jé adi u ome chugúperenie uje que jmanéraque cus
enga que toc utacaringome mu jé chise dabosode. Chamáñaje ore ipesungóri u
Dupade uje Dupade u uje chisíome ore posode ome ore. Jecute chacãra
dacajnaquéode aja cuchade unojo.
english: That’s what these teachings of Jesus are all about. That’s why he gave the example of the flowers and said that the flowers didn’t have hands to groom themselves or to make beautiful clothing for themselves. And the example of the birds that don’t have hands and cannot plant crops – but they always have food. That’s because this is how they were created by*Dupade*, and*Dupade*provides for his creation.  Jesus wanted us to know this and that’s why he used these examples in his teachings.

ayoreo: Ojningai u uyoque ujetiga ca yoco yocáyugu ome cuchade unojo. Uje yoquijmainie cuse enga uyoque urásu ujetiga yipéco yoque enga yisago yoquejórigo ome yoquipotigátigo cucha. Jé uacamóño to uje chugúperenie que jmanéraque cuse. Jé uacamóño uje cucha pojnánguenie que jmanéraque cuse mu e ueradei ore. Chamáñaje Dupade u ore ipesungóri. Ore jmainie u Dupade, enga Dupade ipesute ore ueratigade. Dupade chosóre yoque to ga cho dajnúji yoque uje pésu ayoréode uyoque iji taningai nanique.*(Mateo 6:31-33)*
english: We are the ones he is referring to. He wanted us to stop worrying about our physical needs.*Dupade*has given us hands so that we could have the means of grooming ourselves and working to provide ourselves with the things we need. You see how the birds and the flowers don’t have a means of providing for themselves, because they were made without hands. But the birds have food, and the flowers have beauty. That’s because*Dupade*is their creator – he is their hands and he is the one who makes them beautiful. God provides for us also for we were made in his image in the very beginning.*(Matthew 6:31-34)*

ayoreo: *Ecarai – Tobité, Bolivia – 1960**Marina churúsãre Ecarai acajnaquéode udojo nanique*
english: Dupade – God *Ecarai – Tobité,Bolivia – 1970’s**Transcribed and translated to English by: Maxine Morarie.*

ayoreo: Dupade Uruode iji darujna aone u udojo uje noñome iji yocari nanique, ga yajnerangome ga yingongome ñane iji Dupade Uruode batigade. Mu ujetiga icoi Dupade Uruode mu qué yimóngo iji gaté bajaque jeta yicatecãcoi Dupade jeta Dupade chátaja yoque, ga chudute yoquicatecãraitigade jetiga ca cucha gajnarétique déji yoc. Jeta cucha gajnarétique déji ga ango Dupade, enga chimesẽre yocatígo ga chĩro. Amóñoji gaté ga yingo yogaja Dupade. Yoque Dupade Dios, yicatecã́ri ua gajnerĩ uje yacaranguipis ome ua, mu que yimo yocurasade jeta ore chingongara raja ua. Jeta ore chingo yogaja ua to iji dejai, mu gusu ua uje baraja cuchade. Átaja yoque gajnerĩ jeta yirajágo baruode quitígo gajnerĩ. Yirajágo ua uje bẽrate cucha omone ome banguresórone. Jé basa ga bapuse erámi taja ome guéode, sequeré adode, yoque Dupade.
english: *Dupade uruode batigade* These are*Dupade*’s Written Wordsthat he left with us long ago, and we have translated them and told each other about them where*Dupade’s*words land when we gather together.But before studying them, let’s “*sleep a bit above”*and ask*Dupade*to help us and to hear our prayers so that nothing ugly be among us. If there is any ugliness, tell*Dupade*about it, so that he will accept us and forgive us.*Sleep a bit above*as I bring us to*Dupade*.

ayoreo:
Mu
yicatecã́ri ua gajnerĩ jeta átapísaja banguresórone gajnerĩ iji ne baruode
batigai uje bayé su, aníri Jesute, bayé chimate dasagode cucha nanique iji udé
deréjnai ga chedu degu piyaridíji nanique ome yoque, Dupade. Jecute yatecãcoi
ua to gajnerĩ jeta átabája baruode aja yoque to gajnerĩ jeta yedúgo iji
deréjnai. Mu, yoque Dupade, yirajágo uje cucha pisagode sué yoquibáyode rĩ. Ga
yicoi yoquisagode cucha mu jetiga amesẽre yocatígo ga yicoi yoquisatígo
maringa, ca cuchárique chosi ti iji yoc, yoque Dupade. Yicatecã́ri ua ome
banguresórone ore iji ne Tobité jeta apésu ore enga ore quérã. Ore chajnerame
yoquininaquei iji ua to, yoque Dupade. Jé u
yocurasade misionérone rĩ jetiga átaja ore gajnerĩ jnenga ore chajnerame
yoquirajatigai ga yirajágo baruode gaté iji ore pijnatígo, yoque Dupade.
Yirajágo uje ore chiraja baruode, mu que ore chiraja yocurúrique mu gusu ua uje
baraja cuchade jnese, yoque Dupade.
english: *Yoque Dupade*, I’m praying to you because I’m very thankful for you, but I don’t know if all our countrymen here have presented themselves to you, if they’ve come to you tonight, only you know about that. Help us to understand your words. We recognize that you have given good things to your believers. You are not only good to us,*Yoque**Dupade*, but you include the whole world when you send rain for our rainy season crops.

ayoreo:
Yicatecã́ri ua
ome yocurása di ejode, Rósadé, Soríané ore. Udojo u uje ore changureta ua.
Yiraja uje yiquérango iji guidai to mu baruode batigai tu jeta yocuñeque jno
maringa, jetiga ca gosi cha baruode iji erámi. Adi u Rósadé ore jeta ore
chajnerame baruode iji yocacajnaquéode iji dajesúrigo erámio. Dupade,
yicatecã́ri ua ome cuchade jnese ome bajnacari Jesute i, amẽ.
english: I ask you right now, Lord Jesus, to really help your believers here*where your words land*. Your Father, Lord Jesus, finished his work long ago on this day, and the only thing he wanted us to do on this day was to rest in camp. For this reason, we ask you to kindly bless us with your words, that we*only*think about your words today. But,*Yoque Dupade*, we know we do have lots of work to do. And even though we do our work as unto you, don’t let anything bad happen to us,*Yoque Dupade.*I pray for your believers here in Tobité to grow in their faith, so that we might rejoice together and be happy.

ayoreo:
*(Juan 16:22)*Jesute acajnaquéode u yajirigode ome yoque iji San Juan urusarane uje Jesus qué déji jnumi nanique ga chatata dayé adode. Chatata dasagode ome danguresórone uje qué déji jnumi. Chatata anírone cojñone ijmongaquéode ome. Jesute, chamáñaje ujopierãque, mu chudute pijmongaquéode unojo to. Jecute chojningame dacajnaquéode udojo ome danguresórone. Chojninga:  “Yi jnenga, yijiapo ome gaté jne rĩ, enga páyuguhebátiga jne ome uaque. Jé basa ga pininaqué yicoringai tome uaque,” Jesus chojningame danguresórone uje déji jnumi nanique. Mu anguresórone que ore chiraja. Jesus jorane
que ore chiraja uruode quigode go. Ga chi ore cho dabuja ome ñane. Jesus
acadigode ore chojninga: “¿Chimo jóroque daruode quitique?  Uje chi aníri, chi pininaquei jne mu
pujnusiétiga rĩ.” Ga que ore chiraja Jesus acajnaquéode quitique.
english: I pray for the missionaries who live here in our village also that we might learn more and more of*your Words*from their lips. We know that they know your*Word*, but they do not speak our language very well, and only you know all these things,*Yoque Dupade.*

ayoreo:
Mu chi Jesuse
chudute. Chi chiraja ore áyipiedie quigode uje chi chise ore áyipiedie ajéode. Chi
chojninga: “Que arajáyo yacajnaquéode quitique go. Jecute uacapotáyo jeta
angoño ñane yacajnaquéode,” Jesute chojningame dacadigode. Ga u iji de
deréjnai tudé ujetiga ca yirajágo yocacajnaquérigo jeta ca gosi chiraja
yocacajnaquéode quigode, ga Jesus chacãra cuchade aja dacajnaquéode.*(San Juan 16:17)*
english: We pray for our countrymen Rosadé and Soríané, who have recently returned to us. They believe in you. I know that we grow in our faith here in our village, but it’s because of*the landing of your words*that any of us can leave here, and go on studying*your words*wherever we go, even as these men (Rosadé and Soriané) have done everywhere they’ve gone.

ayoreo:
Mu Jesus chojninga,
aníri, uje cho dacajnaquéode quique ome dacadigode ore: “Uacamóño uje,”
chojninga iji dacajnaquéode, “A chequéraque atique tagúji gajnerĩ enga chiraja
urõsoringuéone, mu ica base ga a urõsoringuéone ucode a chicadígui, a chicai
dajesúrique,” Jesus chojninga.
english: *Dupade*, I talk to you about all of this in the name of your Son Jesus.

ayoreo:
Chojninga:
“Adi jec u, chi cojñone ore chujé yu jne, enga erámi unirisórone uje que ore
changureta yu enga a ore irasique re jne uje ore chimate yu aja jnumi. Jé basa
ga yanguresórone chi ore jnusi jórongui yu, chi ore cho jóroque dáyugu re mu
quigade chi ore chimopo yu dirome rĩ. Chi yiquecapo dirome rĩ,” chi Jesus
chojningame dacajnaquéode uje chi cho quique.

Enga Jesus diringai chi adi jec u jne. Jesus toi nanique uje cojñone chujé ga cojñone ore irasique re uje que jé ore chimopo Jesúterique to gotique rĩ. Mu anguresórone ga que jé ore chimopo Jesus úringai ayoré ga ore jnusi. Ore jnusi. Jé ore cho dáyugu re, ga ore po ejode. Que yimo jeta Jesus anguretasórone u jnese. Mu chi chequé gotórigui ore poji Jesus ejode. Ga adi jec u,
chi Jesus di jne enga pujnusiétiga.  Mu
pininaquei tome yocuñane. Adi tu uje ayoréode, yocuñane a jnome Jesus quigade
to jne. Ga ore irasique rágaja Jesus aníri, idai yui. Enga udore ore uje que
ore changureta Jesus, ore iguiosode a ore jnome Jesus idaidaté yui to jne. Ore
jnusi jórogui daguiosode. Ore jnusi jórogui anírone, dajade, jeoga ore daja ore
ga ore neroi. Ga Jesus diringai gajnui jec u dáringai casicaite ayoré chuque
uje ore chujé iji jnumi nanique ga erámi unirisórone ore irasique re nanique
uje ore chimánaja jnumi. Jé basa Jesus chimate danguresórone ore aja
úringarique ijõre jne, enga u uje ore jnome gaté ga a ore iguiosode jnusi
jórogui ore.
english: I am reading a message that Jesus taught while he was still on earth from the writings of John. He spoke about his Father’s plan. He told his believers about what he had to accomplish while he was still on earth. He told them that people were going to threaten him. Despite being all-powerful, Jesus heard threats about people wanting to kill him. And that’s why He shared this with his believers and taught them saying, “I’m going to be leaving again for heaven, and when I do, it will be a time of sorrow for you. But it should be a time of happiness when I say I’m leaving.” This is what he told them while still with them.

ayoreo:
Que toc jeta
Jesus jé chijna ore to uje que déji darujna aone to jeta chojninga: “Chi Jesus
chejna yogaja dabai iji yoquidánique.” Que déji go. Mu déji
jetiga ayorérigo iji ore idánique chejna raja Dupade Uruode, Dupade úringárique
pangurete ga ore u uje ore chejna raja Dupade idai úringárique piquique.
english: But his believers didn’t understand what he was saying. Even his disciples didn’t understand what these words meant. So they whispered together, and this is what they said: “What is he talking about? One moment he says it will be a time of happiness, and the next he says it will be a time of sorrow.” They didn’t understand at all what this teaching of his meant.

ayoreo:
Ga Jesus acajnaquéode u unojo ome anírone danguresórone uje cho ore quique. Chojninga: “Que arajáyo yacajnaquéode unojo mu a jé diga arajayo jne.” Mu jec ũré enga chise diri uje ore chiraja, ga ore chejna guesi enga ore chojninga: “Jesus á, que qué yirajágopo bacajnaquéode gotique rĩ.” Enga
chojninga uje chejna dacajnaquéode udojo guesi uje cojñone chi ore chujé dirome
ga: “A ajnuso jóroque yu dirome. Que toc jeta que amóñopo yu gotique rĩ,” mu
chojninga, “A uacamóño yu jne,” chojninga.
english: But Jesus heard them. He knew what they were thinking– he could see right into their minds. And so he said: “You don’t understand my teaching at all. That’s why you’re talking about it among yourselves,” this is what Jesus said to his disciples.

ayoreo:
Mu jé ũré Jesus, uje chi cojñone chujé ga ore chijóchame, ga que ore chimopo gotique rĩ. Mu chi Jesus ecapo rĩ enga e chiquetapo raja dacadigode uñane rĩ iji guiguíjna ajénique udojo uñeque. Ga jogadi tu uje ore irasigapo re rĩ. Ga Jesus
acajnaquéode u udojo ome yoque uje que uyoque gotique, Jesus chi acadigode u
nanique, mu jé udojo qui uté Dupade Uruode jeta yingongo ñane. Jesus chiqueta
raja origuíjna ajei uñai te ga, ore irasigápo re rĩ uje ore chimo Jesus. Ore
chingo ñane, ore chingo Dupade anguresórone uje Dupade ujnacari casica e dépoi
ore ujade rĩ. Que yimo jeta guedosírigo a gare, uje déji ore gari uje chi
Jesus ecapo nanique rĩ. Ise ga ca potigayaquérique dépoi rĩ ome anírone jnani
bajade, Jesus anguresórone. Que yiraja jetiga 40 dirode uje chi Jesus déji ore
gari, dacadi casodica ore gari, iji ore iguíjnane, ore aquiningane ga chi
chiqueta raja ore.
english: We are like that today, too; when what we teach is not understood, we must give examples to help people to understand. And that’s what Jesus did, he gave them examples. But I’m telling you, they still didn’t understand. They kept saying: “What do Jesus’ words mean? Why is he saying he’s going to leave, and that we’re not going to see him anymore? We don’t know where he’s going, we don’t understand this teaching of his?”

ayoreo:
Iji taningai Jesus ga chi ca ore chiraja go. Taningapísi u uje chi Jesus chiqueta raja jnanione ore uje qué ore catecãri ñane ome Jesus ga chi ore chojninga: “¿Jesus ucacasica ya? ¿Chamo jóroque gotique uje ore chujé ica? Ipesute uyoque ica múñi.” Mu Jesus chi
e chiqueta raja ore ga chi chojninga, “Jé uacojñane ¿gotígo a?” chi chojninga
Jesus.
english: But Jesus explained his teaching to his disciples in this way: “You know that when a woman goes into labor she feels pain, but when she gives birth, she forgets all about the pain. She doesn’t think about it anymore. She goes happily on her way.

ayoreo:
Mu ore
chojninga: “A jé ca adute to uje ore chujé yoqueduguéjna case Jesus, ga que jé
imóngopo go, ga a ca yimóngopo to jne rĩ.” Ga Jesus
chojninga: “¡Yiraja! Jé go uje a ore angurete gotique diríca mu ome
dejnaréngone, mu jé ore chujé.” Chi Jesus uruode a udojo mu que ore chiraja
Jesuse go uje catecã́ri ore.
english: Jesus said: “That’s how it is with what will happen to me. The people are going to kill me, and it’s going to make them very happy to see me dead. On the other hand, my believers will miss me, and they will be sorrowful, but then they’ll see me again. That’s because I’m going to come back to life.” This is how Jesus explained to them what he meant when he said they would be sorrowful and then they would be happy.

ayoreo:
*(Apocalipsis 21:27)*Yoque gosi uje a yipótago yicoi Dupade Uruode ga yangácoi Dupade Uruode ga yirajágo quigode. Mu que yanguretágo Dupade Abi ga que toc jeta yirajágo jeta yijínango Dupade aja gaté. Que yirajágo yocúringárique Dupade aníri, jeoga yoquiquitique jeta Dupade di jne enga jno yocúdi, ga que yirajágo. Yojningame:
“Dupade á, yijo aja baruode. Yangári baruode ga yiraja quigode uje chi arĩapo
rĩ. Yirate baradedie to ga yiraja baradedie uje yijñecacója. Yiji baruode ga
yirãrája gai ga yiraja.” Ga a gosi chojninga jne: “Mu que yiraja gotique uje
que bo yúdi to,” a gosi chojningaras ome Dupade.
english: And when Jesus comes back it will be like this. Jesus died when the*cojñone*killed him and they rejoiced that they wouldn’t see Jesus anymore. But when his believers did not see Jesus in human form they missed him. They missed him and were sorrowful and cried for him. I don’t know if all the believers cried, but some of the women*cried his nearness*(mourned for him) after he died.

ayoreo:
Mu a Jesuse,
Dupade, chajnai ga chojninga, “Mu bei que déji darujna aone to go. Que yiji
orúdi to uje que ore i déji darujna aone go. Yiraja uje ore ijója yuruode ga
ore tangári. Ore chirate yiradedie ga ore chiraja quigode. Ore chiraja uje ore
catecã́ri yu, mu que ore changureta yu. Que yurúsãre ore i iji gaté ga que
yiji ore údi gotique,” chojninga.*(Efesios
2:8 aja 9)* *Ecarai – Tobité, Bolivia**–**1970**Marina de Morarie churúsãre Ecarai acajnaquéode udojo*
english: Now when he returns, it will be a time of sorrow again, but also, for us who believe, a time of happiness. It will be a happy time for the Ayoreos, and the rest of us when we leave with Jesus. And they will rejoice with Jesus as they near his city in heaven. But their relatives who do not believe in Jesus will not leave for Jesus’s big city in heaven. People will miss each other, in vain. They will miss brothers and sisters. Jesus’s return will be like it was when he was killed in his human body and the earth dwellers rejoiced long ago as they finished him off. But on the other hand, through his death Jesus brought his believers into salvation, and took them up to heaven with him and their unbelieving relatives will miss them with no hope of seeing them again.

ayoreo: Yoque Dupade, yicatecã́ri ua gajneri. Yacaranguipise uje yingome yóguiji ua iji guedé jnoringai to. Átaja yoque gajnerĩ, Dupade.  Dupade, ajé bajmanai tiji yoquibai jnese, Dupade, Davidé (Ecarai) uyoque, Dupade, iji guedé jnoringai.
english: *Ajé bajmanai tiji yoquibai* *Yoque Dupade*, I pray to you right now. I’m grateful that we can come before you this afternoon. Bless us right now,*Dupade*. Lay your hand upon all of us,*Davidé*and the rest of us, this afternoon.

ayoreo:
Unejaque
Davidé acaditigade, Yoque Dupade, enga yacaranguipis. Ajé bajmanai ujopié aja
yoquibai jnese, Dupade, yocurasade yóguiji ne gajnerĩ, Dupade. Enga
yacaranguipise, yoque Dupade, uje unéraja yoque Davidé acajnaquéode iji guedé
jnoringai tuné. Yacaranguipise ome baruode, Dupade. Yacaranguipise uje yingome
ua ome ore ji guedé jnoringai gajnerĩ.
english: *Davide’s*teachings are different,*Yoque Dupade,*and we are very thankful for that. Place your powerful hand upon all of us, all of those here in our village right now. Thank you,*Yoque Dupade,*for*Davide’s*good teachings this afternoon. And I’m thankful for your Word,*Dupade*. It’s a privilege for me to hold you up before those gathered here this afternoon.

ayoreo:
Bamo uje que cucha putocaitique cuse, Dupade,
gusu putocaitó Dupade uje ujopierãc ua, Dupade. Bajneque cuchade, daijna cuchap
ua. Bajnué cucha dacadode gai jnese, Dupade. Ajé bajmanai tiji yoquibai,
Dupade, iji dejai, iji diri to, iji dirode ore jnese, Dupade.
english: You know that there is no other one we can commission,*Dupade*, and you are the only one who sends people on your errands,*Dupade*, because you are powerful. You control everything, and you are a great healer. You are above all there is,*Dupade*. Lay your hand on us,*Dupade*, at night, during the day, every day,*Dupade*.

ayoreo:
Ãro
yocurõsoringuéone iji yocúdode jnese, Dupade. Yocurajédie, yoquejnarec uñane,
Dupade, uje déji chequedíe, disiode ore, jnanione ore jnese, Dupade. Ãro
urõsoringuéone iji yocúdode jnese, Dupade. Etoguipise yoque, Dupade, jeta yico
dínique jnese, Dupade, ome yocúringai ayoré ijõre.
english: Get rid of the pain we have in our bodies,*Dupade*. Heal our coughs and the other illnesses we have,*Dupade*– do this for the women, the children, and the men, all of them. Erase the pain of all our bodies,*Dupade*. May we be strong,*Dupade,*so that we can face each day in good health.

ayoreo:
A yangácoi
baruode iji Davidé pijnani gajnerĩ, enga yacaranguipise, Dupade, uje yutṍri
ua, Dupade. Bamo uje que yáyipie cho cutu ome bajnacari Jesus uje yisa
bajnacari Jesus ome yáyipie, Dupade. Gusu pujnuéngaisõre. Que yicadíjoi ua,
Dupade, enga yacaranguipise, Dupade, uje yingome yogome ua iji guedé jnoringai,
Dupade. A yico baruode gajnerĩ, Dupade.
english: Soon we will be hearing your word from*Davide’s*lips, and I’m so thankful to be able to call upon you,*Dupade*. You know that I have decided to faithfully follow your Son Jesus and I have asked him into my heart. He is the only one who is victorious. We have not forgotten you,*Dupade*. And we are so thankful to be able to come before you this afternoon. We are ready to receive your Word.

ayoreo:
Aródape ore
to, Dupade, iji dajei. Asíome betotiguei ome*camioneta*, Dupade, ga ore chicáji Camiri, Dupade, enga quítequiguíji
yoque, Dupade, jeta asíome betotigué querui tome ore.
english: Watch over all who are traveling,*Dupade*. Give your strength to the pick-up truck,*Dupade,*as they go to the city of Camiri; may they return quickly to us because of your big strength.

ayoreo:
Aródape
Davidé urasade to iji oridai Tobité, Dupade. Ajé bajmanai taja ore gai to jne,
Dupade, iji dejai tuné. Arote ore ome cucha gajnaréngone to, Dupade,
dequejnareque noñángone, Dupade. Que cuchárique pésu urõso ome ore to, iji ore
idai, Dupade. Arote ore ome cucha gajnaréngone jnese to, Dupade.
english: Watch over Davide’s countrymen in their town of Tobité,*Dupade*. Place your hand on them also,*Dupade*, this very night. Protect them from all harm,*Dupade*, and from serious illnesses. Don’t let anything bring them pain in their village. Protect them,*Dupade*, from everything harmful.

ayoreo:
Arote
jnanione ore to, Dupade, uje ore déji dajei, Dupade, uje ore chucué pibosode
ome yoque, Dupade, enga yacaranguipise gajnerĩ, Dupade.
english: Watch over the men who have gone to get food for us and are on the road tonight.  And we thank you for doing this,*Dupade*.

ayoreo:
Ome Satanás,
Dupade, arote yóguiji dirode jnese ome Satanás, Dupade, enga yacaranguipise uje
yutṍri ua ome yoque iji guedé jnoringai, Dupade, uje yicatecã́ri ua ome
bajnacari Jesuse i. Amẽ.
english: About Satan,*Dupade*, protect us every day from him; how thankful I am this afternoon to be able to ask you for these things; I speak to you in the name of your Son Jesus.  Amen

ayoreo: *(Lucas 16:19-31)*Yacajnaquéode a u anire, Dupade anguretasṍri jmatátique uje i tu Lázaro, uje que tagu dabotique mu tibágui cojñoque ugucharãi jetiga tagu posode iredie.
english: **The rich man and Lazarus:***(Luke 16:19-31)*My teaching will be about a poor man named Lazarus who was a believer in*Dupade*, but who had no food to eat and who would go to a certain rich man’s house to ask for the food that he had left over.

ayoreo:
Mu udé
deréjnai ga Pablo que cãra dabosode ome uaque gotique. Jé basa ga jnani bajai
chi ugucharãque, mu que chajire Dupade anguretasṍri yúrique go.
english: These days Paul Wyma has been sharing what food he has with you, not holding anything back. But the rich man I am talking about who lived long ago, had a lot of money, but he didn’t pay any attention to the poor man who believed in*Dupade*.

ayoreo:
Mu chise
gójni diri uje Lázaro uté toi ga cojñoque ugucharãi topoi rĩ. Chi Dupade
anguretasṍri uté chise dojode Dupade anguretasórone iji gaté iji yocai jnani
bajai Abraham gari iji jogadi.
english: But eventually the day arrived when they both died, the poor man and the rich man also. The believer, it’s said, found himself in heaven with other believers and with our forefather Abraham.

ayoreo:
Enga, aníri,
cojñoque ugucharãi casica ujuya dabosode iji jnumi, uté qui tu jnaropié. Jé basa
ga cojñoque jmatai uje changureta Dupade, enga chicáji Dupade anguretasórone
jogadi uté, mu idógosi ore iji jnaropié jogosórone iji te ga. Chi ore chimo
ñane, enga chi cojñoque, aníri, ugucharãi, chi chimo Dupade anguretasórone.
english: And the rich man who had lots of food when he was alive on earth, found himself in*jnaropié**(**the*place of the dead).  But the poor man, as I’ve said, who believed in*Dupade*went to the place where those who believe in*Dupade*go when they die. But the two places were near each other. They could see each other, and it’s said the rich man saw those who believed in*Dupade*.

ayoreo:
Chi chimo
Dupade anguretasórone, enga chojninga: “¿Jé gotique ca ango barasai cojñoque
jmatai uje i tu Lázaro enga chátaja yu jne? Qué ango barasai jeta chaca jmanai
tiji yodi enga chuta yarei tome.”
english: When the rich man saw*Dupade*’s believers, he said to Abraham: “Why don’t you ask the poor man named Lazarus who is there with you to do something for me? Just tell him to dip his hand in some water and touch my tongue with it.”

ayoreo:
Mu yocai
jnani bajai Abraham chojninga: “Úngare,” chojninga. “Cachi ca toque jeta qué
ayoré Dupade anguretasórone uñeque enga chicáji jnaropié jeta chajniapója
pucuécaringué ichárique te rĩ.”
english: But our forefather Abraham said: “That’s impossible. A man who believes in*Dupade*can’t go to*jnaropié*and then come back to the place where he has eternal life.”

ayoreo:
Enga chi
cojñoque ugucharãi chojningame Abraham: “Jé go ¿qué aroque Lázaro uté aja
yocurása casodica iji jnumi te ga, chingo yesa casodica uje yachopise uje
yicajnóriji jnaropié udé,” chojninga.
english: And then the rich man said to Abraham: “Well then, why don’t you send Lazarus to my relatives down on earth to tell my brothers what it’s like here – that I can hardly stand it in*jnaropié*because I am so thirsty here.”

ayoreo:
Mu Abraham
chojninga: “Úngare, Dupade uruode, Moises urusãrane, déji jnumi casodica ome
ore, enga a ore chudute, enga a ore chimate enga ore chicáji yoque to rĩ,”
chojninga. “Mu a ca ore tangári acajnaquéode enga ore qui tu uaité to,”
chojninga.
english: But Abraham said: “No, they have*Dupade*’s*Word*and*Moses’ writings*on earth where you used to be. If they listen to*Dupade*’s*Word*and do what it says, then they will come to where we are,” he said. “But if they don’t pay attention to the teachings of*Dupade*’s*Word*, then they too will go where you are,” that’s what Abraham told the rich man.

ayoreo:
Enga jec
ũrépise jnani bajai Abraham, uje déji deréjnai tudé to uje cojñone, ayoréode
yetájo Dupade Uruode. Enga chise gójni diri ujetiga Dupade jno yocúdi, danguresórone
údi, ga yipótago jóroga ore.
english: And Abraham told him the truth, and it is just as true today, for there are*Ayoreos*and*Cojñone*who refuse to hear*Dupade’s Words*. And eventually the day will come when*Dupade*will take us who believe in him to heaven. In vain, we may wish others were there with us, but it will be too late.

ayoreo: Que toc jeta ayoréode chutṍri Jesus iji diringárique enga ore chojninga: ‘¡Jesus á! ¡Jesute á! Acarape, a yangureta ua jeta yijína ua to é.’ Úngare. Dupade Uruode que qui gotique.
english: It won’t be possible when Jesus returns for non-believers to call upon Him or to say: ‘Jesus! Jesus! Stop! I’ll believe in you now so that I can go with you.’ No,*Dupade*’s*Word*doesn’t say that this is possible at all.

ayoreo:
Dupade Uruode
chojninga iji deréjnai ome ore: ‘Yiroque yirógode ore aja erámi diríca ujetiga
ore chingo yuruode aja ayoréode, cojñone uáguiji erámi, enga, aníri, ore
changureta yu, enga gusu ore jeta ore nóna yu to, ore chijína yu.’
english: What*Dupade’s**Word*does say today is this: ‘I sent my servants into the world to preach my*Word*to all of you,*Ayoréos*and*Cojñone*so that you might believe in me – and those that believe will go with me to heaven, but only them.’

ayoreo:
Mu jé
ũrépise Dupade Uruode uje que toc jeta Jesuse di enga catecã́ri yóguiji jnumi
tuné; jé educadode déji yocari iji jnumi.*(Hebreos 1:1 aja 2)*
english: *Dupade*’s*Word*is very clear about this, that when Jesus returns, it’s not going to be possible for him to talk with those of us who are left behind; and there’s going to be a lot of tears among us who are left on earth.*(Hebrews 1:1-2)*

ayoreo:
Jé basa ga
jnani bajade enga Dupade chi chiqueta raja ore nanique uje cama uruode déji
darujna arone gotique nanique. Jé basa ga deréjnai uné enga utocaidode cuse uje
ore chiraja Dupade Uruode enga ore u uje ore chatata Dupade Ujnacari Jesuse
adode aja yoque.
english: Before his*Word*was written,*Dupade*would appear to the men who lived long ago and talk to them. And now that it is written, he sends those who know his*Word*to tell us the good news about his Son Jesus.

ayoreo: Chi jnani ugucharãi udé chojningame jnani bajai Abraham: “Ango yesai ore enga cáma chicai jnaropié to,” chojninga.
english: And that rich man continued to talk to Abraham and he said: “O please, let my brothers and others know not to come*to jnaropié*,” he said.

ayoreo:
Mu Abraham
chojninga: “Úngare, a ca yipésu bojningane udore. Dupade Uruode anguretasórone
ore chingome Dupade Uruode ome ore iji jnumi uné, mu que ore changureta go.
Enga gusu ore ujetiga ore chicai besai ore jne,” chojninga.
english: But Abraham said: “No, I won’t do what you ask. Those on earth who believe*Dupade*’s*Word*are telling people what*Dupade*’s*Word*says, but they don’t believe them. They are the only ones who can go to your brothers.” This is what Abraham said to the rich man.

ayoreo:
Enga jec u
iji deréjnai tudé to, uje a yingo Dupade adode aja uaque gosode, mu jé yoquiqui
udé jnese. Mu gosode chetaque Dupade Uruode, enga a gosode adi tu cojñoc
ugucharãi uje chirase dáringai ayoré ijõre iji ne jnumi. A ca chise Dupade
Uruode unétique gotique iji dáringai uecai, enga Dupade Uruode que chise uqué,
ga ca unéningai cuse ome gotic. Chetaque gu.
english: And the same thing happens with us today, for when I teach*Dupade*’s*Word*some do not believe it, and they are like the rich man who only took pleasure in being healthy and happy on earth.*Dupade’s Word*wasn’t interesting to the rich man when he was on earth. He didn’t find*Dupade*’s*Word*appealing when he was alive, so it had no affect on him, nor did he benefit from it, because he refused to listen to it.

ayoreo: Jecute jnacarode uyoque yingongome don Len nanique ujetiga Dupade chĩro yoquisijnaringuéone enga yojníjo: “Don Len á, yoquigaidi u ujeta yijínango ua to jne aja Dupade idai.”
english: That’s why long ago some of us young men went to don Len and told him that we wanted*Dupade*to forgive our sins and we said: “Don Len, we want to go with you to*Dupade*’s town.”

ayoreo:
Adi jé u uné
deréjnai, diyajmaque. A déji 20 años, jé u 20 años uje yudúco Dupade uruode
ica, mu qué cho, cáma yejnángo sequeré gotique.
english: It’s been a long time since then. Probably 20 years have gone by since we first heard*Dupade*’s*Word*, but things are the same, and we haven’t given up hope of his coming.

ayoreo:
Jnani bajade
ore chirote Jesute yui te ga, chi déji*dos
mil año*uje ore chirote Dupade ujnacari Jesute yui. Mu uyoque cáma yejnángo
sequeredie gotique. Enga ujetiga yanguretágo Dupade enga que yejnatígo gu
jetiga Dupade di.*(Mateo 24:44)*
english: Those first people who believed long ago expected Jesus to return at any time, and now it’s 2000 years later and we are still waiting for*Dupade*’s Son Jesus to come back. We still believe*Dupade,*though, and have not given up hope of his return.*(Matthew 24:44)*

ayoreo:
Mu Dupade
uruode qui uñeque déji uje Jesus chojninga: “Que yiraja yiríringai, mu a
yiyágue uacamánique,” chojninga, “ujetiga yĩri iji uac.”
english: There is another place in*Dupade*’s*Word*where Jesus says: “I do not know when I will return, but when I do, suddenly I’ll be there.”

ayoreo:
Gosode a
chojninga: “Tac ua, jecute Dupade ujnacari que di.”
english: Some might say: “You’re lying, Jesus is not going to return.”

ayoreo:
Mu Jesus
chojninga: “A yingo yiríringai ome uhuaque gu uje yigaidi tu jeta jec ũrépise
uágome uacanguretarétigai jeta yiyague uacamánique,” chojninga.
english: But Jesus said: “I’m telling you the truth: I am coming back. I want you to be sure that your faith is sincere so that when I suddenly appear, you will be ready.”

ayoreo:
Jecute
yingome uac jeta gosi pota changureta Dupade to ga angome yoque.
english: Therefore, I am telling you that if one of you wants to put his faith in*Dupade*, tell us.

ayoreo:
Que yiraja
jeta uacanguretáyo Jesuse jnese, que yimo jeta anguretáyo, que yimo jeta
uaquetácho Dupade jnese. Mu Dupade a chojninga: Gosi ujeta catecã́ri yu, gosi
uje chiraja yiradedie, gosi uje chiraja yuruode, mu que changureta yu, a ca
toque jne jeta yi údi aja gatajnoquei.
english: I don’t know if all of you believe, I really don’t know; neither do I know if all of you are resisting*Dupade*. But*Dupade*would say to you: “It won’t be the person who prays, or the one who knows the songs about me, or who knows my Wordthat I will take to heaven, but the one who has faith in me.”

ayoreo:
Gusu uje gosi
chojninga: “Dupade á, yangureta bajnacari Jesuse jeta chiqueta yu. E yingo
yisijnaringuéone aja ua jeta ãro ga. Apésu yu ga o bajnacari iji yu, ga yijína
ua to jne aja gatajnoquei to.”
english: Only the person that says: ‘*Dupade*, I am trusting your Son Jesus to save me. I am confessing my sins to you so that you can forgive them. Adopt me as your child and I will go with you to heaven.”

ayoreo:
Ga Dupade
jĩrique chĩro gosi isijnaringuéone. Que yimóngo jetiga chĩro, que yirajágo
isocáringome, mu yirajágo uje Dupade déji yocajei te ga. Cachi yocajei cho
pujnui. Cachi yoco pujnúringai yocadode enga unere rĩ Dupade uruode yúode enga
unojo u uje yirajágo gu.
english: *Dupade*will then quickly forgive that person’s sins. We don’t see him erase our sins, and we don’t know how he does it, but we do know when he is in our hearts. That’s because our hearts feel lighter. The things we go through here on earth seem easier and we look forward to reading or hearing*Dupade*’s*Word*, and that’s how we know*Dupade*has forgiven us.

ayoreo: Mu ujetiga gosi chiraja Dupade ajegaidode, chiraja bisideque uje que changureta Dupade enga que toc jeta Dupade jno uaque gosi údi go.*(Mateo 7:21)*
english: But if someone knows*Dupade*’s*word*, he knows it in vain if he doesn’t believe in*Dupade,*and it will be impossible for*Dupade*to take that person to heaven with him.*(Matthew 7:21)*

ayoreo:
Dupade Uruode
qui u uje chojninga: Gosi a chiraja Dupade Uruode, chiraja Dupade iradedie,
chiraja uruode quigode, mu que chingome dasijnaringuéone ome Dupade jeta
chĩro, enga que toc jeta Dupade jno uté gosi údi to.*(Efesios
2:1, 4, 8, 9)*
english: This is the meaning of*Dupade*’s*Word*: Someone might know*Dupade’s word,*sing his songs, but if he does not confess his sins and ask*Dupade*to forgive them, then*Dupade*will not take such a one to be with him.*(Ephesians 2:1,4,8,9)*

ayoreo: Yingome uaque gajnerĩ ujetiga ca yetájo Dupade uruode atatasórone. Ujetiga ca yetájo Dupade ujnomei jnungórone ore. Enga jé ca yoco jnani bajade ore uje chi ore chetaque Dupade anguresórone iji uraque dajade nanique.
english: StartFragment *Jé ca yetájo Dupade ujnomei jnungórone ore* EndFragment I am telling you that we should not dislike those who preach*Dupade*’s*word*. Nor should we dislike those who carry*Dupade*’s word to other places. We should not be like those who lived long ago that hated God’s believers that lived among them.

ayoreo: *(Jeremías 20:1,2)*Enga yimóngo iji uné darujna aone uje e ore jno Jeremías údi uje ore chigaru jmanecade. Ore chetaque Dupade uruode iji Jeremías pijnani uje chĩ́care ore ome Dupade uruode.
english: **he prophet Jeremiah:***(Jeremiah 20:1,2)* We read in the scriptures that they put chains on Jeremiah’s arms and took him away. They did this because they didn’t like what Jeremiah preached to them from*Dupade*’s word, for he applied what it said to them personally.

ayoreo:
Mu jé ca yoco jnani bajade
udore ore gajnerĩ enga jé ca yicoi ore isocade to gajnerĩ. Enga ca yetájo
Dupade anguresórone iji yocujade to gajnerĩ.
english: But we should not be like the people in Jeremiah’s day and should not treat anyone like they treated Jeremiah. And we should not hate those among us that believe in*Dupade*.

ayoreo: Yirajágo uje qué ore pésu cucha gajnarétique iji Dupade irajasórone uje ore gaidode u Dupade uruode quigode. Enga jé ca yoco unojo gajnerĩ, Jeremías igarisórone ore, jeta ca yetájo Dupade anguresórone, Dupade uruode atatasórone, Dupade ujnomei jnungórone ore.
english: We know that people are still persecuting*Dupade*’s people for doing what*Dupade*instructs them to do. And we should not be like those who put Jeremiah in chains; we should never mistreat any of*Dupade*’s people, any of those that preach his word, or those that are missionaries.

ayoreo: Dupade uruode chojninga: “Ujétiga gosi cho darúja yanguresóri ome daru gajnaréngone, ga a yo yurúja gosi to jne.” Jecute ingome yoque gajnerĩ ujetiga ca yoco yocurúja Dupade anguresórone, Dupade ujnomei jnungórone ore.
english: *Dupade*’s word says: “If anyone curses a believer of mine, I will curse that person also.” Therefore, I’m telling you right now, that we must not curse those that believe in*Dupade*, those that carry his word to other places.

ayoreo: Uacamóño uje jnani bajade ore, ore todo Dupade to nanique uje qué ugúcoi erámi casodicaite, menenéngone ore uyoque jnese. Irajágo uje Dupade cuse, mu que yirajágo uruode quigode gotique.
english: **he Ayoreos knew there was a****Dupade****:** You know that our forefathers feared*Dupade*long ago while they were still in the woods, as did all the other tribal groups. We all knew there was a*Dupade*, but we had no idea that there was a book with his words.

ayoreo:
Mu disi ejode uyoque enga
yirajágo Dupade uruode quigode iji darujna arone, uje darujna arone chingo
yoque. Uje ore churúsãre Dupade uruode quigode iji,  Dupade guetégode quigode iji unojo, darujna
arone. Enga yirajágo uje cuchade ipesungóri tu Dupade gu Dupade uruode.
english: But we who are their descendants now know about the things of*Dupade*, because we have His Book. And in His Book these things are explained, it tells about the things*Dupade*hates. And we know from reading that book that*Dupade*is the one who created all things. **not disregard what****other believers say****:**

ayoreo: Enga jé ca yocuñeque chetaque Dupade anguresónique ojñáringo gajnerĩ. Jé ca yocuñeque cho darúja anguresónique gajnerĩ. Dupade anguresónique gu.
english: May none of us disregard what*Dupade**’**s**believers*tell us. May none of us curse a believer. That is because that person believes in*Dupade*. We now know that power and love are both from*Dupade*, along with all good things. We know this is true because those who carried Dupade’s word to us told us about this.

ayoreo:
Yirajágo uje pujopié,
pijnoraquei ore garani tu Dupade, cucha omone garani u Dupade. Yirajágo unojo
uje eduguéjnane, Dupade ujnomei jnungórone ore chingo yoque.
english: The only thing we knew long ago was that there was a*Dupade*, but we knew nothing about his word. We have only learned about the things of*Dupade*because we now have his word. And we now know something our forefathers did not know, that*Dupade*hates sin. He hates disputing and criticizing. He hates disobedience. This*Dupade*wants good things for us, and he wants us to do good things, and he accepts the things we do that please him. **giving****the people****Dupade****’s messages:**

ayoreo:
Gusu nanique uje yirajágo uje
Dupade cuse, mu que yirajágo uruode quitique go. Gusu uje yirajágo cuchade
udore gu Dupade uruode, enga disi ejode uyoc e yirajágo uje Dupade chi chetaque
pisijnaringuéone. Chetaque pipojnaquéode. Chetaque pangoninguíte paritigáde.
Mu Dupade potigádi u pate omone iji yoque, ujetiga yicoi yocate omone to, enga
Dupade chimesẽ́re yoque to.
english: *Dupade*told Jeremiah: “Go and teach the children of Israel about the things that please me.” So, he went to teach them about these things, but the people of Israel wouldn’t accept his teachings. That’s when they put him in chains and took him to a prison and locked him up. They hated what*Dupade*had to say, and they hated*Dupade*’s servant that told them what*Dupade*said. But Jeremiah wasn’t alone in that prison long ago,*Dupade*was there with him. And he punished the children of Israel for disregarding the word of their Creator and not obeying what he told them to do. This is the end of my message.*(Jeremiah 29:11, 12)*

ayoreo: Chi Dupade chojninga nanique ome Jeremías: “Bo ga acate Israél abode ore ome yimeserane cucha.” Mu uné jno, mu urasade chetaque acajnaquéode. Ore chigaru jmanecade ujetiga ore nája guiguíjnai enga ore suru gai. Ore chetaque Dupade gu, enga ore chetaque irógode ore acajnaquéode. Mu chi ca gusu Jeremías gotique, nanique, Dupade déji gari. Enga Dupade cho dajuñame udore, Israel abode ore, uje ore chetaque Dupade uruode enga que ore doi uraque dapesungóri ajegaidode cucha gotique gu.
english: Dupade – God *Ecarai**–**Tobité**, Bolivia**– 1970’s.*

ayoreo: E gusu yacajnaquéode udojo.*(Jeremías 29:11, 12)* *Ecarai – Tobité, Bolivia – 1960**Marina de Morarie churúsare Ecarai uruode udojo*
english: *Transcribed and translated to English by: Maxine Morarie**.*

ayoreo: Jesus chi chojninga: “A Dupade ujnomei chise erámi jnese jne ome cojñone ore, uyóguiji erámi  jnese, ga ujetiga ore chudute Yiapade ujnomei uje pota cho darásarigo iji erámi unirisórone jnese, ga jé u jeta yiríringai ta yúode a jé u jne.”*(Mateo 24:14)*
english: Jesus said: “When the news about*Dupade*reaches the whole world – when it reaches the*Cojñone*and the*Ayoréode*, and when everyone has heard the news that my Father wants to make everyone in the whole world his countrymen, then my arrival is near.”*(Matthew 24:14*

ayoreo:
Jé uacadúcho uje chi qué ocoi
menenéngone, cojñone ore iji erámi uje que ore chudute Dupade Uruode, mu e
yugúcoi ore gatócoro. Jecute Jesuse ojninga casodicaite, gãrapie uñanie e
tocade.
english: You have heard that there are yet many*menenéngone*(people like the*Ayoré*), and other people in the world that haven’t heard*Dupade*’s words, and at one time the*Ayoré*were among them. Therefore, some of the sayings of Jesus concerning the signs of his arrival have already taken place, because now the*Ayoré*have heard.

ayoreo:
Jesuse chi gãrapié to uje
guédodie básiji gatajnoquei. Jesuse gãrapiedie u uje jnumi tãrara. Jesuse
gãrapie cuchade udojo jnese. Chamáñaje jé urúo nanique. Uje chingo dacadigode
ore uje qué déji jnumi nanique, ga acadigode ore catecã́ri Jesus iji
quedéjnarique enga ore chojninga: “Yocacadisṍri á ¿jé cho rique a ujetiga
cuchade udore tocade a? ¿Jé pãrapiéringui jne iji bãríringai, erámone eruei ore
yui?”
english: He said another sign of his coming is that the stars will fall from the sky. Another sign of his coming is earthquakes. These are all signs of his coming. It’s exactly what he said long ago.

ayoreo:
Mu Jesuse chojningame ore: “Uje
a jiépisi cojñotígo jne ujetiga ore chósi tome uaque: ‘¡Cachi uacangarádejnai
Crísto u yu á!” Udore a ore chãra cojñoque uásejnane jne. Mu ujetiga uñeque
potarása jeta chãra uaque enga chojningame uaque: ‘¡Cristo déji ne!’ Jeonga
uñéngo chojninga: ‘¡Crísto déji ta!’ Mu jé ca angáchoi udore gosode to go. Uje
Dupade utocaidi uyu yiríringárique gajnui a u ajnorátique jne. Uje tṍraji
gaté enga chicáji quedéjnane.  Parecujate
dosóguedie udire quigade enga guedé a toi jne. Guedoside a ca chajniapo jnumi
te jne rĩ. Guédodie a basapo jne rĩ iji gaté. Enga gaté a chijocase re jnese
jne. Mu que pirajaque yiríringarique dire, guedé gatócororaque ore.
english: It’s what he told his disciples when he was still on earth, and they asked him: “Teacher, how long till all of this happens? What are the signs of your arrival and the end of the world?”

ayoreo: Dirode iji Dupade utocaidi uyu yiríringai yui a chopise dirode yui jne uje guei dequejnangóri chejna Noé urásade nanique. Ore áyipiedie gaidode upúsu daquetangóningo cucha ome raque nanique. Uñane ore áyipiedie gaidode u dabosode. Jé uñane áyipiedie gaidode u dajódode yote piócho. Jé basa uñane ore áyipiedie gaidode u dajmatodie gapui, dajmasórone jnacare ore ujetiga jé ore chisa ñane. Mu chise diri uje Noé e ore chacája dabi cuco cuchape ajei. Mu ore chuninga mu guei dequejnangóri yore ore uñane gai enga chejna ore jnese. Adi a jé u jne ujetiga Dupade utocaidi uyu yĩriápo rĩ iji uaque. Beyoi enga ajuso uaquero enga dimane uágome yiríringai yui.”
english: But Jesus said to them: “There will be many people who will say to you, I am the Christ that you call your-great-leader! And they are going to fool a lot of people. But when others try to fool you, they will say, ‘Here is Christ!’ Or others might say, ‘Christ is right over there!’ But don’t pay attention to them. That’s because I, who was sent from*Dupade*, tell you this: God’s sent-one’s coming will be like the lightning that flashes across the sky and it’s over. After this painful time, the sun will die.  The light of the moon will not return to earth again. The stars will also fall from the sky. The whole sky will shake. But the day of my coming, the exact time of my coming, is not known.

ayoreo: *Ecarai – Tobité, Bolivia – 1960**Marina de Morarie churúsãre Ecarai uruode nanique*
english: “The days before the return of*Dupade*’s sent-one, will be exactly like the days before the flood came and destroyed Noah’s countrymen. All they thought about was gaining a living. Some people’s minds at that time were continually on food, the minds of others were on liquor, others only thought about getting girlfriends or boyfriends, and getting married. But the day came when Noah and his family entered the big boat and were safe. But suddenly the rain came down upon the others and destroyed all of them completely. It is going to be like that when I, the-one-sent from*Dupade*, comes again for you. Come now and be watchful and ready for my coming!”

ayoreo:
**Jesus acadigode di enga ore ojñane u Dupade adode ome uráguiji dajesúode:**Mu yirajágo iji yoquidai ga ayoré diode ojñane u cucha anírone, gojña erámi tudé jogonórone cucha, erámi tudé isijnaringuéone. Mu Pedro, jorane ore ojñane u Dupade adode ome uráguiji dajesúode.*(Marcos 6:30)*

Ga jec u uje
unere uje ayoré purasade ore di. Unere uje purása quedéjnane chacája gáguedie to.
Ga jé adi u uje unere to uje Dupade anguresórone ore diji yoque, ga qué ore
pésu uje ore chatata dadode iji Dupade adode, ga que gusu yoque uje unerome
yoque, mu a unéraja Dupade ajei to ome ore ojñane iji dajesúode erámio.

Mu
yicatecãcoi Dupade jétiga Dupade chátaja daruode aja yoque jeta yirajágo
quigode uje Dupade Uruode jé qui jé u.*(Santiago
1:5)*

Yimóngo iji
gaté ga *Soríané catecã́ri Dupade ome yoque jeta yirajágo Dupade Uruode.

***Soríané icatecãraitigade u udojo ome Dupade:**“Dupade Dios, uje yicatecã́ri ua iji de, ipota jetiga yududipise baruode to iji Ecarai, Davidé pijnani jeta chacate yu. Gusu ua uje daijna cuchábi ua ga bajnué cuchade gáyode jnese. Bajnué Satanás, irógode, ore gai. Bajnué pitoringai gai. Yipota jéta acha bajmanai taja yoquibai iji dejai tudé ga yacágoja direcaringuei to gajnerĩ. Acha bajmanai taja yoquibai iji dejai tudé.

Yirajágo uje
cucha omone garani u ua uje yicoi baruode iji deréjnane jnese. Ga arodipise
uyoque gajnerĩ iji dejai udé, iji uyoque jnese ome Satanás. Aranguipise
yoquejode gajnerĩ. Gusu ua uje yicoi baruode iji deréjnane jnese. Que yimóngo
ua mu bamo yoque ga bagúsi yoquejode.

Gusu ua uje daijna cuchábi ua ga yicoi
baruode, babi Jesucristo ore uruode.

Gusu ua uje
bajnué cuchade jnese. Bajnué pitoringai gai. Bajnué Satanás, irógode gai. Uje
Davidé chacate yoque gajnerĩ ga ãro yocate gajnaréngone gajnerĩ,
pisijnaringuéone iji pajéode.

Asi ome cucha
pibose omone ome yoque gajnerĩ, yajo gajnerĩ ga ca pésu dequejnarétiguiji
yocajéode gajnerĩ. Gusu ua uje bamo pajéode. Ga ca yamóngo dequejnarétique ome
yoque gajnerĩ. Arote
ore gajnerĩ, disidie ore ga ore doi baruode to gajnerĩ. Adi u chequedie to
rĩ, asi ome báyipie ome ore gajnerĩ ga ca ore chajéngome baruode to gajnerĩ
iji deréjnane jnese jeta yicoi baruode iji Davidé pijnani.

Yicatecã́ri
ua iji babi Jesucristo i. Amẽ.

**Ecarai acajnaquéone u udojo ome pujnoméone quigode:***(Efesios 6:21 aja 22)*Jec urẽ San Pablo acajnaquéode uje chojninga: “Yiroque Tíquico aja uaque ujetiga angáchoi ojñáringo ome yoque.”

Jé ũrepise
uje yugúcoi Tobité te ga udúco Dupade anguresórone ujnoméone uje ore changureta
Dupade iji Rincón, jéoga Zapoco, enga
unere ome yoque. Uje udúco Poza Verde gosode ujnomei uje ore cho jnome
Dupade Uruode ga yoco yocáyugu.
Ga jé u San Pablo ojningane ome yoque uje chojninga: “Uhuaque uje yiroque
Tíquico aja uaque jeta angáchoi yocujnoméone, angáchoi iji pijnani gajnerĩ.”

Dupade
iroque, Dupade anguresṍre Tíquico, ga que doi ga cutame Dupade atique. Ga jé
ũrepise uje yicagoi udojo to ica, Rincóngosode ore, enga icai ome Dupade
Uruode. Mu adi jec u uje cho jeta yoquidáyo, ga iyajmaque yóguiji mu
yacaranguipis ome Dupade uje Dupade Uruode que chijnime re iji Rincóngosode.
Que chijnime re iji Zapocogosode go. Que chijnime re iji Tobitégosode.

Mu gosode jeta cho jnome Dupade Uruode ga ore ujnomei cuse ome Dupade, mu que ore ujnomei uerate go, ore ujnomei gajnárecho ome Dupade uje ore cho jmiti, jmiti ome Dupade ga ore chicáji quedéjnane ome Dupade Uruode. Jé basa ga Tobité, jeoga Zapoco, jeoga Rincóngosode ore ujnomei chise Dupade idai te ga, pujnomédaté ome anírone Dupade uje chudute yocujnomei.

**San Pablo chiroque Tíquico aja Efésogosode jeta ore jnusi rangome ojñane (Efesio 6:21-22)**Ga Dupade anguretasõre Tíquico uté, ga que doi ga anire, cutame Dupade atique. Enga adi jec uyu iji deréjnai tuné uje Dupade anguresórone iji udé Tobité yocajnei tu jetiga yõco yoque iji Dupade Uruode ga ca yucutágome Dupade Uruode ujetiga yangácoi iji Uruode batigade. Jé ca domingo maringa, Dupade Uruode batiga jeta yicoi Dupade Uruode iji, ga ca yarángo yóguiji quedéjnarique jeta ojninga, ‘Ca yi to, chamáñaje yiquihíjnonguiji dajei.’ Jeoga, ‘Ca yiji to chãje yúdode urõso.’ Mu jé ũré dequejnaréngone déji ga, jé ũré Dupade Uruode déji rĩ uje Dupade Uruode chi gosi ejnaréngone ga ejnaréngone urõso. Mu ujetiga gosi tagu dejnaréngone aja, ga Dupade jé chajnerame gosi ijóningai.

**Ezequías ujnomei uerate ome Dupade:**Jé uacamóño uje jnani bajai, aníri Ezequías a u, uje chojninga: “Dupade á, a yejnaréngone chinguíra yu aja jnumi dirome, ga a yitoi dirome,” chojninga. “E idógosi jeta yitoi mu, Dupade á, que yipota jetiga acadígui yicaniratiguéode ome yurasade ore,” chojninga. “Jé bamo uje yosõrai yocurása gatadíe ore ome yoquibosode,” chi chojninga. “Yosõraque yocurása matágode ore ome yoquibosode.”

Mu Dupade
chimo icaniratiguei, chimo úringai paraque, chimo úringai anire osongáca. Ga
Dupade chojninga: “Udute bacatecãraitigade. Mu a cáma atoi jne. A qué yõrape
sequeredie tiji bei te jne. Ga sequeredie a qué chejnape yirídai jne iji bei
iji batoringai yui.” Ga unojo jéta Dupade chimesẽre yoque gosi, ga Dupade
aníri qué chisi ome yoquijóningai garósi ome yoque jeta jé yicabi Uruode gaté.

Mu
yocurásarigo ayoré ujeta Dupade chiraja ujeta e chajnísi de jeta e ore
changureta Dupade, ga qué chisi ome yocucuecaringuei tome yoque jétiga
yingongomá Uruode aja ore.*(Romanos 9:25
aja 26)*

Ayoréode uyoque uje gajnareque yocujnomei ome Dupade nanique, mu yanguretágo Jesuse enga jé u uje noña ga uerate. Uerate yocujnoméone gu uje yimesẽco Dupade Ujnacari gu.

**Sodómagosode ore ujnoméone gajnárecho ome Dupade:**Jé uacamóño uje Dupade chiroque darógode uñéngaja ore idaidatei, que yiraja jeta Sodóma u. Gaidi u gu ujetiga chejna ore iji ore idai uté gu ore ujnome gajnaréngone. Mu jnani bajei Abraham chijócha Dupade ajei údi ujetiga ca chejna ore idai. Enga Dupade chojningame: “A jé ũré ua, enga parágode iji ore chise 10 enga a ca yejna guidai unirisórone gu jnani parágode udore jne iji ore ujade.” Mu que chise 10 uje changureta Dupade. Mu diga chejna ore idaidatei gu uje que ore áyipieraque dépoi Dupade gotique rĩ. Enga u to uje gajnáreque ore jnomei.

**San Pablo chatata dadode ome ore uje cho daba datatagode Dupade urúo:**Ga San Pablo ojningane u uje chojninga: “A adúcho yocujnomei to,” chojninga. San Pablo ujnomei tu uje chi cojñone chaquesu údode, ore chaquesu údode ga que chise dajóningárique. Ore doi ga ore suru gáyode. Que chimo eníngome re. Jé que chimo direcaringuei rĩ ome re. Ore chigaru ga ore chaquesu údode ome poridie. Que chise anínique daragajnúraque jeta arangajnúnie etoque. Údode tutut. Ga unojo u uje cojñone chaquesu údode gu uje ore chetágome Dupade Uruode, ga ore charatáiyime Dupade Uruode ome. Ore chetaque Dupade Uruode ome, ga ore chaquesu údode mu chojninga: “Yujnomei tu. Que yi ga yãra yu iji quedéjnarique jeta yi ga yojninga: ‘Que Dupade chátaja yu go, ga cojñone jecucha ore chaquesu yúdode. Dupade que chátaja yu. A yiya yacate ore ome Dupade Uruode jne.’ Úngare, a ca yiya jne, enga a ca yuruode u udore jne. Yigaidi u gu ujetiga yo yiba Jesucrísto úringai patataque iji erámi jnese gu.” A San Pablo uruode u udojo.

Mu jec ũré
San Pablo uje udúco ujnoméone iji deréjnai tudé uje chi ore chaquesu údode.*(Hechos 14:19 aja 20)*

Pablo ujnomei
cuse uje chejnareque re ome datatagode Dupade Urúo. Ore chujérase mu que cha
Dupade iroquéode gotique. Ueradipise Pablo ujnomei uté uje chejnáreque re, mu
qué jno Dupade Uruode údi aja erámi jnese.

**Pablo ujnomei u uje gajnarengué edodie:***(Gálatas 4:13 aja 15 ore)*Enga San Pablo, que yimóngo mu, edodie chi edo cho chique nanique, edodie cho ja ja ja aja dajesúode. Ga anire uje ejnaréngone que íjnonguiji, mu que chãra re iji go. Que chãra re iji jeta chojninga: “Que yũrúaji darújna arone, que yũrúaji yajirigode erámio ga que yi ga anire, que yi Dupade Uruode údi go.” Mu a chojninga, “Yujnomei tu, mu jetiga Dupade chimate yu aja yúringárique anire, pijnaque aja Dupade yui enga yigaidi tu,” chojninga.*Flipenses 1:21*

**Pablo ujnomei u uje yote quedéjnai chibéro Pablo pi cucho:**Hechos 27 chatata úyujna cuchábi uje tṍraji casaque garani enga chubúchu Pablo ore pi cuco enga chachẽre ore enga chaquesu cucoi údode. Jecute ore tṍra cucoi ugúchade aja yote quedéjnai. Dirode jiei uje guedé que tocadépo rĩ enga ore doi dateringane uje aníri, chi ore pi cuco pero, ga anire, que ore tagu dabotíque. Uje e iyajmaque uje que ore tagu dabotique enga Pablo chague enga chojningame ore: “Yijorane uaque á, ca oyo uacáyugu uaque. Uje a cucoi pero jne maringa, a ca uacuñeque toi jne. Uje Dupade iroi gaté unirisṍri uñai chiqueta raja yu que iji dejai udé. Enga Dupade iroi uté chojningame yu que: ‘¡Pablo á! Ca atodo. Bajnei tu ujetiga ango ua aja Róma gosode eduguéjna cuchábi dirome jne. Jecute Dupade pota chiqueta ua iji yote quedéjnai udé, enga a chiquetápo bajõráringo ore jnese to jne, enga a ca uñeque toi jne.’”

Uje e ore
chise jnumi jnese nanique enga ore jnome ore idaidatei Róma yui. Mu chise gójni
ica uje Dupade anguretasṍrone chachai ore iji dajei, enga chi Pablo anguretigai
dopoi gaté rĩ. Ga chi chojninga: “Yanguretigai ga dopoi gaté rĩ uje yicoi
aníri, yutuangóa yocujode Dupade anguretasóño uje Roma a qué idája mu, a ore
chachai yoque ome Dupade Uruode.”

Ga jec u uje, ujetiga gosi changureta Dupade ga chudute Dupade Uruode iji Dupade anguresónique pijnatique ga jé ore anguretigai dopoi gaté jne rĩ enga ijṍrapo rĩ. Ijṍrapo ome Dupade Uruode rĩ. Ga San Pablo ojningane ome yoque u uje chojninga: “A adúcho yujnomei to jne.”

**Pablo ujnomei jé upo rĩ uje ore suru gai nanique:**Ga San Pablo ujnoméone u uje chojningame ore: “A adúcho yujnomei to ome Tíquico uje yirógaja uac uje Dupade anguretasṍre,” chojninga.

Ga jec ũré chi tangári daquei te mu uje chi ore suru gai te mu, uje tocade ga jõrai u Sílas nanique uje chojñone ore suru ore gáyore. Mu uje dejnínga gatócoro enga Páblo, Sílas ore chijñecare enga ore chirate Dupade iradedie, enga ore catecãrápoi Dupade rĩ. Ore chudute Dupade iradedie, ore jurúgáyode uñane, enga qué ore tangári Pablo, Sílas ore. Gaidi tu ujeta ore chiraja ga ore tangári anírone dadudedie piradei mu Dupade iradedie u ga anire, ore anguretigai dopoi gaté rĩ, ga ore irasigápo re rĩ uje ore chudute Dupade iradedie iji Pablo ore pijnanone.

**Pujnomé gajnarétigo pésu ga yibetágome:**¿Jec ũré yu a? uje udúco Dupade anguresórone ujnoméone iji erámone, yudúco uje ore cha Dupade Uruode enga yibetágo. Garósi yóguiji Dupade Uruode iguíjnai ajei te ga, yibetágo uje aníri, garósi yogome Dupade Uruode. Gosi jmané urõso, gosi edo urõso, jeoga gosi guide urõso ga betá gosi. Ga adi jec u ome Dupade anguresórone. Chi cuta uje garósi ore ome Dupade Uruode. Ore chudute Dupade anguresṍri ujnomei enga ore cha Dupade Uruode, ga ore beta.

San Pablo
chacãra Dupade anguresórone aja ayorérique adode. Ga ayorérique a chaquesu
daríderaque, jeoga iguitai, jeoga ducotique déji údode uñeque, ga cuta, chãje
údode ga ejnaretaque ga cuta uje urõso.

Ga adi tu San
Pablo acajnaquéode ome yoque uje chojninga: “Uacadúcho yujnoméone,” chojninga.
“Enga anóningacho,” chojninga. “Ca ayorérique déji siñeque jetiga doso útique
ga tagu dejnarei taja jeta ijõre ore jnese.”

Mu San Pablo
acajnaquéode udojo ome yoque uje chojninga: “Ujetiga Dupade*iglesia*ijõre ga ijõre ore jnese, ga que
ore chimo dacutaquérique ga ore nína ráguiji jnumi. Jé basa ga jé garósi yogome
Dupade Uruode, ga Dupade Uruode ipotigasṍrone ore cha uje ore tangári Dupade
Uruode, ga ibetágo.

¿Jé gotique gu a uje ibetágo? Ibetágo gu uje garósi yoque ome Dupade Uruode. Yibetágo gu uje urõso yiríde, ibetágo uje urõso yedo, yibetágo uje urõso yijmané ga que yise yicaniratiguei. Adi jé u ome Dupade Uruode anguresórone uje Dupade ujopié beta iji yocujade uje Dupade guídedíe uña urõso, jeoga Dupade iglesia uña cho jnome.

**Dupade Uruode u uje pésu ga ijõre yoque: (Isaías 55:11)**Jec ũrepise uje Dupade Uruode a diji Dupade jne enga jetiga chajniapója jne rĩ enga que gusu go, a nóna yoc uñengo aja, a nóna ayorérigo uñéngo aja. Dupade Uruode aja Dupade iguíjnai te ga, jé ũrepise Dupade Uruode uje que gusu go iji dajesúode erámio. Que gusu ayoré jogasurãi iji ajesu erámio go.

Jé cho to
sárode irajangṍri uje que gusu iji dajesu erámio nanique to. Ore nóna ñane
aja quedéjnane gu uje ore chiraja uje aníri, irajasṍre chi ore chubúchu ore
iji ore ejnaréngone, ga pésu ore ejnaréngone, ga cho sere, ninguira. Ga ore
nóna aja quedéjnane, dajogasui uraque dajõra*sárode irajangore*.

Cuchiyi tupusu uyoque uje
ueracho yocujnoméone, uyoque uje Dupade ujopié Espiritu Santo irágu yocajéode,
ga yijínango ñane aja quedéjnane to uje yicoi enga yátagoja ñane ome Dupade
uruode, iroquéode ore. A yijínango ñane enga a yicatecãcoi Dupade to ome ñane
uje ejnaretaque yoque.

**Satanás ucuei u ujetiga chĩro yocujnomé ueradode:**Adi jec u to uje yijínango Dupade Uruode aja quedéjnane gu jetiga soca seti yacaco uágome Satanás isocade iji pijogadode jnese. Yoquigaidi u ujetiga jé uacamóño uje chi Satanás chi ca chacája cucha gajnaréngone to go. Satanás píode u cucha ueradéjnane ga que ayorérique chiraja gotique.

Satanas
e gajneque pisijnaringuéone ipesusórone ore, enga uté píode u ayoré sijnac enga
unere yui ome jeta pojna dojórique ayoré. Satanás pi ga que chise anínique, ga
que chise unerotiguérique go enga ca derocome ipesudode cucha gajnaréngone. A
jnacare Dupade anguretasṍri, ga Satanás chacája gapu ueradéjna tome, ga e que
chiraja go.

¿Gotique gu a
uje aniri, jnani bajai etoi Samson pota tungãre purása pimojma, purása anire,
guichaya uje que chudute Dupade urúrique? Chóji gari, doi dateringane ome, uje
uaté urasade chejna abai Samson edodie jne. Satanás chacája udojo cucha
ueradode ga que ayorérique, cojñotique ore doi ga ore chiraja go.*(2 Corintios 11:14)*

Satanás
chi chacája ugárique nanique ga cho jetiga disi udé u gu ome Eba, yocaté,
chequé bajé. A pésu ga jnacare ueradéjna, ga Eba que todo go. Que doi ga
chirajaque go. Jé uacamóño ugade iji deréjnai tudé, diri tudé uje uñane
uruchachade déji ga ueracho. Jé déji dicamichórone uje uruchachade ueradéjna,
achinguirane ueradéjna uruchachade, ga chi adi u nanique ome jnani bajade.
Satanás chacája ga chinoningase ga ayoré. Chiqueta raja yocaté chequé bajé te
ga que chiraja gotique uje uga.

Jecute Dupade
anguresórone uyoque jeta yirajágo udojo, ga jé qué yipojnáningo jorógome
Satanás pírigo cucha ga a Satanás a chicai quedéjnane ome yoque jne.*(1 Pedro 5:8 aja 9)*

Jé uacamóño uje Jesus chojninga,
“Satanás boji yejode, yachopise ua. ¡Cho jétiga yigaidode u baruode!”
Chojninga, “Yigaidode u Yapade uruode.”

Mu Satanás chojninga, “Angári yu to
jnenga jeta achaga bagataidie iji jnumi tiji yiquei tenga bajneque erámi tudé
jne, úringai tu ueradéjnai,” chojninga.

**Ecarai chacãra dacajnaquéode aja ayoréode uje ore churu ñane ga ore chijna úringai cucherãque:**Adi u uje ayoréode churu ñane nanique gu ujetiga ore chinoningase ñane ga ore chijna úringai cucherãque. Jeta ore chinoningasipise ore enga que ore cãra dabotígome darui, que ore anire, que ore chise darui unerotiguérique uje inoningayaque gu.

Adi u Dupade
uje Dupade churu yoc, ga chinoningase yoque, ga ijnángo úringai cucherãque.
Jesus úringai cucherãque u Cristiano uje Dupade ujnuchu Cristo ome dajnacari.
Jécute yingome yoque jeta apota baringárique pinoningayaque ga ango Dupade ga
chinoningase ua to. Ajína Dupade to jne jeta diga Dupade di. Chi Dupade di jne
mu cáma yirajágo diringárique.

**Jesus chatata darapiéringui ome ore:**Jé uacadúcho yocuruode uje jnani bajade ore u nanique marínga, uje ore changureta chaboto ugodie nanique. Ore chojninga: “¡Chabotodie e po que jõ, cucha garapiedie!’ Mu cuchiyi tu Dupade uje Dupade ujnacari Jesus chi chojninga nanique: “A yĩriapo jne rĩ ga yarapiérigui te jne. Yãrapiedie a u jne jetiga jnumi udé tãrara. Yãrapiedie u jeta guédodie basiji gatajnoquei tuté. Yãrapiedie jeta guedé toi jne ga topié jnumi. Yãrapiedie jeta guedoside diquiyo edo.” Mu yudúco unojo pujnomei udojo, jé ujnoméone udojo ga e iyaque yoquibai Jesus diringai.

Mu a ca angureta Dupade to enga a yujnuco jórogui ua to, bajnusi yoque rĩ. Mu que toc jeta yojnicome Dupade: “Dupade á, acarape ga ore nonama yoque to jne. Ãrope ore isijnaringuéone enga ore nonama yoque to jne.”

Mu Dupade que
uruode u go. Dupade Uruode qui tu uje chojninga: “Yujnacari Jesucristo tẽra re
ome ore nanique, enga uté anguretasórone uje qué ore déji jnumi enga ore chingo
yuruode taja darasade ga jeta gosi pota ga changureta Jesus uté to.”

Jécute yingome
uac, apota enga angureta Dupade ga angureta Dupade. Mu Dupade anguresórone ore
gajnei tu jeta ore cha date bajade.

Ayoréode
uyoque uje ugúcoi cojñone gari, ayoréode ore cha date bajade. Ore cha uje ore
chigaru dagaredie nanique, ore cha uje choquibi ore. Ore cha uje ore chujé
cojñone. Ore cha uje ore chujé dojode ayoré. Ga ore cha dadode ga ore chaquesu
dacarane ga ita ore. Ga ore chaca boraidie. Ore chijna pocadie. Ore tagu cucha
puyade: erámoronie, ore tagu tiguiriquidie, ore tagu cabayújnane. Mu ore chécãre
dadode gu udojo.

Mu omi tu
jetiga yécãco yocadode uje yicoi Dupade Uruode ga cojñone, Dupade urásarigo, ca
ore chiraja daru quitíguiji yoque. U to ujetiga uerate yocujnoméone. Yécãco
yocadode ga iyagopúsu yocate bajade. Iraja uje yiyágo yocate bajade. Yiyágo uje
jnani bajade chigaru datoidie. Yiyágo uje choquibi yoc. Iyágo uje yujégo
cojñone. Iyágo uje ore chujé dojode ayoré, mu amiri u jeta iyágo udojo jorane
cucha ga yicoi ga yanguretágo Dupade ga yiquihíjnojo aja Dupade gánique jne
jeta di ga di.

Jecute
jecucha yingome uaque jeta gosi pota nóna darasade, jeoga gotoa pota nóna
dabai, jeoga gosi pota nóna dacoté ore to, ga ango Dupade. Ga Dupade chĩro
basijnaringuéone, uje Ujnacari Jesus cho basijnaringuéone oré uje toji curesei
gai. Ga Dupade chinoningase ua enga báringai ayoré pijõre jogadi tu ome Dupade
uje Dupade chimesẽre ua. Dupade a churúsãre bei iji gatajnoquei.

¿Gotique gu a
uje cojñone churúsãre darusarane ome dachidedie piesórone cojñoc? Ore
churúsarame ore íode tiji darujna aone jeta ore chiraja ga jé ũré ga ore doi
oré tome. Ga Dupade churúsãre yoquíode iji darujna aone to. Chidójninga jeta
jec ũré jeta yanguretágo ga yoqui déji darujna aoi pucuecaringuei gajnéone iji
gatájnoquei. Ga Dupade chidójninga udojo.

Gusu yuruode ome yoque, mu soca seti jé yiguíjna yui ome gosi ga gosi chingoba daninaquéringome Dupade, dasocaringome Dupade, jeta Dupade chijína gosi to, jeta Dupade chátaja gosi, jeta gosi irasique Dupade Uruode.

***Ujnomeraque gosi uje ũré ome danguretigai:***Gosi ga jec ũré ga changureta Dupade ga cha udojo yocájningane cucha. Ga yécãco yoquisocárique. Rosadé cha dorijnai. Cha jetiga chubúchu ayoréode. Enga gusu jetiga changureta Dupade. Que yijna borijnane to mu yetingare to nanique mu yiya. Chamáñaje Dupade chetaque udojo ome yoque. Soríané cha uje chijna dorijnane. Cha ga changureta Dupade ga Dupade inoningayaque ga i jé u Cristiano to. Ga unojo u jeta gosi pota changureta Dupade, chajna. Que chajna, enga chirate dojode jnani, jeoga dojode jnacare. Mu yiraco ñane aja Dupade adode ga jec u uje yoquiqui uté ome Dupade Uruode ga yiquérango iji Dupade Uruode iji guidai tudé. Ga yoquirasique yoque, enga Dupade chimo yocujnome ueradode enga irasique yoque to.

**Ueradi u jetiga disi ejode chagutíyame Dupade uruode to iji ayoréode uruode:**Que yimo jeta yi Dupade Uruode to dirome iji Señora Marina, jeta yáta Dupade Uruode uñéngo aja ore jeta churúsãre, enga anire, yipéco iji ayoréode uruode.

Ueradi u
ujetiga disi ejode, jnacarode disi ejo ore, ga ore chiraja yocuruode uñane,
yocuru bajade. Jé soca seti ore chajire Dupade Uruode dirome iji yocuruode ga
ore chijna yocuruode aja yoc jeta ore chacate yoc, jeta yimáco Dupade Uruode
iji udojo darujna aone aja ayoréode uruode.

Jeta yicáji
Señora Marina ga yacadape ome Dupade Uruode iji yocuruode enga quigade ga yicai
yocurasade erámi jogonórone ayoréode udore jeta Dupade chátaja yoque enga jeta
chátaja yu enga soca seti qué yise yurasade. Jeoga Dupade ajéo Dupade
chaquĩ́ra yu iji durujñeque.

Jé uacamóño
uje ayorérique ejnaretaque ga aníri que changureta jeta eca. Mu angureni u
Dupade guerané pucuecaringuei ejoi. Jé yujnomei u uté uje qué yiqueca enga
yangureta Dupade guerané pucuecaringuei ejoi yui uje chatire yijóningai ome yu
iji gaté.

**Nacore catecã́ri Dupade**:Amóñomi iji gate ga yicatecãrámi Dupade.

Dupade Dios,
jé yicatecãrámi ua iji gajnerĩ. Yocacaranguipisape ua uje ugúcoji de, baruode
jogadi, guiguíjna tajei, baruode jogadi*iglésia*.
Yocacaranguipise gajnerĩ uje yingo babi Cristo i taja yoque uje daijna cuchábi
tu ua, Dupade.

Yocacaranguipisape
ua uje bajnerame yocuniri uje Ecarai chacate yoque. Jec aregape Irónguené,
Soriané, uje catecã́ri ua ome yoc. Átaja yoque gajnerĩ ujetiga yocajneque
bedaidaté ueradi, direcaringuei. Arote yoquejode ome Satanás piesórone cucha
ore yúode. Yirajago gu uje Satanás chucue dasocáringome Dupade anguresórone
uyoque ujetiga chinoningase yocujnomé ueradode, jecute arote chequedie,
jnanione yoquejode ome uté iji dirode, dejade ore jnese. Yicatecã́ri ua iji bajnacari
Jesucristo i. Amẽ.

**Soríané: Misionero bajaguipísode uje cinco ore nanique ore chise ayoréode mu chuque ore ome ayoréode. Enga ore yungórone uñai u Soríané nanique, mu yocacaranguipis uje e Soríané changureta Jesuse to.*

Ecarai – Tobité, Bolivia – 1970Marina de Morarie churúsãre Ecarai uruode udojo nanique
english:
*Ayoré diode ojñane  Ecarai*

**When t****he disciples****arrived they****told Jesus****about****their trip:***(**Mark 6:30**)*

We know in our village that people arrive and bring all kinds of news to us – stories about what is happening in the world and how bad things are. But what Peter and the other disciples told Jesus when they came back after a trip was about what*Dupade*did for them in their travels.

It’s always good when our Ayoré countrymen arrive. Or when Ayoréos from other places visit our group. When believers come they give us their testimonies and tell us what*Dupade*did for them wherever they went. Not only do we like to hear their news, but*Dupade*loves to hear it too.*(Malachi 3:16)*

But let’s pray for*Dupade*to help us to understand His*Words*for that is what God asks us to do.

Let’s*sleep a bit in the sky*and *Soríané will pray for us to understand*Dupade*’s*Words*.

**Sor****í****ané’s prayer****:**

*Dupade*, I pray to you right now; I want very much to hear your words from Ecarai’s lips when he teaches me. You are the only great medicine man, so much more powerful that all others. You are greater than Satan and his demons. You have overcome death. I want you to place your hand upon us tonight, so we might enter the light. Place your hand on us this very night.

We know that good things come from you whenever we meet to study your words*.*Watch over us tonight and protect all of us from Satan. Forbid*our-nearnesses*to him. You are the only one we obey every day. We can not see you, but you can see us and are with us right now.

You are the only powerful medicine man whose words we obey, the words of your Son Jesus Christ.

You alone are over all things. Over death. Over Satan and his demons. As David’s father, Ecarai, teaches us right now, forgive our ugly ways and the sin inside us.
